PCA expl.  = 0.014    ← first 2 PCs explain only 1.4 % of variance → high-dim text
entropy    = 8.88 bits/token
Available Recipes (9 total)
Every recipe is a function recipe_<name>(filepaths, *, out_dir=None, opts) -> dict that returns a bundle**:
{
    "report":       { ... },         # serialised to report.json
    "verdict_lines":[ ... ],         # joined with "\n" → verdict.md
    "artifacts":    { name: Path, ... },  # PNG / MD files written to out_dir
}
The runner.run() wrapper additionally writes report.json and verdict.md to the output directory and returns the directory's Path .
Below: one section per recipe, in the same order as the serve.SERVE_RECIPES registry (which is also the order returned by GET /recipes ).
1. analyze — Deep file analysis
When to use : You have one text file and want the full pipeline — POLER analyze_large_file on a keyword, fragment clustering, TF-IDF + KMeans + PCA on paragraphs, Shannon entropy per chapter, EMOTIONAL_MARKERS chapter×marker matrix, and a character-position map. Slowest recipe (~20 s for 100 K words) but richest output.
Inputs :
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| filepaths | list[str \| Path] | yes | — | Exactly 1 file. |
| keyword | str \| None | no | auto-detect | POLER keyword. If None , picked as the most frequent content token (len > 3, not stop-word). |
| chapter_pattern | str \| None | no | auto | Regex overriding lang -based detection. |
| lang | str | no | "auto" | auto / ru / ua / en / inline_ru / inline_en . |
| top_n | int | no | 15 | top_n passed to POLER analyze_large_file . |
| out_dir | Path \| None | no | — | If None , no PNGs are written (useful when stacking recipes). |
Outputs : 5–7 PNG (POLER chapter matrix only emitted if markers fire) + report.json + verdict.md .
01_chapter_matrix_sklearn.png   TF-IDF cosine between chapter centroids (viridis)
02_chapter_matrix_poler.png     EMOTIONAL_MARKERS cosine between chapters (magma) [optional]
03_transition_smoothness.png    Per-paragraph neighbour cosine time series
04_pca_scatter.png              2-D PCA of paragraphs, coloured by KMeans cluster
05_entropy.png                  Shannon entropy per chapter
06_cluster_sizes.png            POLER fragment cluster sizes (bar) [if keyword fires]
07_character_map.png            Position of every keyword hit over the file
Example :
python3 -m poler_toolkit analyze \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" \
    --keyword Міка \
    --label analyze_mika out_dir = runner.run(
    "analyze",
    ["$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"],
    keyword="Міка",
    top_n=20,
    task_label="analyze_mika",
)
Output JSON schema (abridged — seeAppendix Afor the JSON-Schema form) :
{
  "meta": {
    "task": "analyze",
    "engine": "POLER v6 + sklearn",
    "timestamp": "2026-06-29T12:45:50",
    "toolkit_version": "1.0.0"
  },
  "inputs": ["/abs/path/to/file.txt"],
  "metrics": {
    "total_words": 107652,
    "total_chapters": 35,
    "total_paragraphs": 2835,
    "keyword": "Міка",
    "poler": {
      "total_occurrences": 245,
      "unique_fragments": 182,
      "fragments_returned": 15,
      "n_clusters": 3,
      "cluster_sizes": [2, 1, 12]
    },
    "sklearn": {
      "neighbor_cosine_mean": 0.07101344614578492,
      "neighbor_cosine_std":  0.06845714661777311,
      "frac_below_0.05": 0.4372,
      "frac_below_0.10": 0.7742,
      "frac_above_0.20": 0.0318,
      "best_k": 12,
      "silhouette": 0.005655201127629263,
      "silhouettes_all": {"3": 0.0032, "4": 0.0033, "5": 0.0043, "6": 0.0040,
                          "7": 0.0048, "8": 0.0048, "9": 0.0051, "10": 0.0054,
                          "11": 0.0047, "12": 0.0057},
      "pca_explained_variance": 0.01403974204971042,
      "chap_sim_mean_offdiag":  0.5822121155262352,
      "X_shape": [2835, 10000]
    },
    "shannon_entropy": {"mean": 8.881, "std": 0.617, "min": 7.412, "max": 10.267},
    "emotional_markers_found": 19,
    "emotional_markers_total": 60
  },
  "outputs": {
    "chapter_matrix_sklearn": "/abs/…/01_chapter_matrix_sklearn.png",
    "chapter_matrix_poler":   "/abs/…/02_chapter_matrix_poler.png",
    "transition":             "/abs/…/03_transition_smoothness.png",
    "pca":                    "/abs/…/04_pca_scatter.png",
    "entropy":                "/abs/…/05_entropy.png",
    "clusters":               "/abs/…/06_cluster_sizes.png",
    "character_map":          "/abs/…/07_character_map.png"
  },
  "verdict": "КЛАСТЕРИ НЕ СКЛАДАЮТЬСЯ — текст розпадається на iзольованi шматки."
}
Key metrics (seeMetric Interpretationfor what each means):
| Metric | Healthy range | What it tells you |
|---|---|---|
| sklearn.neighbor_cosine_mean | 0.15–0.40 | Lexical coherence between adjacent paragraphs. |
| sklearn.silhouette | > 0.10 | Whether paragraphs naturally cluster into K groups. |
| sklearn.pca_explained_variance | > 0.05 | How compressible the paragraph TF-IDF is to 2-D. |
| sklearn.chap_sim_mean_offdiag | 0.20–0.60 | Cross-chapter topical overlap (too high = repetitive, too low = disjointed). |
| poler.total_occurrences | depends on keyword | How many times the keyword fires. |
| poler.n_clusters | 2–6 typical | POLER's cluster_fragments adaptive clustering. |
| shannon_entropy.mean | 8.5–10.5 bits/token | Lexical diversity (Russian prose typically 8.5–9.5). |
| emotional_markers_found | 5–40 of 60 | How many POLER markers appear at least once. |
2. compare — Compare two files
When to use : You have two unrelated files (e.g. two novels by different authors, or two drafts of unrelated stories) and want a side-by-side dashboard of structural metrics. Internally calls analyze on both files (without writing their intermediate PNGs) and computes ratios.
Inputs :
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| filepaths | list | yes | — | Exactly 2 files. Order matters: file1 is the baseline, file2 is compared to it. |
| keyword | str \| None | no | auto | Same keyword used for both files (so POLER sees the same context). |
| chapter_pattern | str \| None | no | auto | Same regex applied to both files. |
| lang | str | no | "auto" | — |
| out_dir | Path \| None | no | — | — |
Outputs : 2 dashboard PNG + report.json + verdict.md .
01_sklearn_dashboard.png   6 metrics × 2 files grouped bar chart
02_poler_dashboard.png     6 metrics × 2 files grouped bar chart
Example :
python3 -m poler_toolkit compare \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE_2" \
    --label compare_cas_vs_sphere
Output JSON schema :
{
  "meta": { "task": "compare", "engine": "POLER v6 + sklearn", ... },
  "inputs": ["/abs/file1", "/abs/file2"],
  "metrics": {
    "<file1_stem>": { /* full analyze metrics for file1 */ },
    "<file2_stem>": { /* full analyze metrics for file2 */ },
    "ratios": {
      "coherence_ratio":     7.89,   // coh_b / coh_a
      "silhouette_ratio":   12.29,   // sil_b / sil_a
      "chap_sim_ratio":      0.82,   // chap_b / chap_a
      "pca_variance_ratio": 10.76,   // pca_b / pca_a
      "frac_sharp_ratio":    0.03    // frac_a / frac_b (higher = file_a has more sharp transitions)
    }
  },
  "outputs": { "sklearn_dashboard": "...", "poler_dashboard": "..." },
  "verdict": "<file2> vs <file1>: связность соседних абзацев в 7.9× (0.561 vs 0.071); ..."
}
Key ratios :
| Ratio | Formula | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| coherence_ratio | cos_b / cos_a | > 1: file2 has more lexically coherent paragraph transitions. |
| silhouette_ratio | sil_b / sil_a | > 1: file2 has clearer KMeans cluster separation. |
| chap_sim_ratio | chap_b / chap_a | > 1: file2's chapters are more lexically similar to each other (repetitive if very high). |
| pca_variance_ratio | pca_b / pca_a | > 1: file2 is more compressible to 2-D (sharper topical structure). |
| frac_sharp_ratio | frac_a / frac_b | > 1: file1 has more sharp transitions (worse coherence). |
3. structure — Quick structural analysis
When to use : You only care about lexical / structural metrics — chapter matrix, paragraph transition cosine, KMeans silhouette, PCA, Shannon entropy. No POLER engine , no keyword, no character map. Fastest recipe (~15 s for 100 K words; analyze takes ~20 s for the same file).
Inputs :
| Name | Type | Required | Default |
|---|---|---|---|
| filepaths | list | yes | — (exactly 1) |
| chapter_pattern | str \| None | no | auto |
| lang | str | no | "auto" |
| out_dir | Path \| None | no | — |
Outputs : 4 PNG + report.json + verdict.md .
01_chapter_matrix.png   TF-IDF cosine between chapter centroids
02_transition.png       Neighbour-cosine time series
03_pca.png              2-D PCA, KMeans-coloured
04_entropy.png          Shannon entropy per chapter
Example (real run on the Cassiopeia sample):
python3 -m poler_toolkit structure \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" \
    --label doc_test_structure
Real metrics from report.json :
{
  "meta": {
    "task": "structure",
    "engine": "sklearn (TF-IDF + KMeans + PCA)",
    "timestamp": "2026-06-29T12:42:14",
    "toolkit_version": "1.0.0"
  },
  "inputs": ["$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"],
  "metrics": {
    "total_words": 107652,
    "total_chapters": 35,
    "total_paragraphs": 2835,
    "neighbor_cosine_mean": 0.07101344614578492,
    "neighbor_cosine_std":  0.06845714661777311,
    "frac_below_0.10": 0.7741707833450953,
    "frac_above_0.20": 0.03175723359209598,
    "best_k": 12,
    "silhouette": 0.005655201127629263,
    "pca_explained_variance": 0.01403974204971042,
    "chap_sim_mean_offdiag":  0.5822121155262352,
    "entropy_mean": 8.881348225925969,
    "entropy_std":  0.6172544778406974
  },
  "outputs": { "chapter_matrix": "…", "transition": "…", "pca": "…", "entropy": "…" },
  "verdict": "КЛАСТЕРИ НЕ СКЛАДАЮТЬСЯ — текст розпадається на iзольованi шматки."
}
Real verdict.md :
# Структура: Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt
| Метрика | Значение |
|---|---|
| Слов | 107652 |
| Глав | 35 |
| Абзацев | 2835 |
| Сосед. cosine | 0.071 |
| Доля <0.10 | 77.4% |
| Доля >0.20 | 3.2% |
| Silhouette | 0.006 |
| PCA expl.var | 0.014 |
| Cosine глав | 0.582 |
| Entropy | 8.88 |
## Вердикт
**КЛАСТЕРИ НЕ СКЛАДАЮТЬСЯ — текст розпадається на iзольованi шматки.**
Verdict thresholds (set by report.coherence_verdict(mean_cosine, silhouette) ):
| Condition | Verdict (Ukrainian, English gloss) |
|---|---|
| mean_cosine < 0.10 and silhouette < 0.05 | "КЛАСТЕРИ НЕ СКЛАДАЮТЬСЯ — текст розпадається на iзольованi шматки." (Clusters don't form — text fragments into isolated pieces.) |
| mean_cosine < 0.15 and silhouette < 0.15 | "СЛАБКА СКЛАДАНIСТЬ" — Weak coherence. |
| mean_cosine < 0.25 and silhouette < 0.30 | "СЕРЕДНЯ СКЛАДАНIСТЬ" — Medium coherence. |
| mean_cosine >= 0.25 and silhouette >= 0.30 | "ВИСОКА СКЛАДАНIСТЬ" — High coherence. |
| (any other combo) | "ЗМIШАНА КАРТИНА" — Mixed picture. |
4. characters — Character map
When to use : You want to know who appears where in a text — a per-character timeline of positions, plus a co-occurrence matrix (which characters appear near each other). If you don't supply names , the recipe auto-detects the top-10 capitalised tokens (after filtering pronouns and sentence-initials).
Inputs :
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| filepaths | list | yes | — (exactly 1) |
| names | list[str] \| None | no | auto-detect | List of character names. Case-sensitive ( ignore_case=False is passed to POLER grep_search_file ). |
| out_dir | Path \| None | no | — | — |
Outputs : 2 PNG + report.json + verdict.md .
01_character_map.png   Timeline: one row per character, dots at line positions
02_cooccurrence.png    N×N matrix: co-occurrence within 50-line window
Example (real run):
python3 -m poler_toolkit characters \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" \
    --label doc_test_characters
Real metrics (auto-detect picked top-10 capitalised tokens; pronoun leakage is expected because the stop-word list is short):
{
  "total_words": 107652,
  "characters_tracked": 10,
  "character_counts": {
    "Ти":    378,   "Не":   351,   "Його": 343,   "Люма": 326,
    "Її":    276,   "Роан": 247,   "Міка": 240,   "Юна":  213,
    "Скія":  192,   "Енн":  165
  },
  "protagonist": "Ти",
  "cooccurrence_matrix": [/* 10×10 integer matrix */],
  "cooccurrence_names": ["Ти", "Не", "Його", "Люма", "Її", "Роан", "Міка", "Юна", "Скія", "Енн"]
}
> ⚠️ Pitfall : the auto-detect picks up pronouns ("Ти" = "You", "Не" = "Not", "Його" = "His"). For clean character maps, always pass --names explicitly :
>
> ```bash
> python3 -m poler_toolkit characters FILE --names "Міка,Роан,Люма,Юна,Скія,Енн"
> ```
5. theme_evolution — EMOTIONAL_MARKERS over chapters
When to use : You want to see how the emotional vocabulary of a text changes chapter by chapter. POLER ships a curated list of 60 multi-lingual markers ( хаос , порядок , сила , свідомість , реальність , страх , любов , …). This recipe counts every marker in every chapter, normalises by chapter length (markers per 1000 tokens), ranks markers by total frequency, and renders three visualisations.
> Note : This recipe is in recipes_ext/ . It is not wired into cli.py — invoke it via the Python API, the HTTP API, or directly as a module ( python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.theme_evolution FILE ).
Inputs :
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| filepaths | list | yes | — (exactly 1) | |
| chapter_pattern | str \| None | no | auto | |
| lang | str | no | "auto" | |
| top_n | int | no | 20 | Top-N markers retained for the heatmap + stacked area chart. Line chart always uses top-5. |
| out_dir | Path \| None | no | — | |
Outputs : 3 PNG + report.json + verdict.md .
01_stacked_area.png   Stacked proportions of top-N markers per chapter
02_heatmap.png        Chapters (cols) × top-N markers (rows), magma scale
03_line_top5.png      Top-5 markers' density per chapter
Example (real run on Cassiopeia):
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.theme_evolution \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"
Real verdict.md :
# Theme Evolution: Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt
| Метрика | Значение |
|---|---|
| Файл | $POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE |
| Слов (whitespace) | 107652 |
| Токенов (regex) | 101866 |
| Глав | 35 |
| Маркеров всего | 60 |
| Активных маркеров | 19 |
| Top-N визуализации | 20 |
| Порог 'stable' (глав) | 8 |
| Самый стабильный | реальність |
|   var (stable) | 0.021884 |
|   mean / 1000 | 0.059 |
|   глав с маркером | 8/35 |
| Самый волатильный | хаос |
|   var (volatile) | 0.521958 |
|   mean / 1000 | 0.282 |
|   глав с маркером | 9/35 |
| Глава пик плотности | #33 «Глава 59: Угода з Дияволом (Частина I — План)» |
|   density | 7.82/1000 |
| Глава минимум плотности | #4 «Глава 5: Безіменне небо (Фінальна версія)» |
|   density | 0.00/1000 |
## Вердикт
**Эволюция маркеров: «реальність» — самый стабильный (var=0.0219, mean=0.06/1000, в 8/35 главах);
«хаос» — самый волатильный (var=0.5220, mean=0.28/1000, в 9/35 главах).
Пик эмоциональной плотности — глава «Глава 59: Угода з Дияволом (Частина I — План)» (7.82/1000);
минимум — глава «Глава 5: Безіменне небо (Фінальна версія)» (0.00/1000).
Активных маркеров: 19/60.**
Key metrics :
| Metric | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| markers_total | int | Total markers in POLER's EMOTIONAL_MARKERS (currently 60). |
| markers_appearing | int | How many of those fire at least once in this text. |
| top_markers | list[str] | Top-N markers by total raw frequency. |
| top5_markers | list[str] | Top-5 (subset of top_markers, used in the line chart). |
| chapter_emotional_density_per_1k | list[float] | Per-chapter sum of all marker densities. Length = n_chapters . |
| dominant_marker_per_chapter | list[dict] | One entry per chapter: {chapter_index, title, marker, density_per_1k, unique_markers} . |
| matrix_norm_per_1k | list[list[float]] | Full n_chapters × n_markers normalised density matrix (drop this when you only need the verdict). |
| matrix_raw_counts | list[list[int]] | Same shape, raw counts (pre-normalisation). |
| marker_variance | list[float] | Per-marker variance of norm_per_1k across chapters. |
| marker_chapters_appearing | list[int] | Per-marker count of chapters where it appears at least once. |
| most_stable_marker | dict | Marker with lowest variance among markers appearing in ≥ 25 % of chapters (so singleton markers don't win). |
| most_volatile_marker | dict | Marker with highest variance among all appearing markers. |
| highest_density_chapter | dict | Chapter with max density_per_1k . |
| lowest_density_chapter | dict | Chapter with min density_per_1k . |
| stable_min_chapters_threshold | int | The 25 % threshold used for the "stable" filter. |
6. themes_alpha — Alphabetical inverted word index
When to use : You want a searchable, source-tracked word index — for every word in the text, know which chapter / line / character offset it appears at. Output is paginated alphabetically into index/<LETTER>.json files with three size-budget strategies (per-letter pagination, top-N locations per word, sampled storage for very common words) so a 100 K-word novel produces ~5 MB instead of 10+ MB.
Inputs :
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| filepaths | list | yes | — (exactly 1) | |
| chapter_pattern | str \| None | no | auto | |
| lang | str | no | "auto" | |
| max_locations | int | no | 20 | Max exact locations stored per ordinary word (after the first 5, sampled). |
| top_n | int | no | 100 | Top-frequency words exposed in summary.json and the top-words PNG. |
| out_dir | Path \| None | no | — | |
Outputs : 4 PNG + 3 JSON + report.json + verdict.md + index/ directory.
01_alphabet_histogram.png   Words-per-letter bar chart
02_word_length_hist.png     Word-length distribution
03_top100_words.png         Top-100 words by frequency
04_zipf_curve.png           Log-log rank-vs-frequency
summary.json                Totals, top-N words, alphabet distribution
schema.json                 On-disk layout description
letter_index.json           Per-letter totals + top-10 most frequent
index/A.json, Б.json, …     Paginated inverted index (one file per starting letter)
report.json                 Standard toolkit report
verdict.md                  Standard toolkit verdict
Key metrics (in report.json → metrics ):
| Metric | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| total_words | int | Whitespace-split count. |
| total_tokens_regex | int | Regex-tokenised count (may differ — regex catches don't as one token, whitespace splits it). |
| unique_words | int | Distinct lowercase word forms. |
| alphabet_distribution | dict[str, int] | {"А": 1234, "Б": 567, …} — count of words starting with each letter. |
| top_words | list[dict] | [{"word": "и", "count": 5432, "sample_locations": [...]}, …] |
| total_locations_stored | int | Sum of locations kept across all words (after sampling / max_locations). |
| index_files | list[str] | Relative paths to index/*.json . |
| index_total_bytes | int | Total disk size of the index files. |
7. diff_versions — Compare two versions of a text
When to use : You have two versions of the same narrative (draft v1 vs draft v2, or chapter 12 before and after an edit pass) and want a structured diff: which chapters are the same / changed / removed / added, how much each changed chapter drifted, and POLER's own semantic delta ( diff_files ).
Inputs :
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| filepaths | list | yes | — (exactly 2 ) | [v1, v2] — order matters. |
| keyword | str \| None | no | auto | Picked as the most frequent content token across both files combined so it appears in both. |
| chapter_pattern | str \| None | no | auto | |
| lang | str | no | "auto" | |
| window_size | int | no | 3000 | POLER diff_files window size (characters). |
| out_dir | Path \| None | no | — | |
> ⚠️ Runner limitation : runner.run() does not forward window_size . To override it, call the recipe directly: recipe_diff_versions([f1, f2], window_size=5000, out_dir=…) .
Outputs : 4 PNG + diff.md (raw POLER markdown) + report.json + verdict.md .
01_diff_heatmap.png          v1 × v2 chapter cosine similarity matrix
02_changes_per_chapter.png   Added / removed / changed tokens per chapter
03_wordcount_comparison.png  Side-by-side word count per chapter
04_semantic_drift.png        Best-match cosine per v1 chapter (1.0 = identical)
diff.md                      Raw output of poler_v6.format_diff_markdown(...)
report.json                  Standard toolkit report
verdict.md                   Standard toolkit verdict
Key metrics :
| Metric | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| poler_diff.file1_epsilon | float | POLER ε for file1. |
| poler_diff.file2_epsilon | float | POLER ε for file2. |
| poler_diff.delta_epsilon | float | Δε — how much the "energy" changed. |
| poler_diff.file1_windows | int | Number of analysis windows in file1. |
| poler_diff.delta_windows | int | Window count delta. |
| chapter_classification | list[dict] | One entry per chapter across both files: {side: "v1"|"v2", chapter_index, title, status: "same"|"changed"|"removed"|"added", best_match_index, best_match_similarity, tokens_added, tokens_removed} . |
| same_threshold | float | 0.95 — similarity ≥ this means chapters are essentially identical. |
| match_threshold | float | 0.30 — similarity < this means the chapter was removed/added. |
8. batch_directory — Analyse every file in a directory
When to use : You have a folder of text files (e.g. 50 chapter drafts, or 20 short stories) and want (a) per-file metrics, (b) a cross-file similarity matrix, (c) a 2-D PCA map of files, (d) outlier detection, and (e) POLER's own analyze_directory output.
> ⚠️ CLI limitation : cli.py does not have a batch_directory subcommand. Invoke via Python API, HTTP API, or directly: python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.batch_directory /path/to/dir .
Inputs :
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| filepaths | list | yes | — | Exactly 1 entry — the directory path. (Wrapped in a list for runner compatibility.) |
| keyword | str \| None | no | auto | Shared keyword for POLER analyze_directory . |
| extensions | list[str] \| None | no | [".txt", ".md"] | File extensions to include. |
| recursive | bool | no | True | Walk subdirectories. |
| top_n | int | no | 10 | Top-N for POLER. |
| out_dir | Path \| None | no | — | |
> ⚠️ Runner limitation : runner.run() does not forward extensions or recursive . Call the recipe directly to override.
Outputs : 5 PNG + 4 text/JSON artifacts.
01_file_similarity.png     N×N TF-IDF cosine heatmap
02_pca_files.png           2-D PCA, each file labelled
03_wordcounts.png          Word counts per file (sorted desc)
04_entropy_dist.png        Histogram of Shannon entropy values
05_outliers_radar.png      Radar chart of normalised metrics for top-3 outliers
per_file_report.json       Full per-file metrics
poler_directory.md         POLER's format_directory_markdown output
report.json                Standard toolkit report
verdict.md                 Standard toolkit verdict
Key metrics :
| Metric | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| n_files | int | Number of files analysed. |
| per_file | list[dict] | Per-file: {path, words, chars, lines, entropy, avg_word_len, top5_words} . |
| similarity_matrix | list[list[float]] | N×N cosine similarity (TF-IDF). |
| pca_coords | list[[float, float]] | 2-D PCA coordinates per file. |
| outliers | list[dict] | Files whose max cosine similarity to any other file is < OUTLIER_THRESHOLD (0.20). |
| poler_directory | dict | Raw output of poler_v6.analyze_directory(...) — per-file POLER ε/fragment counts + cross-file clusters. |
9. custom_plugin — Run a Python plugin
When to use : You want to run your own Python code on a text file and get the toolkit's JSON+MD+PNG wrapper around the result. The plugin file must define a run() function with a fixed signature; the toolkit loads it, runs it inside a ThreadPoolExecutor with a hard timeout, validates the return shape, and wraps the output in the standard report format.
Inputs :
| Name | Type | Required | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| filepaths | list | yes | — | If plugin_path is given: list of target files (variable count, ≥ 1). If plugin_path is None : first entry must be the .py plugin file, remaining entries are targets. |
| plugin_path | str \| None | no | None | Path to plugin .py file. |
| timeout | int | no | 300 | Plugin execution timeout in seconds. |
| plugin_opts | dict \| None | no | None | Free-form dict passed to the plugin's run() . |
| out_dir | Path \| None | no | — | |
> ⚠️ Runner limitation : runner.run() does not forward plugin_path , timeout , or plugin_opts . Call the recipe directly.
Plugin contract (the plugin module must define):
def run(
    filepaths,        # list of Path objects (the target files)
    opts,             # dict — the plugin_opts you passed in
    poler,            # poler_v6 module (already imported)
    toolkit_core,     # poler_toolkit.core module
    toolkit_viz,      # poler_toolkit.viz module
    out_dir,          # Path to output directory (already created)
) -> dict:
    """
    Must return a dict with at least:
      - "metrics":  dict           (any JSON-serialisable metrics)
      - "verdict":  str            (one-line human-readable summary)
      - "artifacts": dict          (name -> Path of generated files)
    Optional:
      - "extra_lines": list[str]   (extra Markdown lines appended to verdict.md)
    """
    ...
Outputs : whatever the plugin produces (in artifacts dict) + plugin_info.md + report.json + verdict.md .
plugin_info.md     Signature, docstring, module constants, exec time, peak memory
report.json        Plugin metrics wrapped in standard toolkit report
verdict.md         Plugin verdict + extra_lines
<plugin artifacts> Whatever the plugin wrote (PNG, JSON, …)
Example plugin (minimal):
# /tmp/my_plugin.py
from pathlib import Path
def run(filepaths, opts, poler, toolkit_core, toolkit_viz, out_dir):
    fp = Path(filepaths[0])
    text = fp.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    words = len(text.split())
    unique = len(set(text.lower().split()))
return {
        "metrics": {
            "file": str(fp),
            "words": words,
            "unique_words": unique,
            "ttr": unique / max(words, 1),   # type-token ratio
        },
        "verdict": f"TTR = {unique/max(words,1):.3f} ({unique} unique / {words} total)",
        "artifacts": {},
        "extra_lines": ["", f"- Input file: `{fp}`"],
    }
Invoke:
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.custom_plugin \
    /tmp/my_plugin.py \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"
Or via Python:
from poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.custom_plugin import recipe_custom_plugin
bundle = recipe_custom_plugin(
    ["$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"],
    plugin_path="/tmp/my_plugin.py",
    timeout=60,
    plugin_opts={"min_word_len": 3},
    out_dir=Path("$POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/my_plugin_run"),
)
Sandbox-lite behaviour :
os.environ access → logged at WARNING (allowed).
subprocess.run/Popen/call/check_call/check_output → logged at WARNING (allowed).
socket.socket → logged at WARNING (allowed).
All wrappers restored in a finally block.
Plugin exceptions captured and surfaced as recipe_error in the report — the recipe itself does not crash.
tracemalloc + resource.getrusage report peak memory in plugin_info.md .
HTTP API
The poler_toolkit.serve sub-package exposes all 9 recipes as async REST endpoints. Built with stdlib only ( http.server + threading + json ).
Starting the server
# Foreground (Ctrl-C to stop)
python3 -m poler_toolkit.serve.server --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 --verbose
# Equivalent (via __main__.py)
python3 -m poler_toolkit.serve --port 8000
Server flags:
| Flag | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| --host | 127.0.0.1 | Bind address. |
| --port / -p | 8000 | Port. |
| --verbose / -v | off | DEBUG logging. |
| --quiet / -q | off | WARN+ only. |
Endpoints (14 total)
| Method | Path | Description |
|---|---|---|
| GET  | / | Service info + recipe list. |
| GET  | /health | {"status":"ok","version":"1.0.0","time":"…","tasks_active":N} |
| GET  | /openapi.json | OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec (14 paths). |
| GET  | /recipes | 9 recipes with full option schemas. |
| GET  | /tasks/{task_id} | Poll task status ( pending / running / completed / failed ). |
| POST | /analyze | Submit analyze recipe (1 file). |
| POST | /compare | Submit compare recipe (2 files). |
| POST | /structure | Submit structure recipe (1 file). |
| POST | /characters | Submit characters recipe (1 file). |
| POST | /theme_evolution | Submit theme_evolution recipe (1 file). |
| POST | /themes_alpha | Submit themes_alpha recipe (1 file). |
| POST | /diff_versions | Submit diff_versions recipe (2 files). |
| POST | /batch_directory | Submit batch_directory recipe (1 dir path). |
| POST | /custom_plugin | Submit custom_plugin recipe (variable). |
CORS is enabled on every response ( Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * ); OPTIONS is handled for preflight.
Async pattern
Every POST /<recipe> returns immediately with 202 Accepted and a task_id :
POST /structure HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
{
  "filepaths": ["$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"],
  "options": {"lang": "auto"}
}
Response:
{
  "status": "pending",
  "task_id": "63ebc319f926",
  "recipe": "structure",
  "submitted_at": "2026-06-29T12:35:27",
  "poll_url": "/tasks/63ebc319f926"
}
Then poll GET /tasks/{task_id} every few seconds. States:
pending → running → completed
                  ↘ failed
Completed response (truncated):
{
  "task_id": "63ebc319f926",
  "recipe": "structure",
  "status": "completed",
  "output_dir": "$POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/structure_20260629_123527",
  "report": { "meta": {...}, "inputs": [...], "metrics": {...}, "outputs": {...}, "verdict": "..." },
  "verdict": "КЛАСТЕРИ НЕ СКЛАДАЮТЬСЯ — текст розпадається на iзольованi шматки.",
  "artifacts": {
    "01_chapter_matrix": "<output_root>/01_chapter_matrix.png",
    "02_transition":     "<output_root>/02_transition.png",
    "03_pca":            "<output_root>/03_pca.png",
    "04_entropy":        "<output_root>/04_entropy.png"
  }
}
Failed response:
{
  "task_id": "...",
  "recipe": "structure",
  "status": "failed",
  "error": "POLER engine failed in 'read_epub': ...",
  "error_type": "PolerEngineError",
  "traceback": "..."
}
Task TTL : tasks auto-expire after 3600 s (1 hour) — lazy pruning on every store read/write.
OpenAPI spec
GET /openapi.json returns an OpenAPI 3.0.3 document with:
5 GET paths ( / , /health , /openapi.json , /recipes , /tasks/{task_id} )
9 POST paths (one per recipe)
Per-recipe request body schemas (generated from SERVE_RECIPES[<name>].options )
Shared response schemas: _async_ack_schema (POST 202), _task_status_schema (GET /tasks/{id}), _error_schema (all error responses)
You can paste it into Swagger UI or generate a typed client.
Request body schemas
Every POST endpoint accepts:
{
  "filepaths": ["/abs/path/to/file.txt"],
  "options": { ...recipe-specific... }
}
| Recipe | filepaths | options |
|---|---|---|
| analyze | exactly 1 | keyword , chapter_pattern , lang , top_n |
| compare | exactly 2 | keyword , chapter_pattern , lang |
| structure | exactly 1 | chapter_pattern , lang |
| characters | exactly 1 | names (array) |
| theme_evolution | exactly 1 | chapter_pattern , lang , top_n |
| themes_alpha | exactly 1 | chapter_pattern , lang , max_locations , top_n |
| diff_versions | exactly 2 | keyword , window_size , chapter_pattern , lang |
| batch_directory | exactly 1 (dir path) | keyword , extensions , recursive , top_n |
| custom_plugin | variable | plugin_path , timeout , plugin_opts |
Type coercion: the server coerces simple mismatches ( "15" → 15 , "true" → true ). Unknown options are silently dropped (only declared options are forwarded to the recipe).
Minimal HTTP client (Python stdlib)
import json
import time
import urllib.request
BASE = "http://127.0.0.1:8000"
def submit(recipe, filepaths, options=None):
    body = json.dumps({"filepaths": filepaths, "options": options or {}}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f"{BASE}/{recipe}", data=body,
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
        method="POST",
    )
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as r:
        return json.loads(r.read())["task_id"]
def poll(task_id, interval=3.0, timeout=600.0):
    t0 = time.time()
    while time.time() - t0 < timeout:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(f"{BASE}/tasks/{task_id}") as r:
            data = json.loads(r.read())
        if data["status"] in ("completed", "failed"):
            return data
        time.sleep(interval)
    raise TimeoutError(f"task {task_id} did not finish in {timeout}s")
# Use it:
task_id = submit("structure",
                 ["$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"],
                 {"lang": "auto"})
result = poll(task_id)
print(result["verdict"])
print(result["report"]["metrics"]["silhouette"])
CLI Reference
> ⚠️ Important : as of v1.0.0, the CLI ( cli.py ) only wires up the 4 core recipes : analyze , compare , structure , characters + the list meta-command. The 5 extension recipes ( theme_evolution , themes_alpha , diff_versions , batch_directory , custom_plugin ) are only reachable via:
> - The Python API ( runner.run(...) won't forward their extra options — call the recipe functions directly),
> - The HTTP API ( POST /theme_evolution etc.),
> - Running the recipe module directly: python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.theme_evolution FILE .
Global options
| Flag | Effect |
|---|---|
| --version | Print version and exit. |
| -v / --verbose | DEBUG logging. |
| -q / --quiet | WARN+ only. |
| --output PATH | Root for output dirs (default: $POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT ). |
Per-recipe options
# list available recipes
python3 -m poler_toolkit list
# analyze (1 file)
python3 -m poler_toolkit analyze FILE \
    [--keyword X] [--lang L] [--chapter-pattern REGEX] \
    [--top-n N] [--label LABEL]
# compare (2 files)
python3 -m poler_toolkit compare FILE1 FILE2 \
    [--keyword X] [--lang L] [--chapter-pattern REGEX] [--label LABEL]
# structure (1 file, fast)
python3 -m poler_toolkit structure FILE \
    [--lang L] [--chapter-pattern REGEX] [--label LABEL]
# characters (1 file)
python3 -m poler_toolkit characters FILE \
    [--names "A,B,C"] [--label LABEL]
--lang choices: auto , ru , ua , en , inline_ru , inline_en .
Python API
runner.run() signature
from poler_toolkit import runner
out_dir: Path = runner.run(
    recipe_name: str,                 # "analyze" | "compare" | "structure" | "characters"
    filepaths: list[str | Path],
    *,
    keyword: str | None = None,
    chapter_pattern: str | None = None,
    lang: str = "auto",
    top_n: int = 15,
    names: list[str] | None = None,
    output_root: Path | None = None,  # default: $POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT
    task_label: str | None = None,    # default: recipe_name
) -> Path                              # the created output directory
runner.run() validates recipe_name against recipes.RECIPES , checks the file count, creates <output_root>/<label>_<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>/ , calls the recipe, and writes report.json + verdict.md . Returns the output directory's Path .
> ⚠️ Known limitation : runner.run() only forwards keyword , chapter_pattern , lang , top_n , names . Extension recipes that need other options ( window_size , extensions , recursive , max_locations , plugin_path , timeout , plugin_opts ) cannot be fully driven through runner.run() . Two workarounds:
>
> 1. Call the recipe function directly — seeDirect recipe invocationbelow.
> 2. Use the HTTP API — serve/server.py has its own dispatcher ( _dispatch_recipe ) that calls each recipe function with the full option set and replicates the report.json + verdict.md save step.
Direct recipe invocation
from pathlib import Path
from poler_toolkit.recipes import recipe_analyze, recipe_structure
from poler_toolkit.recipes_ext import (
    recipe_theme_evolution,
    recipe_themes_alpha,
    recipe_diff_versions,
    recipe_batch_directory,
    recipe_custom_plugin,
)
from poler_toolkit import report
out_dir = Path("$POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/manual_run")
out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
# Call directly with any option the recipe supports:
bundle = recipe_theme_evolution(
    ["$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"],
    lang="auto",
    top_n=15,
    out_dir=out_dir,
)
# bundle = {"report": dict, "verdict_lines": list[str], "artifacts": dict[str, Path]}
report.save_json_report(bundle["report"], out_dir, filename="report.json")
report.save_markdown_verdict(bundle["verdict_lines"], out_dir, filename="verdict.md")
print(out_dir / "report.json")
This pattern is also what poler_toolkit.serve.server._dispatch_recipe does internally.
Common patterns
Pattern 1 — Run a recipe and read the JSON :
import json
from poler_toolkit import runner
out_dir = runner.run("structure", [file_path], task_label="quick")
metrics = json.loads((out_dir / "report.json").read_text())["metrics"]
print(metrics["silhouette"], metrics["neighbor_cosine_mean"])
Pattern 2 — Run several recipes on the same file, share the output dir :
from pathlib import Path
from poler_toolkit.recipes import recipe_structure
from poler_toolkit.recipes_ext import recipe_theme_evolution
from poler_toolkit import report, core
out_dir = Path("$POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/multi_recipe")
out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
fp = "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"
for fn, name in [(recipe_structure, "structure"),
                 (recipe_theme_evolution, "theme_evolution")]:
    sub = out_dir / name
    sub.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    bundle = fn([fp], out_dir=sub)
    report.save_json_report(bundle["report"], sub, "report.json")
    report.save_markdown_verdict(bundle["verdict_lines"], sub, "verdict.md")
Pattern 3 — Iterate over a directory, no recipe wrapper :
from pathlib import Path
from poler_toolkit.recipes import recipe_structure
from poler_toolkit import report
for fp in sorted(Path("./upload").glob("*.txt")):
    out = Path("$POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT") / f"{fp.stem}_struct"
    out.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    bundle = recipe_structure([fp], out_dir=out)
    report.save_json_report(bundle["report"], out, "report.json")
Pattern 4 — Call the HTTP API from inside Python (seeMinimal HTTP clientabove).
Output Format
Directory layout
Every recipe (via runner.run() or via serve.server ) writes to a timestamped directory:
$POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/  (default root, override via --output or output_root)
├── analyze_mika_20260629_124530/
│   ├── 01_chapter_matrix_sklearn.png
│   ├── 02_chapter_matrix_poler.png
│   ├── 03_transition_smoothness.png
│   ├── 04_pca_scatter.png
│   ├── 05_entropy.png
│   ├── 06_cluster_sizes.png
│   ├── 07_character_map.png
│   ├── report.json          ← machine-readable
│   └── verdict.md           ← human-readable summary
├── structure_20260629_124155/
│   ├── 01_chapter_matrix.png
│   ├── 02_transition.png
│   ├── 03_pca.png
│   ├── 04_entropy.png
│   ├── report.json
│   └── verdict.md
└── ...
Directory name format: <safe_label>_<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>/ where safe_label has non-alphanumeric chars replaced with _ (so analyze-mika → analyze_mika ).
report.json schema
Every recipe uses the same envelope (built by report.wrap_report ):
{
  "meta": {
    "task":            "string — recipe name",
    "engine":          "string — e.g. 'POLER v6 + sklearn'",
    "timestamp":       "ISO 8601 (seconds precision)",
    "toolkit_version": "1.0.0"
  },
  "inputs":  ["absolute file paths..."],
  "metrics": { /* recipe-specific — see per-recipe sections above */ },
  "outputs": { "artifact_name": "absolute path to PNG/MD file", ... },
  "verdict": "one-line human-readable summary string",
  "extra":   { /* optional recipe-specific extras */ }
}
JSON Schema form (abridged — seeAppendix Afor the full version):
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "title": "poler_toolkit.report",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["meta", "inputs", "metrics", "outputs", "verdict"],
  "properties": {
    "meta": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["task", "engine", "timestamp", "toolkit_version"],
      "properties": {
        "task":            {"type": "string"},
        "engine":          {"type": "string"},
        "timestamp":       {"type": "string", "format": "date-time"},
        "toolkit_version": {"type": "string"}
      }
    },
    "inputs":  {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}},
    "metrics": {"type": "object"},
    "outputs": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": {"type": "string"}},
    "verdict": {"type": "string"},
    "extra":   {"type": "object"}
  }
}
verdict.md format
Markdown with this structure (built by report.verdict_block + report.metric_table ):
# <Title: e.g. "Анализ: filename.txt">
| <Col1> | <Col2> | ... |
|---|---|---|
| ... | ... | ... |
## Вердикт
**<one-line verdict string>**
For compare and diff_versions , the table has 4 columns (Metric, File1, File2, Ratio).
Metric Interpretation
Cosine similarity (neighbour)
Definition : for every adjacent pair of paragraphs (p_{i-1}, p_i) , compute cosine(tfidf(p_{i-1}), tfidf(p_i)) . Then take the mean over all pairs.
Range : [0, 1] (TF-IDF vectors are non-negative).
Interpretation :
| Mean cosine | Verdict | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| < 0.10 | very low | Paragraphs are lexically disjoint; reader feels "jumping". |
| 0.10–0.20 | low | Some continuity, but mostly separate scenes. |
| 0.20–0.40 | medium | Healthy for mixed-scene novels. |
| > 0.40 | high | Paragraphs reuse vocabulary heavily — possibly repetitive. |
Real examples from this toolkit:
Cassiopeia ( structure ): 0.071 → "КЛАСТЕРИ НЕ СКЛАДАЮТЬСЯ"
Сфера Предела EPUB ( structure ): 0.561 → suspiciously high — likely because the EPUB was read as one giant paragraph per chapter (51 paragraphs across 45 chapters → most "adjacent" pairs are within the same chapter).
Silhouette score
Definition : standard sklearn silhouette_score(X, kmeans.labels_, sample_size=min(2000, N)) . Computed for k ∈ [3, 12] ; the k with the highest silhouette is reported as best_k .
Range : [-1, 1] . In practice for text TF-IDF: usually 0.0–0.30 .
Interpretation :
| Silhouette | Verdict | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| < 0.05 | no natural clusters | Paragraphs don't separate into K groups. |
| 0.05–0.15 | weak clusters | Some structure but mostly noise. |
| 0.15–0.30 | medium clusters | Reasonable topical separation. |
| > 0.30 | strong clusters | Clear topical buckets (often means very diverse text — could be an anthology). |
PCA explained variance
Definition : pca.explained_variance_ratio_.sum() for PCA(n_components=2) . The fraction of TF-IDF variance captured by the first 2 principal components.
Range : [0, 1] (typically 0.01–0.30 for text).
Interpretation :
| PCA expl.var | Meaning |
|---|---|
| < 0.05 | High-dimensional text; 2-D PCA is mostly noise. Don't read too much into the scatter plot. |
| 0.05–0.15 | Some 2-D structure visible. |
| > 0.15 | Strong 2-D structure — the PCA scatter is meaningful. |
Shannon entropy
Definition : for each chapter, -sum(p_i * log2(p_i)) over word-type frequencies p_i = count(word) / total_words . Reported as bits/token .
Range : [0, log2(V)] where V = vocabulary size. For 100 K-word Russian texts: typically 8.5–10.5 .
Interpretation :
| Entropy (bits/token) | Meaning |
|---|---|
| < 8.0 | Very repetitive vocabulary (children's book, heavily formatted text). |
| 8.0–9.0 | Normal prose. |
| 9.0–10.0 | Rich vocabulary (literary fiction, technical writing). |
| > 10.0 | Possibly noisy (random tokens, mixed languages, OCR artefacts). |
Real examples:
Cassiopeia mean: 8.88 , std: 0.62 (normal Ukrainian/Russian prose).
Сфера Предела EPUB: 9.44 , std: 0.58 (slightly richer).
EMOTIONAL_MARKERS
Definition : POLER v6 ships a curated list of 60 multi-lingual markers (RU / UK / EN): хаос , порядок , сила , свідомість , істота , реальність , страх , любов , свобода , надія , тінь , світло , … ( poler_v6.EMOTIONAL_MARKERS ).
In analyze : every chapter's body is tokenised, marker counts are collected into a n_chapters × 60 matrix, then a marker×marker cosine similarity is computed (the 02_chapter_matrix_poler.png artifact).
In theme_evolution : the same matrix is normalised per 1000 tokens, markers are ranked by total raw frequency, and the top-N are visualised.
Reading the heatmap :
Rows = markers, columns = chapters (or vice versa in some recipes).
magma colour scale: bright = high density.
A row that's bright everywhere → "stable" marker (consistent emotional vocabulary).
A row with one bright cell → "volatile" marker (spike in one chapter).
An all-dark row → marker never fires in this text.
Real example (Cassiopeia):
19/60 markers fire at least once.
Most stable: реальність (var = 0.022, present in 8/35 chapters).
Most volatile: хаос (var = 0.522, present in 9/35 chapters).
Peak density chapter: Глава 59: Угода з Дияволом at 7.82/1000 .
Cluster sizes
Definition : when analyze calls POLER cluster_fragments(fragments, adaptive=True) , the result is a list of clusters; each cluster is a list of fragments. cluster_sizes[i] is len(clusters[i]) .
Interpretation :
2–6 clusters with sizes [10, 5, 2] → healthy: keyword appears in 3 distinct contexts.
1 cluster with all fragments → keyword has a single usage mode.
15 clusters of size 1 each → fragments are too dissimilar; keyword is too generic.
Empty list → keyword fired 0 times (try a different keyword).
Real example (Cassiopeia, keyword= Міка ):
3 clusters, sizes [2, 1, 12] — POLER found 3 contexts; the largest has 12 fragments.
245 total occurrences, 15 fragments returned (top-N=15), 182 unique fragments.
Common Pitfalls
1. Encoding (BOM, CRLF, UTF-8)
Symptom : text reads fine in your editor but POLER grep_search_file returns 0 hits, or the chapter auto-detect fails.
Cause : file has a UTF-8 BOM ( \ufeff ) at the start, or Windows line endings ( \r\n ).
Fix : core.read_text_file already normalises both — it uses encoding="utf-8-sig" (which strips BOM) and replaces \r\n → \n . If you're reading the file yourself outside the toolkit, do the same.
2. Empty chapters / "only N paragraphs" error
Symptom : RecipeError: only 3 paragraphs after filtering .
Cause : recipes._sklearn_features requires at least 4 paragraphs (with len(p.split()) >= 4 words). If your file has no \n\n paragraph breaks, core._split_paragraphs falls back to grouping 5 sentences per paragraph — but very short files may still fail.
Fix : ensure the file has blank-line paragraph breaks. For EPUBs that lost their breaks (see next pitfall), use --lang inline_ru so chapter detection still works.
3. EPUB without paragraph breaks
Symptom : structure on an EPUB returns suspicious metrics — very high neighbor_cosine_mean (> 0.5), very low total_paragraphs relative to total_chapters .
Real example : Сфера Предела EPUB:
{
  "total_words": 172865,
  "total_chapters": 45,
  "total_paragraphs": 51,         // ← only 51 paragraphs across 45 chapters!
  "neighbor_cosine_mean": 0.561,  // ← suspiciously high
  "frac_above_0.20":     0.98     // ← 98 % of paragraph pairs are very similar
}
Cause : the EPUB's paragraphs were merged during poler_v6.read_epub extraction (likely the source HTML used <br> instead of <p> tags, or the converter stripped them).
Fix :
Use --lang inline_ru to detect chapters by ГЛАВА N anywhere in the text (not just at line start).
Pre-process the EPUB to inject \n\n at sentence boundaries.
Or convert the EPUB to .txt manually with ebook-convert (Calibre) and use the .txt version.
4. Large files (memory)
Symptom : MemoryError or KMeans takes > 60 s.
Cause : TF-IDF matrix gets large. For a 100 K-word file with 2 835 paragraphs, X.shape = (2835, 10000) — that's 28 M cells, ~110 MB sparse, ~225 MB dense (PCA converts to dense). 1 M-word file → 10× that.
Fix :
Use structure instead of analyze (skips POLER engine, saves ~30 % memory).
Pre-split the file into halves and run twice.
For directory-level analysis, use batch_directory (per-file metrics are cheap; the cross-file TF-IDF is bounded by N_files , not file size).
5. Special characters in keywords
Symptom : --keyword "д'Артаньян" returns 0 occurrences.
Cause : POLER's grep_search_file is case-sensitive by default for characters , but analyze passes ignore_case=True . The apostrophe in д'Артаньян is also tricky — it could be ' (U+0027), ’ (U+2019), or ' (U+2018).
Fix :
Try the keyword without the apostrophe: --keyword Артаньян .
If you must keep it, try all three apostrophe variants.
For characters --names , the recipe passes ignore_case=False to POLER — pass the name exactly as it appears in the text (case-sensitive).
6. Auto-detected keyword is a stop-word
Symptom : analyze picks keyword не or что (very frequent but meaningless).
Cause : core._AUTODETECT_STOP has a finite stop-word list. Slang or dialectal forms may slip through.
Fix : always pass --keyword explicitly when you have a specific character or theme in mind.
7. runner.run() doesn't forward extension options
Symptom : you call runner.run("theme_evolution", [fp], top_n=5) and the recipe still uses top_n=20 .
Cause : runner.run() only forwards keyword , chapter_pattern , lang , top_n , names . For theme_evolution , top_n IS forwarded — but max_locations (themes_alpha), window_size (diff_versions), extensions / recursive (batch_directory), plugin_path / timeout / plugin_opts (custom_plugin) are NOT.
Fix : call the recipe function directly (seeDirect recipe invocation) or use the HTTP API.
8. Custom plugin returns non-dict
Symptom : RecipeError: plugin did not return a dict .
Fix : the plugin's run() must return a dict with at least metrics (dict), verdict (str), artifacts (dict). extra_lines (list[str]) is optional. Any other keys are silently ignored.
9. CLI doesn't know about extension recipes
Symptom : python3 -m poler_toolkit theme_evolution FILE → error: argument RECIPE: invalid choice .
Cause : as of v1.0.0, cli.py only has subcommands for the 4 core recipes.
Fix : use one of:
# Direct module invocation (some ext recipes support this):
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.theme_evolution FILE
# HTTP API:
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/theme_evolution \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"filepaths": ["FILE"]}'
# Python direct call:
python3 -c "from poler_toolkit.recipes_ext import recipe_theme_evolution; \
            recipe_theme_evolution(['FILE'], out_dir=__import__('pathlib').Path('/tmp/out'))"
Extension Guide
Adding a new recipe
The recipe contract is small. A recipe is a function:
def recipe_my_thing(
    filepaths: list[str | Path],
    *,
    out_dir: Path | None = None,
    # ... your custom options ...
) -> dict:
    """
    Returns:
        {
            "report":       dict,         # for report.json
            "verdict_lines": list[str],   # for verdict.md (joined with "\n")
            "artifacts":    dict[str, Path],  # name -> file path
        }
    """
Steps:
Implement the recipe . Put it in recipes.py (core) or recipes_ext/<name>.py (extension). Use from . import core, viz, report and from .errors import RecipeError, ConfigurationError .
Validate inputs at the top:
if len(filepaths) != 1:
       raise ConfigurationError("my_thing expects exactly 1 file")
Read the file via core.read_any(path) -> (text, fmt) — handles .txt , .md , .epub uniformly.
Split chapters via core.split_chapters(text, pattern=None, lang="auto") if you need chapter-level metrics.
Call POLER via core.safe_call("function_name", *args, **kwargs) — wraps poler_v6.<func> and raises PolerEngineError on failure.
Render PNGs via viz.<helper>(...) . If you need a plot type that viz.py doesn't have, render it locally with matplotlib (use matplotlib.use("Agg") and viz.DPI for consistency).
Build the report via report.wrap_report(task_name, engine, input_files, metrics, output_files, verdict) .
Build the verdict lines via report.verdict_block(title, report.metric_table(headers, rows), ["## Вердикт", "", f" {verdict} ", ""]) .
Register the recipe:
Core: add to recipes.RECIPES .
Extension: add to recipes_ext/__init__.py imports + (optionally) to serve.SERVE_RECIPES for HTTP exposure.
(Optional) CLI : add a subparser in cli.py and a dispatch branch in main() .
Plugin contract (for custom_plugin )
See thecustom_plugin recipe sectionfor the full signature. The contract is enforced — violations raise RecipeError with a helpful hint:
| Violation | Error |
|---|---|
| Plugin file doesn't exist | RecipeError: plugin file not found |
| Plugin module doesn't define run | RecipeError: plugin '<path>' does not define a callable 'run' |
| run() raises | Captured, surfaced as recipe_error in the report (recipe itself doesn't crash) |
| run() times out | RecipeError: plugin timed out after Ns (worker thread is daemonised and abandoned) |
| run() returns non-dict | RecipeError: plugin did not return a dict |
| Return dict missing metrics / verdict / artifacts | RecipeError: plugin return dict missing required key: '<key>' |
Testing patterns
For a new recipe, smoke-test with:
# test_my_recipe.py
import json
from pathlib import Path
from poler_toolkit.recipes_ext import recipe_my_thing  # or from ..recipes
def test_smoke():
    out_dir = Path("/tmp/test_my_recipe")
    out_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    bundle = recipe_my_thing(
        ["$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"],
        out_dir=out_dir,
    )
    assert "report" in bundle
    assert "verdict_lines" in bundle
    assert "artifacts" in bundle
    assert bundle["report"]["meta"]["task"] == "my_thing"
    # Every artifact path must exist
    for name, p in bundle["artifacts"].items():
        assert Path(p).exists(), f"artifact {name} not written: {p}"
Use the canonical Cassiopeia file ( $POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE ) as a smoke-test fixture — it's 107 652 words, 35 chapters, 2 835 paragraphs, mixed Ukrainian/Russian, and exercises every code path.
Examples
> Full worked examples (with input + command + output + interpretation) are inEXAMPLES.md. Summary:
Compare two versions of a novel— diff_versions on draft v1 vs v2.
Find outliers in a directory— batch_directory on 20 short stories.
Track emotional arc of a character— analyze + theme_evolution stacked.
Build a custom analyzer plugin— custom_plugin with a TTR calculator.
Quick health check on an EPUB— structure on Сфера Предела, diagnose the "no paragraph breaks" issue.
Compare two unrelated novels— compare on Cassiopeia vs Сфера Предела.
HTTP API end-to-end— submit + poll + interpret from a curl one-liner.
Appendix
Appendix A: Full JSON schema
report.json envelope (every recipe):
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "title": "poler_toolkit.report",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["meta", "inputs", "metrics", "outputs", "verdict"],
  "properties": {
    "meta": {
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["task", "engine", "timestamp", "toolkit_version"],
      "properties": {
        "task":            {"type": "string", "enum": [
          "analyze", "compare", "structure", "characters",
          "theme_evolution", "themes_alpha", "diff_versions",
          "batch_directory", "custom_plugin"
        ]},
        "engine":          {"type": "string"},
        "timestamp":       {"type": "string", "format": "date-time"},
        "toolkit_version": {"type": "string"}
      }
    },
    "inputs":  {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}},
    "metrics": {"type": "object"},
    "outputs": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": {"type": "string"}},
    "verdict": {"type": "string"},
    "extra":   {"type": "object"}
  }
}
metrics for analyze (see recipe section for the others):
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "total_words":  {"type": "integer"},
    "total_chapters": {"type": "integer"},
    "total_paragraphs": {"type": "integer"},
    "keyword": {"type": "string"},
    "poler": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "total_occurrences": {"type": "integer"},
        "unique_fragments": {"type": "integer"},
        "fragments_returned": {"type": "integer"},
        "n_clusters": {"type": "integer"},
        "cluster_sizes": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "integer"}}
      }
    },
    "sklearn": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "neighbor_cosine_mean": {"type": "number"},
        "neighbor_cosine_std": {"type": "number"},
        "frac_below_0.05": {"type": "number"},
        "frac_below_0.10": {"type": "number"},
        "frac_above_0.20": {"type": "number"},
        "best_k": {"type": "integer"},
        "silhouette": {"type": "number"},
        "silhouettes_all": {"type": "object", "additionalProperties": {"type": "number"}},
        "pca_explained_variance": {"type": "number"},
        "chap_sim_mean_offdiag": {"type": "number"},
        "X_shape": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "integer"}}
      }
    },
    "shannon_entropy": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "mean": {"type": "number"},
        "std":  {"type": "number"},
        "min":  {"type": "number"},
        "max":  {"type": "number"}
      }
    },
    "emotional_markers_found": {"type": "integer"},
    "emotional_markers_total": {"type": "integer"}
  }
}
Appendix B: Glossary
| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Recipe | A function recipe_<name>(filepaths, **opts) -> bundle that produces a JSON report + Markdown verdict + PNG artifacts. 9 are shipped. |
| Bundle | The dict returned by every recipe: {"report": dict, "verdict_lines": list[str], "artifacts": dict[str, Path]} . |
| POLER v6 | The underlying text-analysis engine ( poler_v6.py in /path/to/poler_toolkit/ ). Provides analyze_large_file , cluster_fragments , grep_search_file , read_epub , diff_files , analyze_directory , EMOTIONAL_MARKERS (60 multi-lingual markers), etc. |
| POLER ε (epsilon) | POLER's per-file "energy" metric — a weighted sum of fragment salience. Higher = more semantically concentrated text around the keyword. |
| POLER fragment | A window of text around a keyword occurrence, ranked by salience. top_n controls how many are returned. |
| POLER cluster | A group of similar fragments (via cluster_fragments(fragments, adaptive=True) ). |
| TF-IDF | Term Frequency × Inverse Document Frequency. The toolkit uses TfidfVectorizer with word (1,2)-grams + char_wb (3,5)-grams, 5000 features each, min_df=2 , sublinear_tf=True . |
| KMeans | Clustering on the TF-IDF matrix. Toolkit tries k ∈ [3, 12] and picks the k with the best silhouette. |
| Silhouette score | sklearn.metrics.silhouette_score — measures how well each sample fits its cluster vs the nearest other cluster. [-1, 1] , higher is better. |
| PCA | Principal Component Analysis, sklearn.decomposition.PCA(n_components=2) . Used for the 2-D scatter plot. |
| Shannon entropy | -sum(p * log2(p)) over word-type frequencies, in bits/token. Lexical diversity metric. |
| EMOTIONAL_MARKERS | POLER v6's curated list of 60 multi-lingual (RU/UK/EN) emotional / conceptual keywords: хаос , порядок , сила , свідомість , реальність , страх , любов , свобода , надія , тінь , світло , … |
| Coherence verdict | The auto-generated 1-line verdict from report.coherence_verdict(mean_cosine, silhouette) . Has 5 buckets: "КЛАСТЕРИ НЕ СКЛАДАЮТЬСЯ" / "СЛАБКА" / "СЕРЕДНЯ" / "ВИСОКА СКЛАДАНIСТЬ" / "ЗМIШАНА КАРТИНА". |
| Co-occurrence (characters) | For characters recipe: counts how often two characters appear within ±50 lines of each other. Stored in metrics.cooccurrence_matrix (N×N integer matrix). |
| Task (HTTP API) | An async job submitted via POST /<recipe> . Has a 12-hex-char task_id and a state machine: pending → running → completed | failed . Auto-expires after 1 hour. |
Appendix C: Changelog
| Version | Date | Changes |
|---|---|---|
| 1.0.0 | 2026-06-29 | Initial toolkit release. 4 core recipes ( analyze , compare , structure , characters ) + Stage 2 added 5 extension recipes ( theme_evolution , themes_alpha , diff_versions , batch_directory , custom_plugin ) + HTTP API server ( poler_toolkit.serve ) with 14 endpoints and OpenAPI 3.0.3 spec. |
Appendix D: File map
poler_toolkit/
├── __init__.py                  package metadata, __version__ = "1.0.0"
├── __main__.py                  entry: python -m poler_toolkit
├── cli.py                       argparse CLI (4 core subcommands + list)
├── runner.py                    runner.run() orchestrator
├── recipes.py                   4 core recipes + RECIPES dict
├── core.py                      read_any / split_chapters / pick_keyword / safe_call
├── viz.py                       matplotlib helpers (chapter_matrix, PCA, entropy, …)
├── report.py                    save_json_report / save_markdown_verdict / wrap_report / coherence_verdict
├── errors.py                    PolerToolkitError hierarchy
├── README.md                    human-facing CLI cheat sheet (Russian)
├── docs/
│   ├── AI_USAGE.md              ← this file
│   └── EXAMPLES.md              worked examples
├── recipes_ext/                 Stage 2 extension recipes
│   ├── __init__.py              re-exports 5 recipe functions
│   ├── theme_evolution.py       EMOTIONAL_MARKERS across chapters (3 PNG)
│   ├── themes_alpha.py          alphabetical inverted index (4 PNG + index/)
│   ├── diff_versions.py         POLER diff_files + chapter classification (4 PNG + diff.md)
│   ├── batch_directory.py       directory-level analysis (5 PNG + per_file_report.json)
│   └── custom_plugin.py         external Python plugin runner (sandbox-lite)
└── serve/                       HTTP API sub-package
    ├── __init__.py              SERVE_RECIPES registry (9 recipes, typed options)
    ├── __main__.py              `python -m poler_toolkit.serve` entry
    ├── server.py                BaseHTTPRequestHandler + ThreadingHTTPServer (533 LOC)
    ├── openapi_spec.py          OpenAPI 3.0.3 generator (385 LOC)
    └── task_store.py            thread-safe in-memory task store with 1-hour TTL
Appendix E: Quick reference card
# List recipes
python3 -m poler_toolkit list
# Quick structural check (fastest)
python3 -m poler_toolkit structure FILE --label my_run
# Deep dive on a keyword
python3 -m poler_toolkit analyze FILE --keyword NAME --label my_run
# Compare two files
python3 -m poler_toolkit compare FILE1 FILE2 --label cmp
# Character map (explicit names recommended)
python3 -m poler_toolkit characters FILE --names "A,B,C"
# Extension recipes — direct module invocation
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.theme_evolution FILE
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.themes_alpha FILE
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.diff_versions FILE1 FILE2
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.batch_directory /path/to/dir
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.custom_plugin PLUGIN.py TARGET...
# HTTP API
python3 -m poler_toolkit.serve --port 8000
curl http://127.0.0.1:8000/recipes
curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8000/structure \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"filepaths":["FILE"]}'
curl http://127.0.0.1:8000/tasks/<task_id> # Python — via runner (4 core recipes only)
from poler_toolkit import runner
out_dir = runner.run("structure", ["/path/to/file"], task_label="my_run")
# Python — direct recipe call (all 9 recipes, full option set)
from poler_toolkit.recipes_ext import recipe_theme_evolution
bundle = recipe_theme_evolution(["/path/to/file"], top_n=15, out_dir=out_dir)
# Python — read the result
import json
report = json.loads((out_dir / "report.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
print(report["verdict"])
print(report["metrics"])
End of AI_USAGE.md — for worked examples seeEXAMPLES.md; for human-facing CLI reference see../README.md.
EXAMPLES.md
Файл: poler_toolkit/docs/EXAMPLES.md · Размер: 41 729 байт
poler_toolkit — Worked Examples
> Companion file toAI_USAGE.md. Each example shows the input , the command (CLI / Python / HTTP), the output , and a short interpretation . All numbers come from real runs against files in ./upload/ .
Table of contents
Example 1 — Compare two versions of a novelExample 2 — Find outliers in a directoryExample 3 — Track the emotional arc of a textExample 4 — Build a custom analyzer pluginExample 5 — Quick health check on an EPUBExample 6 — Compare two unrelated novelsExample 7 — HTTP API end-to-endExample 8 — Quick structural diagnosisExample 9 — Character map with auto-detectExample 10 — Stacked recipes on one file
Example 1
Compare two versions of a novel ( diff_versions )
> Scenario : An author sends you chapter_v1.txt and chapter_v2.txt . You want a structured diff: which chapters are the same / changed / removed / added, and how much each changed chapter drifted.
Setup — for this example we use two close-but-different files: the Cassiopeia canon ( .txt ) and the Сфера Предела EPUB. In real life you'd diff two drafts of the same novel; here we use two unrelated files to exercise the "added/removed/changed" classifier on every chapter.
Input :
$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE   (107 652 words, 35 chapters)
$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE_2                        (172 865 words, 45 chapters)
Command (CLI — note diff_versions isn't wired into cli.py yet, so use the module directly):
cd /path/to/poler_toolkit
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.diff_versions \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE_2"
Python equivalent :
from pathlib import Path
from poler_toolkit.recipes_ext import recipe_diff_versions
from poler_toolkit import report
out_dir = Path("$POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/diff_cas_vs_sphere")
out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
bundle = recipe_diff_versions(
    [
        "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE",
        "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE_2",
    ],
    keyword=None,        # auto-detect across both files
    window_size=3000,
    lang="auto",
    out_dir=out_dir,
)
report.save_json_report(bundle["report"], out_dir, "report.json")
report.save_markdown_verdict(bundle["verdict_lines"], out_dir, "verdict.md")
Output — 4 PNG + diff.md + report.json + verdict.md in $POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/diff_versions_<timestamp>/ :
01_diff_heatmap.png          v1 × v2 chapter cosine similarity matrix
02_changes_per_chapter.png   Added / removed / changed tokens per chapter
03_wordcount_comparison.png  Side-by-side word count per chapter
04_semantic_drift.png        Best-match cosine per v1 chapter
diff.md                      Raw POLER format_diff_markdown output
report.json
verdict.md
Key report.json metrics (real run):
{
  "meta": {
    "task": "diff_versions",
    "engine": "POLER v6 diff_files + chapter TF-IDF cosine + difflib",
    "timestamp": "2026-06-29T…",
    "toolkit_version": "1.0.0"
  },
  "inputs": [
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE",
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE_2"
  ],
  "metrics": {
    "poler_diff": {
      "file1_epsilon":   7.34,
      "file2_epsilon":   9.12,
      "delta_epsilon":   1.78,
      "file1_windows":   222,
      "file2_windows":   358,
      "delta_windows":   136
    },
    "chapter_classification": [
      {"side": "v1", "chapter_index": 0,  "title": "Пролог: Останній Місяць",
       "status": "changed", "best_match_index": 12,
       "best_match_similarity": 0.41, "tokens_added": 412, "tokens_removed": 388},
      {"side": "v1", "chapter_index": 1,  "title": "Глава 3: Тріщина, Що Дихае",
       "status": "removed", "best_match_index": 7,
       "best_match_similarity": 0.18, "tokens_added": 0, "tokens_removed": 0},
      {"side": "v2", "chapter_index": 0,  "title": "Глава 1",
       "status": "added", "best_match_index": 3,
       "best_match_similarity": 0.09, "tokens_added": 0, "tokens_removed": 0},
      // … one entry per chapter across both files
    ],
    "same_threshold":   0.95,
    "match_threshold":  0.30,
    "n_v1_chapters":    35,
    "n_v2_chapters":    45,
    "n_same":           0,
    "n_changed":        21,
    "n_removed":        14,
    "n_added":          24
  },
  "outputs": {
    "diff_heatmap":            "<output_root>/01_diff_heatmap.png",
    "changes_per_chapter":     "<output_root>/02_changes_per_chapter.png",
    "wordcount_comparison":    "<output_root>/03_wordcount_comparison.png",
    "semantic_drift":          "<output_root>/04_semantic_drift.png",
    "diff_md":                 "<output_root>/diff.md"
  },
  "verdict": "v1 vs v2: 21 chapters changed (avg drift 0.38), 14 removed, 24 added. POLER Δε = +1.78 (v2 has higher keyword energy)."
}
Interpretation :
poler_diff.delta_epsilon = +1.78 — the POLER "energy" grew from v1 to v2, meaning v2 has more semantic concentration around the auto-detected keyword. Read this together with delta_windows = +136 (v2 has 60 % more analysis windows → it's longer, so a higher ε is expected).
n_changed = 21, n_removed = 14, n_added = 24 — most chapters don't have a clean 1:1 match; this is expected because the two files are unrelated novels. For a real version-diff of the same novel, expect mostly changed and a handful of added / removed .
best_match_similarity range : this is what the "drift curve" PNG plots. Spikes at 1.0 = identical chapters; dips near 0 = chapters that were rewritten beyond recognition.
same_threshold = 0.95 : chapters with cosine ≥ 0.95 are flagged same . Lower this if your text uses heavy punctuation changes that depress cosine.
> Pitfall (seeAI_USAGE.md → Common Pitfalls #3): the Сфера Предела EPUB has only 51 paragraphs across 45 chapters. This skews the chapter classification toward added / removed because per-chapter TF-IDF vectors become unreliable. For a fair comparison, convert the EPUB to .txt first or pre-process to inject paragraph breaks.
Example 2
Find outliers in a directory ( batch_directory )
> Scenario : You have 20 short stories in a folder and want to find which one is the odd one out — different genre, different author, or just structurally anomalous.
Setup — for this example we'll point at the notebooklm_studio/ subfolder of ./upload/ , which contains 9 Markdown essays on Etheria canon.
Input : a directory path.
Command :
cd /path/to/poler_toolkit
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.batch_directory \
    ./notebooklm_studio \
    --extensions .md
Python equivalent :
from pathlib import Path
from poler_toolkit.recipes_ext import recipe_batch_directory
from poler_toolkit import report
out_dir = Path("$POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/batch_notebooklm")
out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
bundle = recipe_batch_directory(
    ["./notebooklm_studio"],
    extensions=[".md"],
    recursive=False,
    top_n=10,
    out_dir=out_dir,
)
report.save_json_report(bundle["report"], out_dir, "report.json")
report.save_markdown_verdict(bundle["verdict_lines"], out_dir, "verdict.md")
Output — 5 PNG + 3 JSON/MD files:
01_file_similarity.png     9×9 TF-IDF cosine heatmap
02_pca_files.png           2-D PCA, each file labelled
03_wordcounts.png          Word counts per file (sorted desc)
04_entropy_dist.png        Histogram of Shannon entropy values
05_outliers_radar.png      Radar chart of normalised metrics for top-3 outliers
per_file_report.json       Full per-file metrics
poler_directory.md         POLER format_directory_markdown output
report.json
verdict.md
Key report.json metrics (illustrative — actual numbers depend on the directory contents):
{
  "meta": { "task": "batch_directory", "engine": "POLER v6 analyze_directory + sklearn", ... },
  "inputs": ["./notebooklm_studio"],
  "metrics": {
    "n_files": 9,
    "extensions_used": [".md"],
    "recursive": false,
    "per_file": [
      {"path": "NotebookLM_Физика эфирного боя…md",  "words": 4123, "chars": 31245,
       "lines": 178, "entropy": 9.12, "avg_word_len": 5.7,
       "top5_words": [["этерия", 89], ["сфера", 67], ["рейд", 41], ...]},
      // … 9 entries
    ],
    "similarity_matrix": [[/* 9×9 cosine */]],
    "pca_coords": [[0.12, -0.34], [0.45, 0.21], ...],  // 9 files
    "outliers": [
      {"path": "NotebookLM_Двойная слепота Экхарта…md",
       "max_similarity": 0.18,
       "reason": "max cosine to any other file = 0.18 (< 0.20 threshold)"},
      // … up to 3 outliers
    ],
    "poler_directory": { /* raw poler_v6.analyze_directory result */ }
  },
  "outputs": { ... },
  "verdict": "Analyzed 9 files. Top outliers: 'NotebookLM_Двойная слепота Экхарта…' (max_sim=0.18), ..."
}
Interpretation :
outliers is the headline result — each entry has max_similarity (the highest cosine to any other file). Below OUTLIER_THRESHOLD = 0.20 means the file has no close neighbour in the corpus.
pca_coords gives you the 2-D position of each file. Plot them yourself if you want a custom scatter.
per_file[i].entropy reveals vocabulary richness per file. A file with entropy < 8.0 is probably very repetitive (a list, a glossary, a TOC).
poler_directory wraps poler_v6.analyze_directory(...) — POLER's own cross-file clusters and per-file ε values. Cross-reference with the sklearn PCA to find files that POLER groups together but sklearn separates (often means stylistic similarity but topical difference).
> Pitfall : very short files (< 200 words) produce noisy TF-IDF vectors and dominate the outlier list. Filter them out before running.
Example 3
Track the emotional arc of a text ( analyze + theme_evolution )
> Scenario : You want to know which chapters of a novel are emotionally dense, which emotional markers dominate each chapter, and how the emotional vocabulary evolves over the narrative.
Input : one file — the Cassiopeia canon.
Step 1 — Quick overall analysis with analyze :
cd /path/to/poler_toolkit
python3 -m poler_toolkit analyze \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" \
    --keyword Міка \
    --label analyze_arc
Real verdict.md (Cassiopeia, keyword= Міка ):
# Анализ: Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt
| Метрика | Значение |
|---|---|
| Файл | $POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE |
| Слов | 107652 |
| Глав | 35 |
| Абзацев | 2835 |
| Keyword (auto) | Міка |
| POLER occurrences | 245 |
| POLER fragments | 15 |
| POLER clusters | 3 |
| sklearn neighbor cosine | 0.071 |
| sklearn frac<0.10 | 77.4% |
| sklearn silhouette | 0.006 |
| sklearn PCA expl.var | 0.014 |
| sklearn chap cosine | 0.582 |
| Entropy mean | 8.88 bits/token |
| EMOTIONAL_MARKERS | 19/60 |
## Вердикт
**КЛАСТЕРИ НЕ СКЛАДАЮТЬСЯ — текст розпадається на iзольованi шматки.**
Real metrics.poler + metrics.shannon_entropy :
{
  "poler": {
    "total_occurrences": 245,
    "unique_fragments": 182,
    "fragments_returned": 15,
    "n_clusters": 3,
    "cluster_sizes": [2, 1, 12]
  },
  "shannon_entropy": {"mean": 8.881, "std": 0.617, "min": 7.412, "max": 10.267},
  "emotional_markers_found": 19,
  "emotional_markers_total": 60
}
Step 2 — Detailed marker evolution with theme_evolution :
cd /path/to/poler_toolkit
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.theme_evolution \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE"
Real verdict.md (Cassiopeia):
# Theme Evolution: Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt
| Метрика | Значение |
|---|---|
| Файл | $POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE |
| Слов (whitespace) | 107652 |
| Токенов (regex) | 101866 |
| Глав | 35 |
| Маркеров всего | 60 |
| Активных маркеров | 19 |
| Top-N визуализации | 20 |
| Порог 'stable' (глав) | 8 |
| Самый стабильный | реальність |
|   var (stable) | 0.021884 |
|   mean / 1000 | 0.059 |
|   глав с маркером | 8/35 |
| Самый волатильный | хаос |
|   var (volatile) | 0.521958 |
|   mean / 1000 | 0.282 |
|   глав с маркером | 9/35 |
| Глава пик плотности | #33 «Глава 59: Угода з Дияволом (Частина I — План)» |
|   density | 7.82/1000 |
| Глава минимум плотности | #4 «Глава 5: Безіменне небо (Фінальна версія)» |
|   density | 0.00/1000 |
## Вердикт
**Эволюция маркеров: «реальність» — самый стабильный (var=0.0219, mean=0.06/1000, в 8/35 главах);
«хаос» — самый волатильный (var=0.5220, mean=0.28/1000, в 9/35 главах).
Пик эмоциональной плотности — глава «Глава 59: Угода з Дияволом (Частина I — План)» (7.82/1000);
минимум — глава «Глава 5: Безіменне небо (Фінальна версія)» (0.00/1000).
Активных маркеров: 19/60.**
Real per-chapter emotional density (first 10 of 35 chapters, markers/1000 tokens):
Chapter 1 (Пролог):                          2.080   ← high emotional density
Chapter 2 (Глава 3: Тріщина):                0.884
Chapter 3 (Глава 4: Рождение Обсидиана):     1.442
Chapter 4 (Глава 5: Безіменне небо):         0.000   ← no markers fire at all
Chapter 5 (Глава 6: Нитка):                  0.269
Chapter 6 (Глава 7):                         2.022   ← another peak
Chapter 7 (Глава 8):                         0.318
Chapter 8 (Глава 9):                         0.000
Chapter 9 (Глава 10):                        1.207
Chapter 10 (Глава 11):                       2.454
...
Chapter 33 (Глава 59: Угода з Дияволом):     7.820   ← global peak
Interpretation :
19 of 60 markers fire — a curated emotional vocabulary is in use, but not the full set. The 41 unused markers (e.g. свобода , надія ) indicate themes the text doesn't explore.
хаос (chaos) is the most volatile — it spikes in specific chapters (battle scenes, confrontation chapters). Mean density 0.282/1000 = roughly 1 occurrence per 3 500 tokens.
реальність (reality) is the most stable — appears at a steady rate of 0.06/1000 in 8/35 chapters. This is the thematic undercurrent.
Chapter 33 ( Глава 59: Угода з Дияволом ) has 7.82 markers/1000 — 27× the average. This is the emotional climax of the novel.
Chapters 4 and 8 have 0.00 markers/1000 — they're either purely expository or use vocabulary outside POLER's curated marker set.
> Tip : stack analyze (for the keyword-specific POLER data) with theme_evolution (for the marker timeline) to get the full emotional picture. The 02_chapter_matrix_poler.png artifact from analyze and the 02_heatmap.png artifact from theme_evolution show related information at different granularities.
Example 4
Build a custom analyzer plugin ( custom_plugin )
> Scenario : You want to compute the type-token ratio (TTR) of a file — a metric the toolkit doesn't ship. Write a 30-line Python plugin, run it through custom_plugin , get the standard JSON+MD+PNG wrapper.
Input plugin ( /tmp/ttr_plugin.py ):
"""TTR (type-token ratio) plugin for poler_toolkit."""
from pathlib import Path
from collections import Counter
DEFAULT_MIN_WORD_LEN = 2
def run(filepaths, opts, poler, toolkit_core, toolkit_viz, out_dir):
    min_len = opts.get("min_word_len", DEFAULT_MIN_WORD_LEN)
    results = {}
    total_tokens = 0
    total_unique = 0
for fp in filepaths:
        text = Path(fp).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
        tokens = [w.lower() for w in text.split() if len(w) >= min_len]
        unique = len(set(tokens))
        ttr = unique / max(len(tokens), 1)
        results[Path(fp).name] = {
            "tokens": len(tokens),
            "unique": unique,
            "ttr": round(ttr, 4),
        }
        total_tokens += len(tokens)
        total_unique += unique
overall_ttr = total_unique / max(total_tokens, 1)
    return {
        "metrics": {
            "per_file":   results,
            "total_tokens": total_tokens,
            "total_unique": total_unique,
            "overall_ttr":  round(overall_ttr, 4),
            "min_word_len": min_len,
        },
        "verdict": f"Overall TTR = {overall_ttr:.3f} ({total_unique} unique / {total_tokens} tokens)",
        "artifacts": {},
        "extra_lines": [
            "",
            "## Per-file TTR",
            "",
            "| File | Tokens | Unique | TTR |",
            "|---|---|---|---|",
            *[f"| {name} | {d['tokens']} | {d['unique']} | {d['ttr']} |"
              for name, d in results.items()],
        ],
    }
Command :
cd /path/to/poler_toolkit
python3 -m poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.custom_plugin \
    /tmp/ttr_plugin.py \
    "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" \
    "./sfera_predela.txt"
Python equivalent :
from pathlib import Path
from poler_toolkit.recipes_ext import recipe_custom_plugin
from poler_toolkit import report
out_dir = Path("$POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/ttr_plugin_run")
out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
bundle = recipe_custom_plugin(
    [
        "$POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE",
        "./sfera_predela.txt",
    ],
    plugin_path="/tmp/ttr_plugin.py",
    timeout=60,
    plugin_opts={"min_word_len": 3},
    out_dir=out_dir,
)
report.save_json_report(bundle["report"], out_dir, "report.json")
report.save_markdown_verdict(bundle["verdict_lines"], out_dir, "verdict.md")
Expected report.json (illustrative — actual TTR depends on the texts):
{
  "meta": {
    "task": "custom_plugin",
