max ( len ( per_file ), 1 ) : , } " ], ], ), [ f "## Result" , "" , f "** { verdict } **" , "" ], ), "artifacts" : {}, } def _group_by_ext ( per_file : List [ Dict ]) -> Dict [ str , Dict ]: groups : Dict [ str , Dict ] = {} for f in per_file : ext = f . get ( "extension" , "?" ) if ext not in groups : groups [ ext ] = { "count" : 0 , "total_words" : 0 , "total_chars" : 0 } groups [ ext ][ "count" ] += 1 groups [ ext ][ "total_words" ] += f . get ( "words" , 0 ) groups [ ext ][ "total_chars" ] += f . get ( "chars" , 0 ) return groups # ============================================================ # RECIPE: smart_cache # ============================================================ # Cache dir resolved via paths.py (env-aware: $POLER_TOOLKIT_CACHE / XDG) try : from ..paths import get_cache_root as _get_cache_root _CACHE_DIR = _get_cache_root () except ImportError : _CACHE_DIR = Path . home () / ".poler_cache" def recipe_smart_cache ( filepaths : List [ str | Path ], * , action : str = "get" , cache_dir : Optional [ str ] = None , keyword : Optional [ str ] = None , out_dir : Path = None , ) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """ Persistent analysis cache. store — analyze file + save to cache get   — return cached result (or analyze if miss) list  — list all cached entries clear — remove all cache entries """ cache_path = Path ( cache_dir ) if cache_dir else _CACHE_DIR cache_path . mkdir ( parents = True , exist_ok = True ) if action == "list" : entries = [] for cf in sorted ( cache_path . glob ( "*.json" )): try : data = json . loads ( cf . read_text ( encoding = "utf-8" )) entries . append ({ "cache_file" : cf . name , "source" : data . get ( "source_file" , "?" ), "cached_at" : data . get ( "cached_at" , "?" ), "words" : data . get ( "stats" , {}) . get ( "words" , 0 ), }) except Exception : pass metrics = { "action" : "list" , "entries" : entries , "count" : len ( entries )} verdict = f "Cache contains { len ( entries ) } entries." full_report = report . wrap_report ( task_name = "smart_cache" , engine = "smart_toolkit" , input_files = [], metrics = metrics , output_files = {}, verdict = verdict , ) return { "report" : full_report , "verdict_lines" : report . verdict_block ( "Smart Cache — List" , [], [ f "** { verdict } **" ], ), "artifacts" : {}, } if action == "clear" : count = 0 for cf in cache_path . glob ( "*.json" ): cf . unlink () count += 1 metrics = { "action" : "clear" , "removed" : count } verdict = f "Cleared { count } cache entries." full_report = report . wrap_report ( task_name = "smart_cache" , engine = "smart_toolkit" , input_files = [], metrics = metrics , output_files = {}, verdict = verdict , ) return { "report" : full_report , "verdict_lines" : report . verdict_block ( "Smart Cache — Clear" , [], [ f "** { verdict } **" ], ), "artifacts" : {}, } if len ( filepaths ) < 1 : raise ConfigurationError ( "smart_cache requires at least 1 file for store/get" ) filepath = str ( filepaths [ 0 ]) if not os . path . exists ( filepath ): raise FileError ( filepath , "file not found" ) file_hash = _quick_hash ( filepath ) cache_file = cache_path / f " { file_hash } .json" if action == "get" and cache_file . exists (): data = json . loads ( cache_file . read_text ( encoding = "utf-8" )) data [ "cache_hit" ] = True metrics = data verdict = f "Cache HIT for { Path ( filepath ) . name } (cached at { data . get ( 'cached_at' , '?' ) } )." full_report = report . wrap_report ( task_name = "smart_cache" , engine = "smart_toolkit" , input_files = [ filepath ], metrics = metrics , output_files = {}, verdict = verdict , ) return { "report" : full_report , "verdict_lines" : report . verdict_block ( f "Smart Cache — { Path ( filepath ) . name } " , [], [ f "** { verdict } **" ], ), "artifacts" : {}, } if action == "get" and not cache_file . exists (): action = "store" if action == "store" : text , fmt = core . read_any ( filepath ) stats = _file_stats ( text ) sections = P . detect_sections ( text ) keyword_val = keyword or core . pick_keyword ( text ) poler_result = P . analyze_text ( text , keyword_val , top_n = 5 , source_file = filepath ) cache_data = { "source_file" : filepath , "cached_at" : time . strftime ( "%Y-%m- %d T%H:%M:%S" ), "file_hash" : file_hash , "format" : fmt , "stats" : stats , "keyword" : keyword_val , "sections" : [{ "title" : s [ "title" ], "position" : s [ "position" ]} for s in sections [: 50 ]], "top_fragments" : poler_result . get ( "top_fragments" , [])[: 5 ], "total_occurrences" : poler_result . get ( "total_occurrences" , 0 ), } cache_file . write_text ( json . dumps ( cache_data , ensure_ascii = False , indent = 2 , default = str ), encoding = "utf-8" , ) cache_data [ "cache_hit" ] = False metrics = cache_data verdict = f "Cached { Path ( filepath ) . name } ( { stats [ 'words' ] : , } words, keyword=' { keyword_val } ')." full_report = report . wrap_report ( task_name = "smart_cache" , engine = "smart_toolkit" , input_files = [ filepath ], metrics = metrics , output_files = {}, verdict = verdict , ) return { "report" : full_report , "verdict_lines" : report . verdict_block ( f "Smart Cache — Store { Path ( filepath ) . name } " , report . metric_table ( [ "Parameter" , "Value" ], [ [ "File" , filepath ], [ "Hash" , file_hash ], [ "Words" , f " { stats [ 'words' ] : , } " ], [ "Keyword" , keyword_val ], [ "Cache" , str ( cache_file )], ], ), [ f "** { verdict } **" ], ), "artifacts" : {}, } raise ConfigurationError ( f "Unknown action ' { action } '. Use: store, get, list, clear" ) # ============================================================ # RECIPE: smart_diff # ============================================================ def recipe_smart_diff ( filepaths : List [ str | Path ], * , keyword : Optional [ str ] = None , cache_dir : Optional [ str ] = None , out_dir : Path = None , ) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """ Fast differential: compare two files OR file vs cached version. Returns ONLY the delta — no full file dumps. """ cache_path = Path ( cache_dir ) if cache_dir else _CACHE_DIR if len ( filepaths ) == 1 : filepath = str ( filepaths [ 0 ]) file_hash = _quick_hash ( filepath ) cache_file = cache_path / f " { file_hash } .json" if not cache_file . exists (): raise RecipeError ( f "no cache entry for { Path ( filepath ) . name } " , hint = "Run 'smart_cache store' first, or pass 2 files." , ) cached = json . loads ( cache_file . read_text ( encoding = "utf-8" )) text_a = json . dumps ( cached . get ( "stats" , {})) label_a = f "cached( { Path ( filepath ) . name } )" text_b = cached . get ( "stats" , {}) . get ( "words" , 0 ) source_file = filepath elif len ( filepaths ) == 2 : fp_a , fp_b = str ( filepaths [ 0 ]), str ( filepaths [ 1 ]) text_a , _ = core . read_any ( fp_a ) text_b , _ = core . read_any ( fp_b ) label_a = Path ( fp_a ) . name source_file = f " { fp_a } vs { fp_b } " else : raise ConfigurationError ( "smart_diff expects 1 or 2 files" ) if len ( filepaths ) == 2 : stats_a = _file_stats ( text_a ) stats_b = _file_stats ( text_b ) words_a = set ( w . lower () for w in text_a . split () if len ( w ) > 2 ) words_b = set ( w . lower () for w in text_b . split () if len ( w ) > 2 ) common = words_a & words_b only_a = words_a - words_b only_b = words_b - words_a keyword_val = keyword or core . pick_keyword ( text_a + " " + text_b ) r1 = P . analyze_text ( text_a , keyword_val , top_n = 3 , source_file = fp_a ) r2 = P . analyze_text ( text_b , keyword_val , top_n = 3 , source_file = fp_b ) metrics = { "file_a" : fp_a , "file_b" : fp_b , "stats_a" : stats_a , "stats_b" : stats_b , "delta" : { "words" : stats_b [ "words" ] - stats_a [ "words" ], "chars" : stats_b [ "chars" ] - stats_a [ "chars" ], "lines" : stats_b [ "lines" ] - stats_a [ "lines" ], "entropy" : round ( stats_b [ "entropy" ] - stats_a [ "entropy" ], 3 ), }, "vocabulary" : { "common_words" : len ( common ), "only_in_a" : len ( only_a ), "only_in_b" : len ( only_b ), "top_only_a" : sorted ( list ( only_a ))[: 20 ], "top_only_b" : sorted ( list ( only_b ))[: 20 ], }, "keyword" : keyword_val , "occurrences_a" : r1 . get ( "total_occurrences" , 0 ), "occurrences_b" : r2 . get ( "total_occurrences" , 0 ), } verdict = ( f "Diff: { label_a } vs { Path ( fp_b ) . name } : " f "Δ words= { metrics [ 'delta' ][ 'words' ] : +d } , " f "Δ entropy= { metrics [ 'delta' ][ 'entropy' ] : +.3f } , " f " { len ( only_a ) } words only in A, { len ( only_b ) } only in B." ) else : stats_a = cached . get ( "stats" , {}) metrics = { "file" : source_file , "cached_stats" : stats_a , "keyword" : cached . get ( "keyword" , "?" ), "cache_hit" : True , } verdict = f "Diff vs cache for { Path ( source_file ) . name } : stats loaded from cache." full_report = report . wrap_report ( task_name = "smart_diff" , engine = "smart_toolkit" , input_files = [ source_file ] if len ( filepaths ) == 1 else [ fp_a , fp_b ], metrics = metrics , output_files = {}, verdict = verdict , ) return { "report" : full_report , "verdict_lines" : report . verdict_block ( f "Smart Diff: { label_a } " , [ f "## Result" , "" , f "** { verdict } **" , "" ], ), "artifacts" : {}, } __all__ = [ "recipe_smart_read" , "recipe_smart_search" , "recipe_smart_batch" , "recipe_smart_cache" , "recipe_smart_diff" , ]
theme_evolution.py
Файл: poler_toolkit/recipes_ext/theme_evolution.py · Размер: 25 402 байт
""" poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.theme_evolution ========================================= Recipe: track how ``EMOTIONAL_MARKERS`` from POLER v6 evolve across chapters of a narrative text. What the recipe does -------------------- 1. Read the file via ``core.read_any`` (supports .txt / .md / .epub). 2. Split the text into chapters via ``core.split_chapters``. 3. For each chapter, count occurrences of every marker in ``poler_v6.EMOTIONAL_MARKERS`` (a set of 60 multi-lingual terms: RU / UK / EN). 4. Normalize counts by chapter length (markers per 1000 tokens) so that long and short chapters are directly comparable. 5. Rank markers by total raw frequency and pick top-N (default 20) for the visualizations. 6. Render three PNG artifacts: - **Stacked area chart** — top-N marker *proportions* per chapter. - **Heatmap** — chapters (rows) × top-N markers (cols), normalized density on a ``magma`` colour scale. - **Line chart** — top-5 markers' evolution across chapters. 7. Emit a JSON ``report`` with the full chapters × markers matrix, the per-chapter dominant marker, and variance statistics. 8. Emit a Markdown ``verdict`` identifying: - Most stable marker (lowest variance of normalized density). - Most volatile marker (highest variance of normalized density). - Chapter with the highest emotional density. - Chapter with the lowest emotional density. The function signature matches the Stage 2 contract so the main agent can register it in ``recipes.RECIPES`` directly: "theme_evolution": { "fn": recipe_theme_evolution, "n_files": 1, "description": "Evolution of EMOTIONAL_MARKERS across chapters", "options": ["chapter_pattern", "lang", "top_n"], } """ from __future__ import annotations import re import logging from pathlib import Path from typing import Any , Dict , List , Optional from collections import Counter import numpy as np import matplotlib matplotlib . use ( "Agg" ) import matplotlib.pyplot as plt # Recipe lives in poler_toolkit.recipes_ext, so parent-of-parent is # poler_toolkit itself. Primary path: relative imports (matches Stage 2 # integration contract). Fallback: absolute imports, so the file can also # be invoked directly as `python3 .../theme_evolution.py FILE` for testing. try : from .. import core from .. import report from .. import viz from ..errors import RecipeError , ConfigurationError except ImportError : # pragma: no cover - script-mode fallback import os as _os import sys as _sys _here = _os . path . dirname ( _os . path . abspath ( __file__ )) _scripts = _os . path . dirname ( _os . path . dirname ( _here )) if _scripts not in _sys . path : _sys . path . insert ( 0 , _scripts ) from poler_toolkit import core # type: ignore from poler_toolkit import report # type: ignore from poler_toolkit import viz # type: ignore from poler_toolkit.errors import RecipeError , ConfigurationError # type: ignore log = logging . getLogger ( "poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.theme_evolution" ) # ============================================================ # Local visualization helpers # ------------------------------------------------------------ # Stage 2 constraint: do NOT modify viz.py. The existing viz.* functions # cover heatmaps for cosine similarity (square matrix) and stacked BAR # charts, but the task explicitly asks for stacked AREA + a non-square # chapter×marker heatmap + a multi-series line chart. We implement those # three locally so viz.py stays untouched for the main agent. # ============================================================ def _short_label ( title : str ) -> str : """Collapse 'Глава 12 (Робоча назва): Уламок' -> '12'.""" m = re . search ( r "\d+" , title ) return m . group ( 0 ) if m else title [: 10 ] def _safe_savefig ( fig , path : Path ) -> None : """Save + close. Honours toolkit DPI.""" try : fig . savefig ( path , dpi = viz . DPI ) log . info ( f "Saved: { path } " ) except Exception as e : raise viz . VisualizationError ( f "savefig failed: { e } " ) from e finally : plt . close ( fig ) def _stacked_area ( matrix : np . ndarray , # shape (chapters, n_markers) markers : List [ str ], chapter_titles : List [ str ], out_path : Path , title : str = "Marker proportions per chapter" , ) -> None : """ Stacked area chart of top-N marker *proportions* per chapter. Each chapter's row is normalized so that the visible top-N markers sum to 1.0 (residual 'other' mass, if any, is dropped — it would always be zero here because we sum over the same top-N subset). """ n_chap , n_m = matrix . shape row_sums = matrix . sum ( axis = 1 , keepdims = True ) row_sums [ row_sums == 0 ] = 1.0 prop = matrix / row_sums fig , ax = plt . subplots ( figsize = ( 13 , 6 ), constrained_layout = True ) x = np . arange ( n_chap ) colors = plt . cm . tab20 ( np . linspace ( 0 , 1 , max ( n_m , 1 ))) stack = np . zeros ( n_chap ) for k in range ( n_m ): ax . fill_between ( x , stack , stack + prop [:, k ], color = colors [ k ], alpha = 0.85 , linewidth = 0.4 , label = markers [ k ], ) stack = stack + prop [:, k ] labels = [ _short_label ( t ) for t in chapter_titles ] ax . set_xticks ( x ) ax . set_xticklabels ( labels , rotation = 90 , fontsize = max ( 5 , 8 - n_chap // 30 ), ) ax . set_xlabel ( "Глава" ) ax . set_ylabel ( "Доля маркера (в top-N)" ) ax . set_ylim ( 0 , 1.0 ) ax . set_title ( title , fontsize = 13 , pad = 10 ) ax . legend ( loc = "center left" , bbox_to_anchor = ( 1.01 , 0.5 ), fontsize = 7 , ncol = 1 , framealpha = 0.9 , ) ax . grid ( alpha = 0.25 , axis = "y" ) _safe_savefig ( fig , out_path ) def _marker_heatmap ( matrix : np . ndarray , # shape (chapters, n_markers) markers : List [ str ], chapter_titles : List [ str ], out_path : Path , title : str = "Marker density heatmap" , cmap : str = "magma" , ) -> None : """ Heatmap chapters × markers, normalized density (markers / 1000 tokens). Rows are markers (sorted by total frequency desc), columns are chapters in textual order. Cell colour encodes density. """ n_chap , n_m = matrix . shape fig_w = max ( 10 , n_chap * 0.32 ) fig_h = max ( 6 , n_m * 0.40 + 2 ) fig , ax = plt . subplots ( figsize = ( fig_w , fig_h ), constrained_layout = True , ) display = matrix . T # rows=markers, cols=chapters vmax = float ( display . max ()) if display . size and display . max () > 0 else 1.0 im = ax . imshow ( display , aspect = "auto" , cmap = cmap , vmin = 0 , vmax = vmax , ) ax . set_xticks ( range ( n_chap )) ax . set_yticks ( range ( n_m )) chap_labels = [ _short_label ( t ) for t in chapter_titles ] ax . set_xticklabels ( chap_labels , rotation = 90 , fontsize = max ( 5 , 8 - n_chap // 30 ), ) ax . set_yticklabels ( markers , fontsize = max ( 7 , 10 - n_m // 15 )) ax . set_xlabel ( "Глава" ) ax . set_ylabel ( "Маркер" ) ax . set_title ( title , fontsize = 13 , pad = 10 ) fig . colorbar ( im , ax = ax , fraction = 0.025 , pad = 0.02 , label = "маркеров / 1000 токенов" , ) _safe_savefig ( fig , out_path ) def _top5_line_chart ( matrix : np . ndarray , # shape (chapters, k)  (k <= 5) markers : List [ str ], chapter_titles : List [ str ], out_path : Path , title : str = "Top-5 markers evolution" , ) -> None : """Line chart of top-5 markers' normalized density across chapters.""" n_chap , k = matrix . shape fig , ax = plt . subplots ( figsize = ( 13 , 6 ), constrained_layout = True ) x = np . arange ( n_chap ) colors = plt . cm . tab10 ( np . linspace ( 0 , 1 , max ( k , 1 ))) for j in range ( k ): ax . plot ( x , matrix [:, j ], marker = "o" , markersize = 3.5 , linewidth = 1.7 , color = colors [ j ], label = markers [ j ], alpha = 0.9 , ) labels = [ _short_label ( t ) for t in chapter_titles ] ax . set_xticks ( x ) ax . set_xticklabels ( labels , rotation = 90 , fontsize = max ( 5 , 8 - n_chap // 30 ), ) ax . set_xlabel ( "Глава" ) ax . set_ylabel ( "Маркеров на 1000 токенов" ) ax . set_title ( title , fontsize = 13 , pad = 10 ) ax . legend ( loc = "best" , fontsize = 9 , ncol = min ( k , 2 ), framealpha = 0.9 ) ax . grid ( alpha = 0.3 ) _safe_savefig ( fig , out_path ) # ============================================================ # Recipe # ============================================================ def recipe_theme_evolution ( filepaths : list , * , chapter_pattern : Optional [ str ] = None , lang : str = "auto" , top_n : int = 20 , out_dir : Path = None , ) -> dict : """ Track how ``poler_v6.EMOTIONAL_MARKERS`` evolve across chapters. Args: filepaths: length-1 list with the input file path (.txt / .md / .epub supported via ``core.read_any``). chapter_pattern: optional regex for chapter headings. If ``None``, ``core.split_chapters`` auto-detects. lang: chapter-pattern language hint (``auto`` / ``ru`` / ``ua`` / ``en`` / ``inline_ru`` / ``inline_en``). top_n: how many top markers to keep for the heatmap + stacked area chart (default 20). Line chart always shows the top 5. out_dir: directory for PNG artifacts. If ``None``, no artifacts are written (useful when stacking recipes). Returns: ``{"report": dict, "verdict_lines": list, "artifacts": dict}``. The ``artifacts`` mapping contains at most three entries: ``stacked_area``, ``heatmap``, ``line_chart``. """ # ----- 0. validate inputs ------------------------------------------- if len ( filepaths ) != 1 : raise ConfigurationError ( "theme_evolution expects exactly 1 file" , hint = "Pass a single .txt / .md / .epub path." , ) filepath = Path ( filepaths [ 0 ]) log . info ( f "[theme_evolution] file= { filepath } " ) # ----- 1. read + split chapters ------------------------------------- text , fmt = core . read_any ( filepath ) log . info ( f "  read: { len ( text ) } chars, format= { fmt } " ) chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , pattern = chapter_pattern , lang = lang ) n_chap = len ( chapters ) log . info ( f "  chapters: { n_chap } " ) if n_chap < 1 : raise RecipeError ( "no chapters detected" , hint = "Try --lang inline_ru or pass an explicit chapter_pattern." , ) # ----- 2. load EMOTIONAL_MARKERS (sorted for stable column order) -- poler = core . get_poler () markers = sorted ( poler . EMOTIONAL_MARKERS ) n_markers = len ( markers ) log . info ( f "  markers: { n_markers } " ) # ----- 3. per-chapter counts + token counts ------------------------- # NB: we deliberately use a local regex tokenizer here (matches what # core.auto_detect_keyword and recipes.recipe_analyze do) instead of # 60×N calls to core.safe_call('grep_search_file', ...) — the latter # would re-scan the file 60 times per chapter and is needlessly slow. # The pattern is word-aware (Cyrillic-safe via re.UNICODE default). token_re = re . compile ( r "[\w’']+" ) raw_counts = np . zeros (( n_chap , n_markers ), dtype = np . int64 ) token_counts = np . zeros ( n_chap , dtype = np . int64 ) for ci , ch in enumerate ( chapters ): body_lower = ch [ "body" ] . lower () tokens = token_re . findall ( body_lower ) token_counts [ ci ] = len ( tokens ) tc = Counter ( tokens ) for mi , m in enumerate ( markers ): raw_counts [ ci , mi ] = tc . get ( m , 0 ) log . info ( f "  total markers found: { int ( raw_counts . sum ()) } " f "across { int (( raw_counts . sum ( axis = 0 ) > 0 ) . sum ()) } / { n_markers } active markers" ) # ----- 4. normalize: markers per 1000 tokens ------------------------ norm_matrix = np . zeros (( n_chap , n_markers ), dtype = np . float64 ) for ci in range ( n_chap ): if token_counts [ ci ] > 0 : norm_matrix [ ci ] = raw_counts [ ci ] / token_counts [ ci ] * 1000.0 # ----- 5. rank markers by total raw frequency ----------------------- total_per_marker = raw_counts . sum ( axis = 0 ) top_n_actual = max ( 1 , min ( top_n , n_markers )) # indices sorted by total count descending top_idx_list = list ( np . argsort ( - total_per_marker )[: top_n_actual ]) top_markers = [ markers [ i ] for i in top_idx_list ] top_norm = norm_matrix [:, top_idx_list ] log . info ( f "  top- { top_n_actual } markers: { top_markers [: 5 ] } ..." ) # Top-5 for the line chart top5_idx = top_idx_list [: 5 ] top5_markers = [ markers [ i ] for i in top5_idx ] top5_norm = norm_matrix [:, top5_idx ] # ----- 6. per-chapter dominant marker ------------------------------- dominant_per_chapter : List [ Dict [ str , Any ]] = [] for ci in range ( n_chap ): row = norm_matrix [ ci ] if row . sum () == 0 : dominant_per_chapter . append ({ "chapter_index" : ci , "title" : chapters [ ci ][ "title" ], "marker" : None , "density_per_1k" : 0.0 , "unique_markers" : 0 , }) else : mi = int ( np . argmax ( row )) dominant_per_chapter . append ({ "chapter_index" : ci , "title" : chapters [ ci ][ "title" ], "marker" : markers [ mi ], "density_per_1k" : float ( row [ mi ]), "unique_markers" : int (( raw_counts [ ci ] > 0 ) . sum ()), }) # ----- 7. variance per marker (on normalized values) ---------------- # Stable = low variance of per-1000-tokens density across chapters. # Volatile = high variance. # # Two filters apply: #   - For 'most stable' we additionally require the marker to appear #     in >= min(max(2, n_chap // 4), n_chap) chapters. Without this #     filter, a marker that appears exactly once in one long chapter #     (giving a tiny density value) would have variance ≈ 0 and #     win 'most stable' — semantically wrong. We want 'stable' to #     mean 'consistently present at a similar rate'. #   - For 'most volatile' we only require appearance in >= 1 chapter #     (singletons can still spike). marker_variance = np . zeros ( n_markers , dtype = np . float64 ) chapters_appearing = np . zeros ( n_markers , dtype = np . int64 ) for mi in range ( n_markers ): if total_per_marker [ mi ] > 0 : marker_variance [ mi ] = float ( np . var ( norm_matrix [:, mi ])) chapters_appearing [ mi ] = int (( raw_counts [:, mi ] > 0 ) . sum ()) appears_mask = total_per_marker > 0 n_appearing = int ( appears_mask . sum ()) # threshold for "stable": appear in at least 25% of chapters (>= 2) stable_min_chapters = max ( 2 , min ( n_chap , n_chap // 4 )) stable_mask = appears_mask & ( chapters_appearing >= stable_min_chapters ) if stable_mask . any (): stable_idx_pool = np . where ( stable_mask )[ 0 ] var_stable = marker_variance [ stable_idx_pool ] most_stable_idx = int ( stable_idx_pool [ int ( np . argmin ( var_stable ))]) elif n_appearing > 0 : # fall back to all appearing markers if none is "consistent" appearing_idx = np . where ( appears_mask )[ 0 ] var_among = marker_variance [ appearing_idx ] most_stable_idx = int ( appearing_idx [ int ( np . argmin ( var_among ))]) else : most_stable_idx = 0 if n_appearing > 0 : appearing_idx = np . where ( appears_mask )[ 0 ] var_among = marker_variance [ appearing_idx ] most_volatile_idx = int ( appearing_idx [ int ( np . argmax ( var_among ))]) else : most_volatile_idx = 0 # ----- 8. emotional density per chapter ----------------------------- density_per_chapter = norm_matrix . sum ( axis = 1 ) if n_chap > 0 and density_per_chapter . sum () > 0 : hi_ch = int ( np . argmax ( density_per_chapter )) lo_ch = int ( np . argmin ( density_per_chapter )) else : hi_ch = 0 lo_ch = 0 # ----- 9. render artifacts ------------------------------------------ artifacts : Dict [ str , Path ] = {} if out_dir is not None : out_dir = Path ( out_dir ) out_dir . mkdir ( parents = True , exist_ok = True ) chapter_titles = [ c [ "title" ] for c in chapters ] artifacts [ "stacked_area" ] = out_dir / "01_stacked_area.png" _stacked_area ( top_norm , top_markers , chapter_titles , artifacts [ "stacked_area" ], title = f " { filepath . stem } — эволюция маркеров (stacked area)" , ) artifacts [ "heatmap" ] = out_dir / "02_heatmap.png" _marker_heatmap ( top_norm , top_markers , chapter_titles , artifacts [ "heatmap" ], title = f " { filepath . stem } — плотность маркеров (на 1000 токенов)" , ) artifacts [ "line_chart" ] = out_dir / "03_line_top5.png" _top5_line_chart ( top5_norm , top5_markers , chapter_titles , artifacts [ "line_chart" ], title = f " { filepath . stem } — топ-5 маркеров (на 1000 токенов)" , ) # ----- 10. build metrics dict for JSON ------------------------------ stable = { "marker" : markers [ most_stable_idx ], "variance" : float ( marker_variance [ most_stable_idx ]), "mean_density_per_1k" : float ( norm_matrix [:, most_stable_idx ] . mean ()), "total_count" : int ( total_per_marker [ most_stable_idx ]), "chapters_appearing" : int ( chapters_appearing [ most_stable_idx ]), } volatile = { "marker" : markers [ most_volatile_idx ], "variance" : float ( marker_variance [ most_volatile_idx ]), "mean_density_per_1k" : float ( norm_matrix [:, most_volatile_idx ] . mean ()), "total_count" : int ( total_per_marker [ most_volatile_idx ]), "chapters_appearing" : int ( chapters_appearing [ most_volatile_idx ]), } hi = { "chapter_index" : hi_ch , "title" : chapters [ hi_ch ][ "title" ], "density_per_1k" : float ( density_per_chapter [ hi_ch ]), "tokens" : int ( token_counts [ hi_ch ]), } lo = { "chapter_index" : lo_ch , "title" : chapters [ lo_ch ][ "title" ], "density_per_1k" : float ( density_per_chapter [ lo_ch ]), "tokens" : int ( token_counts [ lo_ch ]), } metrics : Dict [ str , Any ] = { # input summary "file" : str ( filepath ), "format" : fmt , "total_chars" : int ( len ( text )), "total_words" : int ( len ( text . split ())), "total_tokens_regex" : int ( token_counts . sum ()), "total_chapters" : int ( n_chap ), # markers summary "markers_total" : int ( n_markers ), "markers_appearing" : int ( n_appearing ), "top_n" : int ( top_n_actual ), "top_markers" : top_markers , "top5_markers" : top5_markers , # per-chapter info "chapter_titles" : [ c [ "title" ] for c in chapters ], "chapter_token_counts" : token_counts . tolist (), "chapter_emotional_density_per_1k" : density_per_chapter . tolist (), "dominant_marker_per_chapter" : dominant_per_chapter , # full matrix (chapters × all markers) "matrix_markers" : markers , "matrix_norm_per_1k" : norm_matrix . tolist (), "matrix_raw_counts" : raw_counts . tolist (), # top-N matrix (subset, for the 2 charts) "top_matrix_markers" : top_markers , "top_matrix_norm_per_1k" : top_norm . tolist (), # variance stats "marker_variance" : marker_variance . tolist (), "marker_chapters_appearing" : chapters_appearing . tolist (), "stable_min_chapters_threshold" : int ( stable_min_chapters ), "most_stable_marker" : stable , "most_volatile_marker" : volatile , "highest_density_chapter" : hi , "lowest_density_chapter" : lo , } # ----- 11. Markdown verdict ----------------------------------------- verdict_text = ( f "Эволюция маркеров: « { stable [ 'marker' ] } » — самый стабильный " f "(var= { stable [ 'variance' ] : .4f } , mean= { stable [ 'mean_density_per_1k' ] : .2f } /1000, " f "в { stable [ 'chapters_appearing' ] } / { n_chap } главах); " f "« { volatile [ 'marker' ] } » — самый волатильный " f "(var= { volatile [ 'variance' ] : .4f } , mean= { volatile [ 'mean_density_per_1k' ] : .2f } /1000, " f "в { volatile [ 'chapters_appearing' ] } / { n_chap } главах). " f "Пик эмоциональной плотности — глава « { hi [ 'title' ] } » " f "( { hi [ 'density_per_1k' ] : .2f } /1000); " f "минимум — глава « { lo [ 'title' ] } » ( { lo [ 'density_per_1k' ] : .2f } /1000). " f "Активных маркеров: { n_appearing } / { n_markers } ." ) verdict_lines = report . verdict_block ( f "Theme Evolution: { filepath . name } " , report . metric_table ( [ "Метрика" , "Значение" ], [ [ "Файл" , str ( filepath )], [ "Слов (whitespace)" , str ( metrics [ "total_words" ])], [ "Токенов (regex)" , str ( metrics [ "total_tokens_regex" ])], [ "Глав" , str ( metrics [ "total_chapters" ])], [ "Маркеров всего" , str ( metrics [ "markers_total" ])], [ "Активных маркеров" , str ( metrics [ "markers_appearing" ])], [ "Top-N визуализации" , str ( metrics [ "top_n" ])], [ "Порог 'stable' (глав)" , str ( stable_min_chapters )], [ "Самый стабильный" , stable [ "marker" ]], [ "  var (stable)" , f " { stable [ 'variance' ] : .6f } " ], [ "  mean / 1000" , f " { stable [ 'mean_density_per_1k' ] : .3f } " ], [ "  глав с маркером" , f " { stable [ 'chapters_appearing' ] } / { n_chap } " ], [ "Самый волатильный" , volatile [ "marker" ]], [ "  var (volatile)" , f " { volatile [ 'variance' ] : .6f } " ], [ "  mean / 1000" , f " { volatile [ 'mean_density_per_1k' ] : .3f } " ], [ "  глав с маркером" , f " { volatile [ 'chapters_appearing' ] } / { n_chap } " ], [ "Глава пик плотности" , f "# { hi [ 'chapter_index' ] + 1 } « { hi [ 'title' ] } »" ], [ "  density" , f " { hi [ 'density_per_1k' ] : .2f } /1000" ], [ "  tokens" , str ( hi [ "tokens" ])], [ "Глава минимум плотности" , f "# { lo [ 'chapter_index' ] + 1 } « { lo [ 'title' ] } »" ], [ "  density" , f " { lo [ 'density_per_1k' ] : .2f } /1000" ], [ "  tokens" , str ( lo [ "tokens" ])], ], ), [ "## Вердикт" , "" , f "** { verdict_text } **" , "" ], ) # ----- 12. wrap into standard report bundle ------------------------- full_report = report . wrap_report ( task_name = "theme_evolution" , engine = "POLER v6 EMOTIONAL_MARKERS" , input_files = [ str ( filepath )], metrics = metrics , output_files = { k : str ( v ) for k , v in artifacts . items ()}, verdict = verdict_text , ) return { "report" : full_report , "verdict_lines" : verdict_lines , "artifacts" : artifacts , } __all__ = [ "recipe_theme_evolution" ] # ============================================================ # Standalone test entry-point # ------------------------------------------------------------ # Run:  python3 poler_toolkit/recipes_ext/theme_evolution.py [FILE] # If no FILE is given, defaults to env $POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE or # the Cassiopeia canon sample (if available). # Output: env $POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/test_theme_evolution_<ts>/ # ============================================================ if __name__ == "__main__" : import sys import os import json from datetime import datetime logging . basicConfig ( level = logging . INFO , format = " %(asctime)s [ %(name)s ] %(levelname)s : %(message)s " , ) default_file = os . environ . get ( "POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" , "" ) if not default_file or not Path ( default_file ) . exists (): # Fallback: try common sample locations for cand in [ "upload/Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt" , "upload/Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt" , ]: if Path ( cand ) . exists (): default_file = cand break test_file = sys . argv [ 1 ] if len ( sys . argv ) > 1 else default_file # Output dir via paths.py (env-aware) from .. import paths as _paths output_root = _paths . get_output_root () ts = datetime . now () . strftime ( "%Y%m %d _%H%M%S" ) out_dir = output_root / f "test_theme_evolution_ { ts } " out_dir . mkdir ( parents = True , exist_ok = True ) print ( f "Running theme_evolution on: { test_file } " ) print ( f "Output dir: { out_dir } " ) bundle = recipe_theme_evolution ( [ test_file ], chapter_pattern = None , lang = "auto" , top_n = 20 , out_dir = out_dir , ) # Write report.json + verdict.md (mirrors what runner.run() does) ( out_dir / "report.json" ) . write_text ( json . dumps ( bundle [ "report" ], ensure_ascii = False , indent = 2 , default = str , ), encoding = "utf-8" , ) ( out_dir / "verdict.md" ) . write_text ( " \n " . join ( bundle [ "verdict_lines" ]), encoding = "utf-8" , ) print ( " \n === DONE ===" ) print ( f "Artifacts ( { len ( bundle [ 'artifacts' ]) } ):" ) for name , p in bundle [ "artifacts" ] . items (): print ( f " { name : 14s } -> { p } (exists= { p . exists () } )" ) print ( f " \n report.json: { out_dir / 'report.json' } " ) print ( f "verdict.md: { out_dir / 'verdict.md' } " ) print ( f " \n Verdict: { bundle [ 'report' ][ 'verdict' ] } " )
themes_alpha.py
Файл: poler_toolkit/recipes_ext/themes_alpha.py · Размер: 42 308 байт
""" poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.themes_alpha ====================================== Recipe: build an **alphabetical inverted index** of every word in a text, with source tracking (chapter / line / char-offset) and aggressive size optimization so a 100 K-word novel produces < 5 MB of output instead of the 10+ MB a naive full index would consume. What the recipe does -------------------- 1. Read the file via ``core.read_any`` (supports .txt / .md / .epub). 2. Split the text into chapters via ``core.split_chapters`` so we can map any character offset to a 1-indexed chapter number (0 = pre-chapter). 3. In a single regex pass over the full text, capture every word and its absolute character offset; for each occurrence compute ``(chapter_num, line_num, char_offset)`` via ``bisect`` on pre-computed line/chapter starts (O(log N) per token). 4. Group occurrences by the lower-cased word form. Apply three size-bounding strategies: * **Pagination** — split the index alphabetically into one JSON file per starting letter (``index/A.json`` … ``index/Z.json`` for Latin, ``index/А.json`` … ``index/Я.json`` for Cyrillic, etc.) plus ``index/_other.json`` for words that start with a digit / underscore / other non-letter. Each file is capped at ``MAX_FILE_BYTES`` (500 KB) by further sharding if needed (``A.json``, ``A_2.json`` …). * **Top-N per word** — for ordinary words keep at most ``max_locations`` (default 20) source locations. * **Sampling common words** — words with more than ``SAMPLING_THRESHOLD`` (100) occurrences are stored as a chapter-level summary + the first ``SAMPLE_FIRST_N`` (5) exact locations only. 5. Emit: * ``summary.json``       — totals, top-N words, alphabet distribution. * ``schema.json``        — describes the on-disk layout. * ``letter_index.json``  — per-letter totals + top-10 most frequent. * ``index/<LETTER>.json`` — the paginated inverted index. * 4 PNG artifacts (alphabet histogram, word-length histogram, top-100 words, Zipf curve). * Standard ``report.json`` + ``verdict.md`` via the report bundle. The function signature matches the Stage 2 contract so the main agent can register it in ``recipes.RECIPES`` directly: "themes_alpha": { "fn": recipe_themes_alpha, "n_files": 1, "description": "Alphabetical inverted word index with source tracking + size optimization", "options": ["chapter_pattern", "lang", "max_locations", "top_n"], } """ from __future__ import annotations import bisect import json import logging import re from pathlib import Path from typing import Any , Dict , List , Optional , Tuple from collections import Counter , defaultdict import numpy as np import matplotlib matplotlib . use ( "Agg" ) import matplotlib.pyplot as plt # Recipe lives in poler_toolkit.recipes_ext, so parent-of-parent is # poler_toolkit itself. Primary path: relative imports (matches Stage 2 # integration contract). Fallback: absolute imports, so the file can also # be invoked directly as `python3 .../themes_alpha.py FILE` for testing. try : from .. import core from .. import report from .. import viz from ..errors import RecipeError , ConfigurationError except ImportError : # pragma: no cover - script-mode fallback import os as _os import sys as _sys _here = _os . path . dirname ( _os . path . abspath ( __file__ )) _scripts = _os . path . dirname ( _os . path . dirname ( _here )) if _scripts not in _sys . path : _sys . path . insert ( 0 , _scripts ) from poler_toolkit import core # type: ignore from poler_toolkit import report # type: ignore from poler_toolkit import viz # type: ignore from poler_toolkit.errors import RecipeError , ConfigurationError # type: ignore log = logging . getLogger ( "poler_toolkit.recipes_ext.themes_alpha" ) # ============================================================ # Tunable size-budget constants # ------------------------------------------------------------ # These are *module-level* constants (not function arguments) because they # are structural limits of the format, not user-tunable knobs. The # user-tunable knobs (max_locations, top_n) are function arguments. # ============================================================ SAMPLING_THRESHOLD = 100 """Words with > this many occurrences get the chapter-summary + first-5 exact-locations treatment instead of the full ``max_locations`` list.""" SAMPLE_FIRST_N = 5 """For sampled (common) words, how many exact ``[chapter, line, offset]`` locations to keep verbatim.""" MAX_FILE_BYTES = 500 * 1024 # 500 KB """Hard cap on a single ``index/<LETTER>.json`` file. If a letter's bucket would exceed this, it is sharded into ``<LETTER>.json``, ``<LETTER>_2.json``, ``<LETTER>_3.json`` … — each shard carries a contiguous slice of the alphabetically-sorted word list.""" # Tokenizer — same family as core.auto_detect_keyword and # recipes_ext.theme_evolution. ``\w`` is Unicode-aware in Python 3 so it # matches Cyrillic letters natively. The apostrophe inclusion lets us keep # Ukrainian words like ``ібн'я`` and English contractions like ``don't`` as # single tokens. _TOKEN_RE = re . compile ( r "[\w’']+" ) # ============================================================ # Local visualization helpers # ------------------------------------------------------------ # Stage 2 constraint: do NOT modify viz.py. The existing viz.* functions # cover heatmaps, stacked bars, scatter plots and dashboards, but not the # four specific plots this recipe needs (alphabet histogram, word-length # histogram, top-N horizontal bar, Zipf log-log curve). We implement them # locally so viz.py stays untouched for the main agent. # ============================================================ def _safe_savefig ( fig , path : Path ) -> None : """Save + close. Honours toolkit DPI.""" try : fig . savefig ( path , dpi = viz . DPI ) log . info ( f "Saved: { path } " ) except Exception as e : raise viz . VisualizationError ( f "savefig failed: { e } " ) from e finally : plt . close ( fig ) def _alphabet_histogram ( letter_counts : List [ Tuple [ str , int ]], out_path : Path , title : str = "Alphabet distribution" , ) -> None : """Bar chart of token occurrences per starting letter.""" if not letter_counts : letter_counts = [( "(none)" , 0 )] letters = [ lc [ 0 ] for lc in letter_counts ] counts = [ lc [ 1 ] for lc in letter_counts ] n = len ( letters ) fig_w = max ( 8 , min ( 28 , n * 0.55 + 2 )) fig , ax = plt . subplots ( figsize = ( fig_w , 6 ), constrained_layout = True ) xs = np . arange ( n ) colors = plt . cm . viridis ( np . linspace ( 0.1 , 0.9 , n )) if n > 1 else [ viz . COLOR_PRIMARY ] ax . bar ( xs , counts , color = colors , alpha = 0.9 , edgecolor = "black" , linewidth = 0.3 ) ax . set_xticks ( xs ) ax . set_xticklabels ( letters , fontsize = max ( 6 , 11 - n // 20 )) ax . set_xlabel ( "Первая буква" ) ax . set_ylabel ( "Количество слов (с повторами)" ) ax . set_title ( title , fontsize = 13 , pad = 10 ) ax . grid ( alpha = 0.25 , axis = "y" ) if counts : ymax = max ( counts ) ax . set_ylim ( 0 , ymax * 1.12 + 1 ) _safe_savefig ( fig , out_path ) def _wordlength_histogram ( lengths : np . ndarray , out_path : Path , title : str = "Word-length histogram" , ) -> None : """Histogram of word lengths (in characters).""" fig , ax = plt . subplots ( figsize = ( 11 , 5 ), constrained_layout = True ) if lengths . size == 0 : ax . text ( 0.5 , 0.5 , "no tokens" , ha = "center" , va = "center" ) else : max_len = int ( lengths . max ()) bins = np . arange ( 1 , max_len + 2 ) - 0.5 # bin edges centered on integers ax . hist ( lengths , bins = bins , color = viz . COLOR_SECONDARY , alpha = 0.85 , edgecolor = "black" , linewidth = 0.4 ) mean_len = float ( lengths . mean ()) ax . axvline ( mean_len , color = viz . COLOR_ACCENT , linestyle = "--" , linewidth = 1.5 , label = f "среднее= { mean_len : .2f } " ) ax . legend ( fontsize = 9 ) ax . set_xlim ( 0.5 , max_len + 0.5 ) ax . set_xlabel ( "Длина слова (символов)" ) ax . set_ylabel ( "Количество слов" ) ax . set_title ( title , fontsize = 13 , pad = 10 ) ax . grid ( alpha = 0.25 , axis = "y" ) _safe_savefig ( fig , out_path ) def _top_words_bar ( top_words : List [ Tuple [ str , int ]], out_path : Path , title : str = "Top-N most frequent words" , max_label_len : int = 22 , ) -> None : """Horizontal bar chart of the top-N most frequent words.""" n = len ( top_words ) if n == 0 : top_words = [( "(none)" , 0 )] n = 1 # Reverse so the largest bar is at the top of the chart pairs = list ( reversed ( top_words )) words = [ w if len ( w ) <= max_label_len else w [: max_label_len - 1 ] + "…" for w , _ in pairs ] counts = [ c for _ , c in pairs ] fig_h = max ( 6 , min ( 34 , n * 0.30 + 2 )) fig , ax = plt . subplots ( figsize = ( 11 , fig_h ), constrained_layout = True ) ys = np . arange ( n ) colors = plt . cm . plasma ( np . linspace ( 0.05 , 0.85 , n )) if n > 1 else [ viz . COLOR_PRIMARY ] ax . barh ( ys , counts , color = colors , alpha = 0.9 , edgecolor = "black" , linewidth = 0.3 ) ax . set_yticks ( ys ) ax . set_yticklabels ( words , fontsize = max ( 5 , 9 - n // 25 )) ax . invert_yaxis () # so the largest is at top after our manual reverse cancels out ax . set_xlabel ( "Количество вхождений" ) ax . set_title ( title , fontsize = 13 , pad = 10 ) ax . grid ( alpha = 0.25 , axis = "x" ) # Annotate counts on bars (only if not too crowded) if n <= 50 : for y , c in zip ( ys , counts ): ax . text ( c + max ( counts ) * 0.005 , y , str ( c ), va = "center" , fontsize = 7 ) _safe_savefig ( fig , out_path ) def _zipf_curve ( counts_sorted : np . ndarray , out_path : Path , title : str = "Zipf distribution (log-log)" , ) -> None : """Log-log Zipf plot: rank vs frequency.""" fig , ax = plt . subplots ( figsize = ( 10 , 6 ), constrained_layout = True ) if counts_sorted . size == 0 : ax . text ( 0.5 , 0.5 , "no tokens" , ha = "center" , va = "center" ) else : ranks = np . arange ( 1 , counts_sorted . size + 1 ) ax . loglog ( ranks , counts_sorted , color = viz . COLOR_PRIMARY , alpha = 0.55 , linewidth = 0 , marker = "o" , markersize = 2.5 ) # Reference 1/r line (Zipf's law): intercept = count of #1 word c1 = float ( counts_sorted [ 0 ]) ref = c1 / ranks ax . loglog ( ranks , ref , color = viz . COLOR_ACCENT , linestyle = "--" , linewidth = 1.5 , alpha = 0.8 , label = "идеал 1/r (Zipf)" ) ax . legend ( fontsize = 9 ) ax . set_xlabel ( "Ранг слова (log)" ) ax . set_ylabel ( "Частота (log)" ) ax . set_title ( title , fontsize = 13 , pad = 10 ) ax . grid ( alpha = 0.25 , which = "both" , linestyle = ":" ) _safe_savefig ( fig , out_path ) # ============================================================ # Index construction helpers # ============================================================ def _compute_line_starts ( text : str ) -> List [ int ]: """ Build the array of absolute char-offsets where each line begins. ``line_starts[i]`` is the offset of line ``i+1``. The sentinel ``line_starts[-1]`` is one past the end of the text (so bisect_right always returns a valid 1-indexed line number for any offset inside the text). """ starts = [ 0 ] # Use find for speed (str.split makes a copy, which is wasteful for # 600 KB of text). We walk the \n positions and add +1 to land on the # first char of the next line. pos = text . find ( " \n " ) while pos != - 1 : starts . append ( pos + 1 ) pos = text . find ( " \n " , pos + 1 ) starts . append ( len ( text ) + 1 ) # sentinel return starts def _classify_letter ( ch : str ) -> str : """ Return the bucket key for a word starting with character ``ch``. * Upper-cased first letter (e.g. ``"А"``, ``"B"``, ``"Z"``) for letter starts. * ``"_other"`` for any non-letter start (digits, underscores, etc.). This becomes the filename ``index/_other.json`` per the Stage 2 spec. """ if not ch : return "_other" if ch . isalpha (): return ch . upper () return "_other" def _compact_locations ( locs : List [ Tuple [ int , int , int ]]) -> List [ List [ int ]]: """Convert list of 3-tuples to list of 3-element lists for JSON.""" return [[ int ( c ), int ( l ), int ( o )] for c , l , o in locs ] def _build_word_entry ( word : str , locations : List [ Tuple [ int , int , int ]], max_locations : int , ) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """ Build a single word entry, applying size-bounding rules. * count > SAMPLING_THRESHOLD  → sampled format (first SAMPLE_FIRST_N exact + per-chapter summary). * otherwise                   → up to ``max_locations`` exact locations. """ count = len ( locations ) if count > SAMPLING_THRESHOLD : # Per-chapter summary per_chapter : Counter = Counter () for chap , _line , _off in locations : per_chapter [ chap ] += 1 by_chapter = [ { "c" : int ( c ), "n" : int ( n )} for c , n in sorted ( per_chapter . items ()) ] return { "w" : word , "n" : count , "sampled" : True , "first_5" : _compact_locations ( locations [: SAMPLE_FIRST_N ]), "by_chapter" : by_chapter , } # Regular word — cap at max_locations capped = locations [: max_locations ] return { "w" : word , "n" : count , "loc" : _compact_locations ( capped ), "truncated" : count > max_locations , } def _write_sharded_json ( bucket : List [ Dict [ str , Any ]], base_path : Path , max_bytes : int = MAX_FILE_BYTES , ) -> List [ Path ]: """ Write ``bucket`` (a list of word entries) to JSON, sharding if the serialized form exceeds ``max_bytes``. Returns the list of files actually written. Always writes at least one file (``base_path``); if sharding is needed, subsequent shards are named ``base_path_2``, ``base_path_3``, … """ if not bucket : # Empty bucket — still emit an (almost) empty file for the schema # to be consistent. base_path . write_text ( json . dumps ({ "words" : []}, ensure_ascii = False ), encoding = "utf-8" , ) return [ base_path ] # First, sort the bucket alphabetically by word so shard boundaries are # stable and meaningful. bucket = sorted ( bucket , key = lambda e : e [ "w" ]) # Try to write as one file payload = json . dumps ({ "words" : bucket }, ensure_ascii = False , separators = ( "," , ":" )) encoded = payload . encode ( "utf-8" ) if len ( encoded ) <= max_bytes : base_path . write_bytes ( encoded ) return [ base_path ] # Need to shard — binary-search for the largest prefix that fits. # Each shard needs ~30 bytes of JSON wrapper ({"words":[...]}). written : List [ Path ] = [] remaining = bucket shard_idx = 1 while remaining : # Binary search: find max k such that json of remaining[:k] fits lo , hi = 1 , len ( remaining ) best_k = 1 while lo <= hi : mid = ( lo + hi ) // 2 payload = json . dumps ( { "words" : remaining [: mid ]}, ensure_ascii = False , separators = ( "," , ":" ), ) . encode ( "utf-8" ) if len ( payload ) <= max_bytes : best_k = mid lo = mid + 1 else : hi = mid - 1 shard = remaining [: best_k ] remaining = remaining [ best_k :] if shard_idx == 1 : shard_path = base_path else : shard_path = base_path . with_name ( f " { base_path . stem } _ { shard_idx }{ base_path . suffix } " ) shard_path . write_bytes ( json . dumps ({ "words" : shard }, ensure_ascii = False , separators = ( "," , ":" )) . encode ( "utf-8" ) ) written . append ( shard_path ) shard_idx += 1 return written # ============================================================ # Recipe # ============================================================ def recipe_themes_alpha ( filepaths : list , * , chapter_pattern : Optional [ str ] = None , lang : str = "auto" , max_locations : int = 20 , top_n : int = 100 , out_dir : Path = None , ) -> dict : """ Build an alphabetical inverted index of every word in the input text, with source tracking (chapter / line / char-offset) and aggressive size optimization. Args: filepaths: length-1 list with the input file path (.txt / .md / .epub supported via ``core.read_any``). chapter_pattern: optional regex for chapter headings. If ``None``, ``core.split_chapters`` auto-detects. lang: chapter-pattern language hint (``auto`` / ``ru`` / ``ua`` / ``en`` / ``inline_ru`` / ``inline_en``). max_locations: max number of exact ``[chapter, line, offset]`` locations stored per ordinary word (default 20). Words with more than 100 occurrences bypass this and use a sampled format (chapter summary + first 5 exact locations). top_n: how many top-frequency words to expose in ``summary.json`` and the top-words PNG (default 100). out_dir: directory for all artifacts. If ``None``, no artifacts are written (useful when stacking recipes). Returns: ``{"report": dict, "verdict_lines": list, "artifacts": dict}``. The ``artifacts`` mapping contains at most four entries: ``alphabet_histogram``, ``wordlength_histogram``, ``top_words``, ``zipf_curve``. """ # ----- 0. validate inputs ------------------------------------------- if len ( filepaths ) != 1 : raise ConfigurationError ( "themes_alpha expects exactly 1 file" , hint = "Pass a single .txt / .md / .epub path." , ) if max_locations < 1 : raise ConfigurationError ( f "max_locations must be >= 1, got { max_locations } " , ) if top_n < 1 : raise ConfigurationError ( f "top_n must be >= 1, got { top_n } " , ) filepath = Path ( filepaths [ 0 ]) log . info ( f "[themes_alpha] file= { filepath } " ) # ----- 1. read + split chapters ------------------------------------- text , fmt = core . read_any ( filepath ) log . info ( f "  read: { len ( text ) } chars, format= { fmt } " ) chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , pattern = chapter_pattern , lang = lang ) n_chap = len ( chapters ) # Defensive fallback: ``core.split_chapters`` returns [] when the pattern # matched but every chapter was shorter than ``min_chapter_chars`` (500). # For the inverted-index recipe we do NOT want to fail in that case — # the index is still useful with the whole text treated as one chapter. # Every token will then get chapter_num=1 (since chapter_starts=[0]). if n_chap < 1 : log . warning ( "no chapters passed min_chapter_chars filter; " "treating whole text as single chapter" , ) chapters = [{ "title" : "Whole text" , "body" : text , "start" : 0 , "end" : len ( text ), "paragraphs" : [ text ], }] n_chap = 1 log . info ( f "  chapters: { n_chap } " ) # ----- 2. precompute offset→chapter and offset→line lookup tables -- # chapter_starts: sorted list of `ch["start"]` (= m.end() of the # heading) — i.e. the offset where chapter body starts. chapter_starts = [ ch [ "start" ] for ch in chapters ] line_starts = _compute_line_starts ( text ) log . info ( f "  lookup tables: chapter_starts= { len ( chapter_starts ) } , " f "line_starts= { len ( line_starts ) } " ) # ----- 3. single-pass tokenization with location tracking ---------- word_locations : Dict [ str , List [ Tuple [ int , int , int ]]] = defaultdict ( list ) total_tokens = 0 word_lengths : List [ int ] = [] for m in _TOKEN_RE . finditer ( text ): word = m . group ( 0 ) offset = m . start () # chapter number: 0 if before any chapter body, else 1-indexed if not chapter_starts : chap_num = 0 else : idx = bisect . bisect_right ( chapter_starts , offset ) - 1 chap_num = 0 if idx < 0 else idx + 1 # line number: 1-indexed (bisect_right on line_starts) line_num = bisect . bisect_right ( line_starts , offset ) word_locations [ word . lower ()] . append (( chap_num , line_num , offset )) word_lengths . append ( len ( word )) total_tokens += 1 log . info ( f "  tokenized: { total_tokens } occurrences, " f " { len ( word_locations ) } unique words" ) # ----- 4. frequency ranking + summary stats ------------------------- word_counts = { w : len ( locs ) for w , locs in word_locations . items ()} # sorted (word, count) pairs by count desc, then word asc for stability sorted_words = sorted ( word_counts . items (), key = lambda kv : ( - kv [ 1 ], kv [ 0 ]), ) top_words = sorted_words [: top_n ] if sorted_words : most_frequent_word = sorted_words [ 0 ][ 0 ] # Longest word — pick the longest by character count; ties broken # alphabetically for determinism. longest_word = max ( word_counts . keys (), key = lambda w : ( len ( w ), - ord ( w [ 0 ]) if w else 0 )) else : most_frequent_word = None longest_word = "" # ----- 5. alphabet distribution + per-letter top-10 ---------------- letter_total : Counter = Counter () letter_unique : Counter = Counter () letter_top10 : Dict [ str , List [ Tuple [ str , int ]]] = defaultdict ( list ) for w , c in sorted_words : key = _classify_letter ( w [ 0 ]) letter_total [ key ] += c letter_unique [ key ] += 1 if len ( letter_top10 [ key ]) < 10 : letter_top10 [ key ] . append ([ w , c ]) # letter_index: every letter that actually appears (no empty entries) letter_index : Dict [ str , Any ] = {} for key in sorted ( letter_total . keys ()): letter_index [ key ] = { "total_words_starting" : int ( letter_total [ key ]), "unique_words" : int ( letter_unique [ key ]), "top10" : letter_top10 [ key ], } # alphabet distribution sorted by letter (for the histogram + summary) alphabet_distribution = [ ( k , letter_total [ k ]) for k in sorted ( letter_total . keys ()) ] # ----- 6. build word entries + group by letter ---------------------- # Each entry is the JSON-ready dict from _build_word_entry. entries_by_letter : Dict [ str , List [ Dict [ str , Any ]]] = defaultdict ( list ) sampled_count = 0 truncated_count = 0 for w , locs in word_locations . items (): entry = _build_word_entry ( w , locs , max_locations ) if entry . get ( "sampled" ): sampled_count += 1 if entry . get ( "truncated" ): truncated_count += 1 key = _classify_letter ( w [ 0 ]) entries_by_letter [ key ] . append ( entry ) log . info ( f "  size-bounding: sampled= { sampled_count } (> { SAMPLING_THRESHOLD } occ.), " f "truncated= { truncated_count } (> { max_locations } loc.), " f "letters= { len ( entries_by_letter ) } " ) # ----- 7. write all artifacts (if out_dir given) -------------------- artifacts : Dict [ str , Path ] = {} written_files : List [ Path ] = [] total_output_bytes = 0 largest_file : Tuple [ str , int ] = ( "(none)" , 0 ) if out_dir is not None : out_dir = Path ( out_dir ) out_dir . mkdir ( parents = True , exist_ok = True ) index_dir = out_dir / "index" index_dir . mkdir ( parents = True , exist_ok = True ) # --- 7a. paginated index files --- # Always write all letter keys we saw + _other.json (which may be empty) all_keys = sorted ( entries_by_letter . keys ()) if "_other" not in all_keys : all_keys . append ( "_other" ) index_file_paths : Dict [ str , List [ Path ]] = {} for key in all_keys : bucket = entries_by_letter . get ( key , []) base_path = index_dir / f " { key } .json" paths = _write_sharded_json ( bucket , base_path , MAX_FILE_BYTES ) index_file_paths [ key ] = paths for p in paths : written_files . append ( p ) sz = p . stat () . st_size total_output_bytes += sz if sz > largest_file [ 1 ]: largest_file = ( str ( p . relative_to ( out_dir )), sz ) # --- 7b. letter_index.json --- letter_index_path = out_dir / "letter_index.json" letter_index_path . write_text ( json . dumps ( letter_index , ensure_ascii = False , indent = 2 ), encoding = "utf-8" , ) written_files . append ( letter_index_path ) sz = letter_index_path . stat () . st_size total_output_bytes += sz if sz > largest_file [ 1 ]: largest_file = ( str ( letter_index_path . relative_to ( out_dir )), sz ) # --- 7c. summary.json --- summary = { "file" : str ( filepath ), "format" : fmt , "total_chars" : int ( len ( text )), "total_token_occurrences" : int ( total_tokens ), "unique_words" : int ( len ( word_locations )), "total_chapters" : int ( n_chap ), "alphabet_distribution" : { k : v for k , v in alphabet_distribution }, "top_words" : [[ w , c ] for w , c in top_words ], "most_frequent_word" : most_frequent_word , "most_frequent_word_count" : int ( sorted_words [ 0 ][ 1 ]) if sorted_words else 0 , "longest_word" : longest_word , "longest_word_len" : len ( longest_word ), "mean_word_length" : float ( np . mean ( word_lengths )) if word_lengths else 0.0 , "median_word_length" : float ( np . median ( word_lengths )) if word_lengths else 0.0 , "sampled_words_count" : int ( sampled_count ), "truncated_words_count" : int ( truncated_count ), "config" : { "max_locations" : int ( max_locations ), "top_n" : int ( top_n ), "sampling_threshold" : SAMPLING_THRESHOLD , "sample_first_n" : SAMPLE_FIRST_N , "max_file_bytes" : MAX_FILE_BYTES , }, "pagination" : { "letter_files" : { k : [ str ( p . name ) for p in ps ] for k , ps in index_file_paths . items () }, "total_index_files" : sum ( len ( ps ) for ps in index_file_paths . values ()), }, } summary_path = out_dir / "summary.json" summary_path . write_text ( json . dumps ( summary , ensure_ascii = False , indent = 2 ), encoding = "utf-8" , ) written_files . append ( summary_path ) sz = summary_path . stat () . st_size total_output_bytes += sz if sz > largest_file [ 1 ]: largest_file = ( str ( summary_path . relative_to ( out_dir )), sz ) # --- 7d. schema.json --- schema = { "version" : "1.0.0" , "recipe" : "themes_alpha" , "description" : ( "Alphabetical inverted word index with source tracking " "(chapter, line, char-offset) and size optimization." ), "files" : { "summary.json" : "Top-level summary: totals, top-N words, alphabet distribution, config." , "schema.json" : "This file — describes the on-disk layout." , "letter_index.json" : "Per-letter summary: total words, unique words, top-10 most frequent." , "index/<LETTER>.json" : "Inverted index for words starting with <LETTER> (upper-cased)." , "index/_other.json" : "Inverted index for words starting with non-letter characters (digits, underscores)." , "index/<LETTER>_2.json" : "Overflow shard if a letter's bucket exceeds MAX_FILE_BYTES (rare)." , "01_alphabet_histogram.png" : "Bar chart of token occurrences per starting letter." , "02_wordlength_histogram.png" : "Histogram of word lengths (in characters)." , "03_top100_words.png" : f "Top- { top_n } most frequent words (horizontal bar chart)." , "04_zipf_curve.png" : "Log-log Zipf distribution curve (rank vs frequency)." , "report.json" : "Standard poler_toolkit report bundle (meta + metrics + verdict)." , "verdict.md" : "Markdown verdict summary (human-readable)." , }, "word_entry_formats" : { "regular_word (count <= SAMPLING_THRESHOLD)" : { "w" : "string — the lower-cased word" , "n" : "int — total occurrence count" , "loc" : "list of [chapter_num, line_num, char_offset] tuples (up to max_locations)" , "truncated" : "bool — true if count > max_locations (some locations were dropped)" , }, "sampled_word (count > SAMPLING_THRESHOLD)" : { "w" : "string — the lower-cased word" , "n" : "int — total occurrence count" , "sampled" : "true (marker)" , "first_5" : "first 5 exact [chapter_num, line_num, char_offset] tuples" , "by_chapter" : "list of {c: chapter_num, n: count_in_chapter} (sorted by chapter_num)" , }, }, "source_format" : ( "[chapter_num, line_num, char_offset] where " "chapter_num is 1-indexed (0 = before any chapter body), " "line_num is 1-indexed, char_offset is the absolute Non-letter starts go to _other.json." , "max_file_bytes" : MAX_FILE_BYTES , "shard_naming" : "If a letter's bucket exceeds max_file_bytes, shards are named <LETTER>_2.json, <LETTER>_3.json, ..." , }, "size_bounding" : { "max_locations_per_word" : "configurable via max_locations (default 20)" , "sampling_threshold" : SAMPLING_THRESHOLD , "sample_first_n" : SAMPLE_FIRST_N , }, "tokenizer" : r "re.compile(r \" [\w’']+ \" )  — Unicode-aware, includes apostrophes" , "notes" : [ "Words are lower-cased before grouping (case-insensitive index)." , "Apostrophes are preserved inside words (e.g. Ukrainian ібн'я, English don't)." , "Words starting with a digit, underscore, or other non-letter go to _other.json." , "Each index file has the shape { \" words \" : [entry, entry, ...]}." , ], } schema_path = out_dir / "schema.json" schema_path . write_text ( json . dumps ( schema , ensure_ascii = False , indent = 2 ), encoding = "utf-8" , ) written_files . append ( schema_path ) sz = schema_path . stat () . st_size total_output_bytes += sz if sz > largest_file [ 1 ]: largest_file = ( str ( schema_path . relative_to ( out_dir )), sz ) # --- 7e. ------------------------------- metrics : Dict [ str , Any ] = { # input summary "file" : str ( filepath ), "format" : fmt , "total_chars" : int ( len ( text )), "total_token_occurrences" : int ( total_tokens ), "unique_words" : int ( len ( word_locations )), "total_chapters" : int ( n_chap ), "chapter_titles" : [ c [ "title" ] for c in chapters ], # size-bounding config (echo for audit) "max_locations" : int ( max_locations ), "top_n" : int ( top_n ), "sampling_threshold" : SAMPLING_THRESHOLD , "sample_first_n" : SAMPLE_FIRST_N , "max_file_bytes" : MAX_FILE_BYTES , # index shape "sampled_words_count" : int ( sampled_count ), "truncated_words_count" : int ( truncated_count ), "letters_seen" : sorted ( letter_total . keys ()), "alphabet_distribution" : { k : v for k , v in alphabet_distribution }, # frequency stats "top_words" : [[ w , c ] for w , c in top_words ], "most_frequent_word" : most_frequent_word , "most_frequent_word_count" : int ( sorted_words [ 0 ][ 1 ]) if sorted_words else 0 , "longest_word" : longest_word , "longest_word_len" : len ( longest_word ), "mean_word_length" : float ( np . mean ( word_lengths )) if word_lengths else 0.0 , "median_word_length" : float ( np . median ( word_lengths )) if word_lengths else 0.0 , # size report (only meaningful if out_dir was given) "size_report" : { "total_output_bytes" : int ( total_output_bytes ), "total_output_mb" : round ( total_output_bytes / ( 1024 * 1024 ), 4 ), "largest_file" : largest_file [ 0 ], "largest_file_bytes" : int ( largest_file [ 1 ]), "file_count" : len ( written_files ), }, } # ----- 9. Markdown verdict ----------------------------------------- size_mb = total_output_bytes / ( 1024 * 1024 ) budget_ok = total_output_bytes < 5 * 1024 * 1024 if most_frequent_word : verdict_text = ( f "Индекс: { total_tokens : , } вхождений, { len ( word_locations ) : , } уникальных слов, " f " { n_chap } глав. Топ-1: « { most_frequent_word } » ( { sorted_words [ 0 ][ 1 ] : , } ). " f "Самое длинное: « { longest_word } » ( { len ( longest_word ) } симв.). " f "Size-bounding: { sampled_count } sampled, { truncated_count } truncated. " f "Объём вывода: { size_mb : .2f } MB " f "( { 'OK' if budget_ok else 'ВНИМАНИЕ: превышает 5 MB' } )" ) else : verdict_text = "Текст пуст — индекс не построен." verdict_lines = report . verdict_block ( f "Themes Alpha: { filepath . name } " , report . metric_table ( [ "Метрика" , "Значение" ], [ [ "Файл" , str ( filepath )], [ "Формат" , fmt ], [ "Символов" , str ( metrics [ "total_chars" ])], [ "Токенов (regex)" , f " { metrics [ 'total_token_occurrences' ] : , } " ], [ "Уникальных слов" , f " { metrics [ 'unique_words' ] : , } " ], [ "Глав" , str ( metrics [ "total_chapters" ])], [ "Букв в индексе" , str ( len ( metrics [ "letters_seen" ]))], [ "Top-N" , str ( metrics [ "top_n" ])], [ "Max locations/word" , str ( metrics [ "max_locations" ])], [ "Sampling threshold" , str ( metrics [ "sampling_threshold" ])], [ "Sample first-N" , str ( metrics [ "sample_first_n" ])], [ "Max file bytes" , f " { metrics [ 'max_file_bytes' ] : , } " ], [ "Sampled words" , str ( metrics [ "sampled_words_count" ])], [ "Truncated words" , str ( metrics [ "truncated_words_count" ])], [ "Топ-1 слово" , most_frequent_word or "(none)" ], [ "  count" , str ( metrics [ "most_frequent_word_count" ])], [ "Самое длинное" , longest_word or "(none)" ], [ "  длина" , str ( metrics [ "longest_word_len" ])], [ "Средняя длина" , f " { metrics [ 'mean_word_length' ] : .3f } " ], [ "Медианная длина" , f " { metrics [ 'median_word_length' ] : .3f } " ], [ "Объём вывода (bytes)" , f " { total_output_bytes : , } " ], [ "Объём вывода (MB)" , f " { size_mb : .4f } " ], [ "Лимит (MB)" , "5.0000" ], [ "В бюджет?" , "ДА" if budget_ok else "НЕТ" ], [ "Файлов написано" , str ( len ( written_files ))], [ "Крупнейший файл" , largest_file [ 0 ]], [ "  размер (bytes)" , f " { largest_file [ 1 ] : , } " ], ], ), [ "## Распределение по буквам" , "" , * ( "| Буква | Всего слов | Уникальных |" for _ in [ 0 ] ), "|---|---|---|" , * ( f "| { k } | { letter_total [ k ] : , } | { letter_unique [ k ] : , } |" for k in sorted ( letter_total . keys ()) ), "" , ], [ "## Вердикт" , "" , f "** { verdict_text } **" , "" ], ) # ----- 10. wrap into standard report bundle ------------------------- full_report = report . wrap_report ( task_name = "themes_alpha" , engine = "POLER toolkit inverted index + size optimization" , input_files = [ str ( filepath )], metrics = metrics , output_files = { k : str ( v ) for k , v in artifacts . items ()}, verdict = verdict_text , extra = { "letters_seen" : sorted ( letter_total . keys ()), "size_budget_mb" : 5.0 , "size_actual_mb" : round ( size_mb , 4 ), "size_budget_ok" : bool ( budget_ok ), "index_files" : [ str ( p ) for p in written_files ], }, ) return { "report" : full_report , "verdict_lines" : verdict_lines , "artifacts" : artifacts , } __all__ = [ "recipe_themes_alpha" ] # ============================================================ # Standalone test entry-point # ------------------------------------------------------------ # Run:  python3 poler_toolkit/recipes_ext/themes_alpha.py [FILE] # If no FILE is given, defaults to $POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE or the # Cassiopeia canon sample (if available). # Output: $POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/test_themes_alpha_<ts>/ with summary.json, # schema.json, letter_index.json, index/*.json, 4 PNG artifacts, # report.json, verdict.md. # ============================================================ if __name__ == "__main__" : import sys from datetime import datetime logging . basicConfig ( level = logging . INFO , format = " %(asctime)s [ %(name)s ] %(levelname)s : %(message)s " , ) import os default_file = os . environ . get ( "POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" , "" ) if not default_file or not Path ( default_file ) . exists (): for cand in [ "upload/Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt" , "upload/Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt" , ]: if Path ( cand ) . exists (): default_file = cand break test_file = sys . argv [ 1 ] if len ( sys . argv ) > 1 else default_file # Output dir via paths.py (env-aware) from .. import paths as _paths ts = datetime . now () . strftime ( "%Y%m %d _%H%M%S" ) out_dir = _paths . get_output_root () / f "test_themes_alpha_ { ts } " out_dir . mkdir ( parents = True , exist_ok = True ) print ( f "Running themes_alpha on: { test_file } " ) print ( f "Output dir: { out_dir } " ) bundle = recipe_themes_alpha ( [ test_file ], chapter_pattern = None , lang = "auto" , max_locations = 20 , top_n = 100 , out_dir = out_dir , ) # Write report.json + verdict.md (mirrors what runner.run() does) ( out_dir / "report.json" ) . write_text ( json . dumps ( bundle [ "report" ], ensure_ascii = False , indent = 2 , default = str , ), encoding = "utf-8" , ) ( out_dir / "verdict.md" ) . write_text ( " \n " . join ( bundle [ "verdict_lines" ]), encoding = "utf-8" , ) print ( " \n === DONE ===" ) print ( f "Artifacts ( { len ( bundle [ 'artifacts' ]) } ):" ) for name , p in bundle [ "artifacts" ] . items (): print ( f " { name : 22s } -> { p } (exists= { p . exists () } )" ) print ( f " \n report.json: { out_dir / 'report.json' } " ) print ( f "verdict.md: { out_dir / 'verdict.md' } " ) print ( f " \n Verdict: { bundle [ 'report' ][ 'verdict' ] } " ) # Print size summary sr = bundle [ "report" ][ "metrics" ][ "size_report" ] print ( f " \n Size report:" ) print ( f "  total bytes: { sr [ 'total_output_bytes' ] : , } " ) print ( f "  total MB: { sr [ 'total_output_mb' ] : .4f } " ) print ( f "  files: { sr [ 'file_count' ] } " ) print ( f "  largest: { sr [ 'largest_file' ] } ( { sr [ 'largest_file_bytes' ] : , } bytes)" )

__init__.py
Файл: poler_toolkit/serve/__init__.py · Размер: 13 550 байт
""" poler_toolkit.serve — HTTP API wrapper for poler_toolkit. Exposes every toolkit recipe (4 core + 5 ext = 9 total) as a REST endpoint, plus a status endpoint and an OpenAPI 3.0 spec. Files ----- - ``__init__.py``       (this file) — recipe registry + package metadata. - ``server.py``         — HTTP server (http.server + threading). - ``openapi_spec.py``   — programmatic OpenAPI 3.0 spec generator. - ``task_store.py``     — thread-safe task store with 1-hour expiry. Why a serve-side recipe registry? --------------------------------- The core ``poler_toolkit.recipes.RECIPES`` dict only contains the 4 Stage-1 recipes. Stage 2 added 5 extension recipes in ``recipes_ext/`` but the spec says "do NOT modify recipes.py". We therefore build our own ``SERVE_RECIPES`` registry here that fuses both lists. The runner is *not* used for ext recipes because ``runner.run()`` only forwards ``keyword / chapter_pattern / lang / top_n / names``; the ext recipes have their own option sets (``plugin_path``, ``window_size``, ``extensions``, ``max_locations``…) that the runner can't forward. The serve dispatcher therefore calls each recipe function directly and replicates the runner's report.json / verdict.md saving step. Usage ----- # Start the server (foreground) python -m poler_toolkit.serve.server --port 8765 --host 127.0.0.1 # Or as a module entry point (also works) python -m poler_toolkit.serve --port 8765 # From Python from poler_toolkit.serve.server import run_server run_server(port=8765) """ from __future__ import annotations from typing import Any , Callable , Dict , List , Optional # Recipe functions --------------------------------------------------------- from ..recipes import ( recipe_analyze , recipe_compare , recipe_structure , recipe_characters , ) from ..recipes_ext import ( recipe_theme_evolution , recipe_themes_alpha , recipe_diff_versions , recipe_batch_directory , recipe_custom_plugin , recipe_smart_read , recipe_smart_search , recipe_smart_batch , recipe_smart_cache , recipe_smart_diff , ) __version__ = "1.0.0" # Language enum shared by every recipe that takes a ``lang`` option. LANG_ENUM = [ "auto" , "ru" , "ua" , "en" , "inline_ru" , "inline_en" ] # -------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Option schema helper # -------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Each option is described by a small dict so the OpenAPI generator can # build a JSON Schema automatically. ``required`` here means "the user # must explicitly pass this option" — most options are optional because # the recipes have sensible defaults. def _opt ( type_ : str , * , default : Any = None , required : bool = False , enum : Optional [ List [ str ]] = None , description : str = "" , items : Optional [ Dict [ str , Any ]] = None , minimum : Optional [ float ] = None , ) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: d : Dict [ str , Any ] = { "type" : type_ , "required" : required , "description" : description , } if default is not None : d [ "default" ] = default if enum is not None : d [ "enum" ] = enum if items is not None : d [ "items" ] = items if minimum is not None : d [ "minimum" ] = minimum return d # -------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Recipe registry # -------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Each entry mirrors the structure of ``recipes.RECIPES`` but adds a # typed ``options`` dict for OpenAPI generation. ``n_files`` is either # an int (exact count) or None (variable — used by custom_plugin). SERVE_RECIPES : Dict [ str , Dict [ str , Any ]] = { "analyze" : { "fn" : recipe_analyze , "n_files" : 1 , "description" : ( "Deep analysis of one file: chapters, POLER analyze_large_file " "by keyword, fragment clustering, TF-IDF + KMeans + PCA, " "Shannon entropy, character map. 5-7 PNG + JSON + Markdown." ), "options" : { "keyword" : _opt ( "string" , description = "POLER keyword (default: auto-detect)." ), "chapter_pattern" : _opt ( "string" , description = "Custom regex for chapter headings (overrides --lang)." ), "lang" : _opt ( "string" , default = "auto" , enum = LANG_ENUM , description = "Chapter-pattern language hint." ), "top_n" : _opt ( "integer" , default = 15 , minimum = 1 , description = "top_n for POLER analyze." ), }, }, "compare" : { "fn" : recipe_compare , "n_files" : 2 , "description" : ( "Run analyze on both files and compare: neighbour cosine, " "silhouette, PCA explained variance, chapter similarity, " "jump fractions, entropy. Dashboard + verdict." ), "options" : { "keyword" : _opt ( "string" ), "chapter_pattern" : _opt ( "string" ), "lang" : _opt ( "string" , default = "auto" , enum = LANG_ENUM ), }, }, "structure" : { "fn" : recipe_structure , "n_files" : 1 , "description" : ( "Structural analysis only — no POLER engine. Chapters + " "TF-IDF + KMeans + PCA + entropy + chapter matrix. Faster " "than analyze. 4 PNG + JSON + Markdown." ), "options" : { "chapter_pattern" : _opt ( "string" ), "lang" : _opt ( "string" , default = "auto" , enum = LANG_ENUM ), }, }, "characters" : { "fn" : recipe_characters , "n_files" : 1 , "description" : ( "Character map: POLER grep_search_file per name + co-" "occurrence matrix. If --names not given, auto-detects " "top-10 capitalised words." ), "options" : { "names" : _opt ( "array" , items = { "type" : "string" }, description = "List of character names. Default: auto-detect." , ), }, }, "theme_evolution" : { "fn" : recipe_theme_evolution , "n_files" : 1 , "description" : ( "Track poler_v6.EMOTIONAL_MARKERS evolution across chapters. If omitted, filepaths[0] must end in .py." , ), "timeout" : _opt ( "integer" , default = 300 , minimum = 1 , description = "Plugin execution timeout in seconds." ), "plugin_opts" : _opt ( "object" , description = "Free-form dict passed as plugin_opts to the plugin's run()." , ), }, }, "smart_read" : { "fn" : recipe_smart_read , "n_files" : 1 , "description" : "Token-efficient file read: summary / context / full / streaming modes." , "options" : { "mode" : _opt ( "string" , default = "summary" , enum = [ "summary" , "context" , "full" , "streaming" ], description = "Read mode." ), "query" : _opt ( "string" , description = "Query for context mode." ), "max_chars" : _opt ( "integer" , default = 2000 , minimum = 100 , description = "Max chars for full mode." ), "top_n" : _opt ( "integer" , default = 20 , minimum = 1 , description = "Top-N words for summary." ), "context_chars" : _opt ( "integer" , default = 500 , minimum = 50 , description = "Context window size." ), }, }, "smart_search" : { "fn" : recipe_smart_search , "n_files" : None , "description" : "Unified search: regex + full-text + file discovery. registry. Drops the non-serialisable ``fn`` reference and any ``items`` sub- dicts that the OpenAPI generator needs but a plain JSON dump might choke on if a Path sneaks in. Keeps everything else. """ out : Dict [ str , Dict [ str , Any ]] = {} for name , spec in SERVE_RECIPES . items (): out [ name ] = { "n_files" : spec [ "n_files" ], "description" : spec [ "description" ], "options" : spec [ "options" ], } return out __all__ = [ "SERVE_RECIPES" , "LANG_ENUM" , "list_recipes" , "__version__" , ]
