""" poler_toolkit.serve.openapi_spec — programmatic OpenAPI 3.0 generator. Builds an OpenAPI 3.0 spec from the ``SERVE_RECIPES`` registry in ``poler_toolkit.serve.__init__``. The spec covers: * ``GET  /``             — service info. * ``GET  /health``       — health check. * ``GET  /openapi.json`` — this spec. * ``GET  /recipes``      — recipe list. * ``GET  /tasks/{id}``   — poll task status. * ``POST /<recipe>``     — submit a recipe for async execution. The request schema is the same for every POST endpoint (filepaths + options); the response schema is the same async-ack envelope. Per- recipe options are exposed as a discriminated ``options`` sub-schema via ``oneOf`` so a client can introspect what each recipe accepts. Stdlib only (``json``). """ from __future__ import annotations from typing import Any , Dict from . import SERVE_RECIPES , __version__ as PKG_VERSION # -------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Shared schema fragments # -------------------------------------------------------------------------- def _filepaths_schema () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: return { "type" : "array" , "items" : { "type" : "string" }, "description" : ( "List of input file paths. Most recipes take exactly one file; " "`compare` and `diff_versions` take two; `batch_directory` takes " "a single directory path; `custom_plugin` takes a variable " "number (plugin_path + target files, or just targets when " "plugin_path is given in options)." ), } def _options_for_recipe ( name : str , spec : Dict [ str , Any ]) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """Build a JSON-Schema fragment for one recipe's options dict.""" props : Dict [ str , Any ] = {} required : list = [] for opt_name , opt_spec in spec . get ( "options" , {}) . items (): prop : Dict [ str , Any ] = { "type" : opt_spec [ "type" ]} if "description" in opt_spec and opt_spec [ "description" ]: prop [ "description" ] = opt_spec [ "description" ] if "enum" in opt_spec and opt_spec [ "enum" ] is not None : prop [ "enum" ] = list ( opt_spec [ "enum" ]) if "default" in opt_spec and opt_spec [ "default" ] is not None : prop [ "default" ] = opt_spec [ "default" ] if "minimum" in opt_spec and opt_spec [ "minimum" ] is not None : prop [ "minimum" ] = opt_spec [ "minimum" ] if "items" in opt_spec and opt_spec [ "items" ] is not None : prop [ "items" ] = opt_spec [ "items" ] props [ opt_name ] = prop if opt_spec . get ( "required" ): required . append ( opt_name ) schema : Dict [ str , Any ] = { "type" : "object" , "properties" : props , "additionalProperties" : True , "description" : f "Options for the ` { name } ` recipe." , } if required : schema [ "required" ] = required return schema def _request_body_for_recipe ( name : str , spec : Dict [ str , Any ]) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """Build the OpenAPI requestBody object for a POST /<recipe> endpoint.""" options_schema = _options_for_recipe ( name , spec ) n_files = spec . get ( "n_files" ) if n_files == 1 : filepaths_desc = "Exactly one file path." elif n_files == 2 : filepaths_desc = "Exactly two file paths (order matters for compare / diff_versions)." elif n_files is None : filepaths_desc = ( "Variable file count: when `options.plugin_path` is set, this is " "the list of target files; otherwise the first entry must be the " "plugin .py file." ) else : filepaths_desc = f "Exactly { n_files } file paths." schema = { "type" : "object" , "properties" : { "filepaths" : { "type" : "array" , "items" : { "type" : "string" }, "description" : filepaths_desc , }, "options" : options_schema , }, "required" : [ "filepaths" ], "additionalProperties" : False , } return { "required" : True , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : schema }, }, } def _async_ack_schema () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """Schema for the immediate POST response (task accepted, not done).""" return { "type" : "object" , "properties" : { "status" : { "type" : "string" , "enum" : [ "pending" ]}, "task_id" : { "type" : "string" , "description" : "12-char hex ID. Poll GET /tasks/ {task_id} for status." , }, "recipe" : { "type" : "string" }, "submitted_at" : { "type" : "string" , "format" : "date-time" }, "poll_url" : { "type" : "string" , "description" : "Absolute or relative URL to poll for task status." , }, }, "required" : [ "status" , "task_id" , "recipe" ], } def _task_status_schema () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """Schema for GET /tasks/{task_id} responses (all states).""" return { "type" : "object" , "properties" : { "task_id" : { "type" : "string" }, "recipe" : { "type" : "string" }, "status" : { "type" : "string" , "enum" : [ "pending" , "running" , "completed" , "failed" , "expired" ], }, "created_at" : { "type" : "number" , "format" : "float" }, "updated_at" : { "type" : "number" , "format" : "float" }, # present only when status == "completed": "output_dir" : { "type" : "string" }, "report" : { "type" : "object" }, "verdict" : { "type" : "string" }, "artifacts" : { "type" : "object" , "additionalProperties" : { "type" : "string" }, "description" : "Mapping artifact_name → absolute file path." , }, # present only when status == "failed": "error" : { "type" : "string" }, "error_type" : { "type" : "string" }, }, "required" : [ "task_id" , "recipe" , "status" ], } def _error_schema () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: return { "type" : "object" , "properties" : { "status" : { "type" : "string" , "enum" : [ "error" ]}, "error" : { "type" : "string" }, "error_type" : { "type" : "string" }, }, "required" : [ "status" , "error" ], } # -------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Top-level spec builder # -------------------------------------------------------------------------- def build_spec ( server_host : str = "127.0.0.1" , server_port : int = 8000 ) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """Build the full OpenAPI 3.0 spec as a JSON-serialisable dict. Args: server_host: hostname used in the ``servers`` list (informational). server_port: port used in the ``servers`` list (informational). """ base_url = f "http:// { server_host } : { server_port } " paths : Dict [ str , Dict [ str , Any ]] = {} # ---- GET / ------------------------------------------------------- paths [ "/" ] = { "get" : { "summary" : "Service info + list of recipes" , "operationId" : "getServiceInfo" , "tags" : [ "meta" ], "responses" : { "200" : { "description" : "Service metadata and recipe list." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : { "type" : "object" , "properties" : { "name" : { "type" : "string" }, "version" : { "type" : "string" }, "endpoints" : { "type" : "object" }, "recipes" : { "type" : "object" }, }, }, }, }, }, }, } } # ---- GET /health ------------------------------------------------- paths [ "/health" ] = { "get" : { "summary" : "Health check" , "operationId" : "healthCheck" , "tags" : [ "meta" ], "responses" : { "200" : { "description" : "Server is healthy." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : { "type" : "object" , "properties" : { "status" : { "type" : "string" , "enum" : [ "ok" ]}, "version" : { "type" : "string" }, "time" : { "type" : "string" , "format" : "date-time" }, "tasks_active" : { "type" : "integer" }, }, "required" : [ "status" ], }, }, }, }, }, } } # ---- GET /openapi.json ------------------------------------------- paths [ "/openapi.json" ] = { "get" : { "summary" : "This OpenAPI 3.0 spec" , "operationId" : "getOpenApiSpec" , "tags" : [ "meta" ], "responses" : { "200" : { "description" : "OpenAPI 3.0 JSON document." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : { "type" : "object" }, }, }, }, }, } } # ---- GET /recipes ------------------------------------------------ paths [ "/recipes" ] = { "get" : { "summary" : "List recipes with their options" , "operationId" : "listRecipes" , "tags" : [ "meta" ], "responses" : { "200" : { "description" : "Recipe registry: name → {description, n_files, options}." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : { "type" : "object" , "additionalProperties" : { "type" : "object" }, }, }, }, }, }, } } # ---- GET /tasks/{task_id} ---------------------------------------- paths [ "/tasks/ {task_id} " ] = { "get" : { "summary" : "Poll task status by ID" , "operationId" : "getTaskStatus" , "tags" : [ "tasks" ], "parameters" : [ { "name" : "task_id" , "in" : "path" , "required" : True , "schema" : { "type" : "string" }, "description" : "12-char hex task ID returned by any POST /<recipe> endpoint." , }, ], "responses" : { "200" : { "description" : "Current task state. `result` is populated when `status == 'completed'`; `error`/`error_type` when `status == 'failed'`." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : _task_status_schema (), }, }, }, "404" : { "description" : "Task not found or expired (TTL is 1 hour)." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : _error_schema ()}, }, }, }, } } # ---- POST /<recipe> (one path per recipe) ------------------------ for name , spec in SERVE_RECIPES . items (): path = f "/ { name } " paths [ path ] = { "post" : { "summary" : f "Run the ` { name } ` recipe (async)" , "description" : spec [ "description" ], "operationId" : f "runRecipe_ { name } " , "tags" : [ "recipes" ], "requestBody" : _request_body_for_recipe ( name , spec ), "responses" : { "202" : { "description" : "Task accepted; client should poll GET /tasks/ {task_id} ." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : _async_ack_schema ()}, }, }, "400" : { "description" : "Malformed request body or wrong file count." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : _error_schema ()}, }, }, "404" : { "description" : "Unknown recipe." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : _error_schema ()}, }, }, "500" : { "description" : "Internal error submitting the task." , "content" : { "application/json" : { "schema" : _error_schema ()}, }, }, }, } } spec : Dict [ str , Any ] = { "openapi" : "3.0.3" , "info" : { "title" : "POLER Toolkit HTTP API" , "description" : ( "REST wrapper around the poler_toolkit recipe runner. " "Every POST endpoint accepts a list of filepaths + an " "options dict, returns a task_id immediately, and runs " "the recipe in a background thread. Poll " "GET /tasks/ {task_id} for the result. Tasks auto-expire " "after 1 hour." ), "version" : PKG_VERSION , "license" : { "name" : "MIT" }, }, "servers" : [{ "url" : base_url }], "tags" : [ { "name" : "meta" , "description" : "Service metadata endpoints." }, { "name" : "recipes" , "description" : "Recipe execution endpoints (async)." }, { "name" : "tasks" , "description" : "Task status polling." }, ], "paths" : paths , } return spec __all__ = [ "build_spec" ]
server.py
Файл: poler_toolkit/serve/server.py · Размер: 19 253 байт
#!/usr/bin/env python3 """ poler_toolkit.serve.server — HTTP API server. Exposes every poler_toolkit recipe (4 core + 5 ext = 9 total) as an async REST endpoint, plus status / health / OpenAPI endpoints. Uses stdlib only (``http.server`` + ``threading`` + ``json``). Endpoints --------- GET  /                       service info + recipe list GET  /health                 {"status": "ok", ...} GET  /openapi.json           OpenAPI 3.0 spec GET  /recipes                recipe registry GET  /tasks/<task_id>        poll task status POST /analyze                run analyze (1 file) POST /compare                run compare (2 files) POST /structure              run structure (1 file) POST /characters             run characters (1 file) POST /theme_evolution        run theme_evolution (1 file) POST /themes_alpha           run themes_alpha (1 file) POST /diff_versions          run diff_versions (2 files) POST /batch_directory        run batch_directory (1 dir path) POST /custom_plugin          run custom_plugin (variable) Request body for POST endpoints ------------------------------- { "filepaths": ["/path/to/file.txt"], "options": {"keyword": "Лёша", "lang": "auto"} } Response (POST, immediate) -------------------------- { "status": "pending", "task_id": "abc123def456", "recipe": "structure", "submitted_at": "2026-...", "poll_url": "/tasks/abc123def456" } Response (GET /tasks/<task_id>, when complete) ---------------------------------------------- { "task_id": "abc123def456", "recipe": "structure", "status": "completed", "output_dir": "<output_root>/structure_<timestamp>", "report": {...}, "verdict": "...", "artifacts": {"01_...": "<output_root>/structure_<timestamp>/01_...png", ...} } On error: status == "failed" with ``error`` + ``error_type`` fields. CLI --- python -m poler_toolkit.serve.server [--port 8765] [--host 127.0.0.1] python -m poler_toolkit.serve          [--port 8765] [--host 127.0.0.1] """ from __future__ import annotations import argparse import json import logging import sys import threading import traceback from datetime import datetime from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler , ThreadingHTTPServer from pathlib import Path from typing import Any , Dict , List , Optional from urllib.parse import urlparse from . import SERVE_RECIPES , __version__ as PKG_VERSION , list_recipes from .openapi_spec import build_spec from .task_store import TaskStore from .. import core from .. import report from ..errors import PolerToolkitError log = logging . getLogger ( "poler_toolkit.serve.server" ) # Shared task store — one per process is fine; ThreadingHTTPServer handles # concurrent requests, and TaskStore is internally thread-safe. _STORE = TaskStore () # Module-level server config (set by ``run_server``) so the handler can # build correct absolute poll URLs and OpenAPI servers list. _HOST = "127.0.0.1" _PORT = 8000 # ============================================================ # Recipe dispatcher # ============================================================ def _filter_options ( recipe_name : str , options : Dict [ str , Any ]) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """Keep only options that are declared in the recipe's spec. Drops unknown keys silently (they would be rejected by the recipe function anyway). Also coerces simple type mismatches where possible (e.g. ``top_n`` as string → int). """ spec = SERVE_RECIPES . get ( recipe_name ) if spec is None : return {} allowed = spec [ "options" ] out : Dict [ str , Any ] = {} for k , v in ( options or {}) . items (): if k not in allowed : continue type_ = allowed [ k ][ "type" ] try : if v is None : out [ k ] = v elif type_ == "integer" and not isinstance ( v , int ): out [ k ] = int ( v ) elif type_ == "boolean" and not isinstance ( v , bool ): out [ k ] = str ( v ) . lower () in ( "1" , "true" , "yes" , "on" ) elif type_ == "string" and not isinstance ( v , str ): out [ k ] = str ( v ) else : out [ k ] = v except ( TypeError , ValueError ): out [ k ] = v # let the recipe reject it with a clear error return out def _validate_filepaths ( recipe_name : str , filepaths : List [ str ]) -> Optional [ str ]: """Return an error message string if file count is wrong, else None.""" spec = SERVE_RECIPES . get ( recipe_name ) if spec is None : return f "unknown recipe ' { recipe_name } '" expected = spec [ "n_files" ] if expected is None : # variable count (custom_plugin); require at least 1 entry if not filepaths : return "filepaths must contain at least one entry" return None if len ( filepaths ) != expected : return ( f "recipe ' { recipe_name } ' expects { expected } file(s), " f "got { len ( filepaths ) } " ) return None def _dispatch_recipe ( recipe_name : str , filepaths : List [ str ], options : Dict [ str , Any ], ) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """Run a recipe synchronously and return the result payload. Returns a dict with ``output_dir``, ``report``, ``verdict``, ``artifacts`` keys. Raises on any error. This is the function the background worker thread calls. It replicates the runner.run() flow but supports every recipe's option set (not just the 5 the runner forwards). """ spec = SERVE_RECIPES [ recipe_name ] fn = spec [ "fn" ] filtered = _filter_options ( recipe_name , options ) # Output dir for this run. out_dir = core . make_output_dir ( recipe_name ) # Every recipe takes (filepaths, *, <opts>, out_dir=None). kwargs : Dict [ str , Any ] = dict ( filtered ) kwargs [ "out_dir" ] = out_dir log . info ( f "[dispatch] recipe= { recipe_name } files= { len ( filepaths ) } " f "opts_keys= { list ( filtered . keys ()) } out_dir= { out_dir } " ) bundle = fn ( filepaths , ** kwargs ) # Persist report.json + verdict.md (mirrors runner.run). report_path = report . save_json_report ( bundle [ "report" ], out_dir , filename = "report.json" , ) md_path = report . save_markdown_verdict ( bundle [ "verdict_lines" ], out_dir , filename = "verdict.md" , ) # Serialise artifacts: Path → str. artifacts_serial : Dict [ str , str ] = {} for name , p in ( bundle . get ( "artifacts" ) or {}) . items (): try : artifacts_serial [ name ] = str ( Path ( p )) except Exception : artifacts_serial [ name ] = str ( p ) verdict_text = "" try : verdict_text = Path ( md_path ) . read_text ( encoding = "utf-8" ) except Exception : verdict_text = bundle [ "report" ] . get ( "verdict" , "" ) return { "output_dir" : str ( out_dir ), "report" : bundle [ "report" ], "verdict" : verdict_text , "artifacts" : artifacts_serial , "report_path" : str ( report_path ), "verdict_path" : str ( md_path ), } def _worker ( task_id : str , recipe_name : str , filepaths : List [ str ], options : Dict [ str , Any ]) -> None : """Background worker: transition task through running → done/failed.""" _STORE . mark_running ( task_id ) try : result = _dispatch_recipe ( recipe_name , filepaths , options ) _STORE . mark_completed ( task_id , result ) log . info ( f "[worker] task { task_id } completed: { result [ 'output_dir' ] } " ) except PolerToolkitError as e : msg = f " { e . message } " + ( f " \n Hint: { e . hint } " if e . hint else "" ) _STORE . mark_failed ( task_id , error = msg , error_type = type ( e ) . __name__ ) log . warning ( f "[worker] task { task_id } failed ( { type ( e ) . __name__ } ): { e } " ) except Exception as e : tb = traceback . format_exc ( limit = 4 ) _STORE . mark_failed ( task_id , error = f " { type ( e ) . __name__ } : { e } \n { tb } " , error_type = type ( e ) . __name__ , ) log . error ( f "[worker] task { task_id } crashed: { e } " , exc_info = True ) # ============================================================ # HTTP helpers # ============================================================ def _send_json ( handler : BaseHTTPRequestHandler , data : Any , status : int = 200 ) -> None : body = json . dumps ( data , ensure_ascii = False , indent = 2 , default = str ) . encode ( "utf-8" ) handler . send_response ( status ) handler . send_header ( "Content-Type" , "application/json; charset=utf-8" ) handler . send_header ( "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" , "*" ) handler . send_header ( "Access-Control-Allow-Methods" , "GET, POST, OPTIONS" ) handler . send_header ( "Access-Control-Allow-Headers" , "Content-Type" ) handler . send_header ( "Content-Length" , str ( len ( body ))) handler . end_headers () handler . wfile . write ( body ) def _read_body ( handler : BaseHTTPRequestHandler ) -> Dict [ str , Any ]: length = int ( handler . headers . get ( "Content-Length" , 0 ) or 0 ) if length == 0 : return {} raw = handler . rfile . read ( length ) if not raw : return {} return json . loads ( raw . decode ( "utf-8" )) def _ok_error ( handler : BaseHTTPRequestHandler , error : str , error_type : str = "Error" , status : int = 400 ) -> None : _send_json ( handler , { "status" : "error" , "error" : error , "error_type" : error_type , }, status = status ) # ============================================================ # HTTP handler # ============================================================ class ServeHandler ( BaseHTTPRequestHandler ): """HTTP request handler for the poler_toolkit API.""" # BaseHTTPRequestHandler sets protocol_version = "HTTP/1.0" by # default, which closes the connection after every response. We # want keep-alive so curl/python-requests can poll efficiently. protocol_version = "HTTP/1.1" @property def server_version ( self ) -> str : # type: ignore[override] return f "poler-toolkit-serve/ { PKG_VERSION } " # ---- CORS preflight --------------------------------------------- def do_OPTIONS ( self ) -> None : self . send_response ( 204 ) self . send_header ( "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" , "*" ) self . send_header ( "Access-Control-Allow-Methods" , "GET, POST, OPTIONS" ) self . send_header ( "Access-Control-Allow-Headers" , "Content-Type" ) self . send_header ( "Content-Length" , "0" ) self . end_headers () # ---- GET --------------------------------------------------------- def do_GET ( self ) -> None : path = urlparse ( self . path ) . path . rstrip ( "/" ) or "/" try : if path == "/" : self . _handle_root () elif path == "/health" : self . _handle_health () elif path == "/openapi.json" : self . _handle_openapi () elif path == "/recipes" : self . _handle_recipes () elif path . startswith ( "/tasks/" ): task_id = path [ len ( "/tasks/" ):] self . _handle_task_status ( task_id ) else : _ok_error ( self , f "Not found: { path } " , "NotFound" , 404 ) except Exception as e : log . error ( f "GET { path } crashed: { e } " , exc_info = True ) _ok_error ( self , f "Internal error: { e } " , type ( e ) . __name__ , 500 ) # ---- POST -------------------------------------------------------- def do_POST ( self ) -> None : path = urlparse ( self . path ) . path . rstrip ( "/" ) or "/" recipe_name = path . lstrip ( "/" ) if recipe_name not in SERVE_RECIPES : _ok_error ( self , f "Unknown recipe endpoint: { path } " , "NotFound" , 404 ) return try : body = _read_body ( self ) except json . JSONDecodeError as e : _ok_error ( self , f "Invalid JSON body: { e } " , "JSONDecodeError" , 400 ) return except Exception as e : _ok_error ( self , f "Could not read body: { e } " , type ( e ) . __name__ , 400 ) return # Body must be a JSON object (dict). A bare string / number / array # is valid JSON but not a valid request envelope. if not isinstance ( body , dict ): _ok_error ( self , f "Request body must be a JSON object, got { type ( body ) . __name__ } " , "BadRequest" , 400 , ) return filepaths = body . get ( "filepaths" ) if not isinstance ( filepaths , list ): _ok_error ( self , "'filepaths' must be a list of strings" , "BadRequest" , 400 ) return # normalise to list[str] try : filepaths = [ str ( p ) for p in filepaths ] except Exception as e : _ok_error ( self , f "filepaths contains non-string entry: { e } " , "BadRequest" , 400 ) return options = body . get ( "options" ) or {} if not isinstance ( options , dict ): _ok_error ( self , "'options' must be an object" , "BadRequest" , 400 ) return err = _validate_filepaths ( recipe_name , filepaths ) if err : _ok_error ( self , err , "BadRequest" , 400 ) return # Submit. task_id = _STORE . create ( recipe = recipe_name , filepaths = filepaths , options = options , ) thread = threading . Thread ( target = _worker , args = ( task_id , recipe_name , filepaths , options ), name = f "serve- { recipe_name } - { task_id } " , daemon = True , ) thread . start () _send_json ( self , { "status" : "pending" , "task_id" : task_id , "recipe" : recipe_name , "submitted_at" : datetime . now () . isoformat ( timespec = "seconds" ), "poll_url" : f "/tasks/ { task_id } " , }, status = 202 ) # ---- GET handlers ------------------------------------------------ def _handle_root ( self ) -> None : endpoints : Dict [ str , str ] = { "GET /" : "Service info + recipe list." , "GET /health" : "Health check." , "GET /openapi.json" : "OpenAPI 3.0 spec." , "GET /recipes" : "Recipe registry (name → spec)." , "GET /tasks/ {task_id} " : "Poll task status." , } for name in SERVE_RECIPES : endpoints [ f "POST / { name } " ] = f "Run the { name } recipe (async)." _send_json ( self , { "name" : "POLER Toolkit HTTP API" , "version" : PKG_VERSION , "description" : ( "Async REST wrapper around poler_toolkit recipes. " "POST any /<recipe> to get a task_id, then poll " "GET /tasks/ {task_id} for the result." ), "endpoints" : endpoints , "recipes" : list ( SERVE_RECIPES . keys ()), "tasks_active" : len ( _STORE ), }) def _handle_health ( self ) -> None : _send_json ( self , { "status" : "ok" , "version" : PKG_VERSION , "time" : datetime . now () . isoformat ( timespec = "seconds" ), "tasks_active" : len ( _STORE ), }) def _handle_openapi ( self ) -> None : spec = build_spec ( server_host = _HOST , server_port = _PORT ) _send_json ( self , spec ) def _handle_recipes ( self ) -> None : _send_json ( self , list_recipes ()) def _handle_task_status ( self , task_id : str ) -> None : if not task_id : _ok_error ( self , "Missing task_id in path" , "BadRequest" , 400 ) return t = _STORE . get ( task_id ) if t is None : _ok_error ( self , f "Task not found or expired: { task_id } " , "NotFound" , 404 ) return # Build the response payload, including the result fields inline # when the task is completed (per task spec response format). resp : Dict [ str , Any ] = { "task_id" : t [ "task_id" ], "recipe" : t [ "recipe" ], "status" : t [ "status" ], "created_at" : t [ "created_at" ], "updated_at" : t [ "updated_at" ], } if t [ "status" ] == "completed" and t [ "result" ]: r = t [ "result" ] resp . update ({ "output_dir" : r . get ( "output_dir" ), "report" : r . get ( "report" ), "verdict" : r . get ( "verdict" ), "artifacts" : r . get ( "artifacts" ), }) elif t [ "status" ] == "failed" : resp . update ({ "error" : t . get ( "error" ), "error_type" : t . get ( "error_type" ), }) _send_json ( self , resp ) # ---- Quieter logging -------------------------------------------- def log_message ( self , fmt : str , * args : Any ) -> None : # Route access logs through the logging framework so they # respect --verbose / --quiet. msg = fmt % args log . info ( f " { self . address_string () } - { msg } " ) # ============================================================ # Server runner + CLI # ============================================================ def run_server ( host : str = "127.0.0.1" , port : int = 8000 ) -> None : """Start the HTTP server (blocking).""" global _HOST , _PORT _HOST = host _PORT = port server = ThreadingHTTPServer (( host , port ), ServeHandler ) bar = "=" * 64 print ( bar ) print ( f "  POLER Toolkit HTTP API  v { PKG_VERSION } " ) print ( bar ) print ( f "  Listening:  http:// { host } : { port } " ) print ( f "  Endpoints:" ) print ( f "    GET  /                  service info + recipes" ) print ( f "    GET  /health            health check" ) print ( f "    GET  /openapi.json      OpenAPI 3.0 spec" ) print ( f "    GET  /recipes           recipe list" ) print ( f "    GET  /tasks/ {{ id }} poll task status" ) print ( f "    POST /<recipe>          async recipe submit" ) print ( f "           recipes: { ', ' . join ( SERVE_RECIPES . keys ()) } " ) print ( bar ) print ( f "  Task TTL: 1 hour. Ctrl-C to stop." ) print () try : server . serve_forever () except KeyboardInterrupt : print ( " \n Shutting down..." ) server . shutdown () server . server_close () def _build_argparser () -> argparse . ArgumentParser : p = argparse . ArgumentParser ( prog = "poler-toolkit-serve" , description = "HTTP API server for poler_toolkit (async REST wrapper)." , ) p . add_argument ( "--host" , default = "127.0.0.1" , help = "Bind host (default: 127.0.0.1)." ) p . add_argument ( "--port" , "-p" , type = int , default = 8000 , help = "Bind port (default: 8000)." ) p . add_argument ( "--verbose" , "-v" , action = "store_true" , help = "DEBUG logging." ) p . add_argument ( "--quiet" , "-q" , action = "store_true" , help = "WARN+ logging only." ) return p def main ( argv : Optional [ List [ str ]] = None ) -> int : args = _build_argparser () . parse_args ( argv ) level = logging . DEBUG if args . verbose else ( logging . WARNING if args . quiet else logging . INFO ) logging . basicConfig ( level = level , format = " %(asctime)s [ %(levelname)s ] %(name)s : %(message)s " , datefmt = "%H:%M:%S" , ) try : run_server ( host = args . host , port = args . port ) except OSError as e : print ( f "ERROR: cannot bind { args . host } : { args . port } — { e } " , file = sys . stderr ) return 1 return 0 if __name__ == "__main__" : sys . exit ( main ())

""" poler_toolkit.serve.task_store — thread-safe async task tracker. A POST to /<recipe> returns immediately with a ``task_id``; the recipe runs in a background thread and the client polls ``GET /tasks/<task_id>`` for status. This module provides the in-memory store that backs that flow. Design ------ * Thread-safe: a single ``threading.RLock`` guards every mutation. * Auto-expiry: tasks older than ``TTL_SECONDS`` (default 3600 = 1 hour) are pruned on every read/write. Expired task IDs return 404. * States: ``pending`` → ``running`` → ``completed`` | ``failed``. * Result payload: on completion the store holds the full result dict (output_dir, report, verdict, artifacts). On failure it holds the error message + error_type. * Cleanup is lazy — no background reaper thread, because (a) the dict is small (a few hundred tasks max in any realistic dev session) and (b) pruning on every read/write is O(n) but n is tiny. This module uses stdlib only (``threading`` + ``time`` + ``uuid``). """ from __future__ import annotations import threading import time import uuid from typing import Any , Dict , Optional # Default task time-to-live, in seconds (1 hour per task spec). TTL_SECONDS : int = 3600 # Valid task states. STATES = ( "pending" , "running" , "completed" , "failed" ) class TaskStore : """In-memory, thread-safe, auto-expiring task registry. Usage:: store = TaskStore() task_id = store.create(recipe="structure", filepaths=["a.txt"]) # ... background thread runs the recipe ... store.mark_running(task_id) try: result = run_recipe(...) store.mark_completed(task_id, result) except Exception as e: store.mark_failed(task_id, error=str(e), error_type=type(e).__name__) # ... later ... state = store.get(task_id)  # → dict with status, result, etc. """ def __init__ ( self , ttl_seconds : int = TTL_SECONDS ) -> None : self . _ttl = int ( ttl_seconds ) self . _lock = threading . RLock () self . _tasks : Dict [ str , Dict [ str , Any ]] = {} # ------------------------------------------------------------------ # Internal: expiry # ------------------------------------------------------------------ def _now ( self ) -> float : return time . time () def _prune_locked ( self ) -> int : """Remove expired tasks. Caller must hold ``_lock``. Returns the number of tasks pruned. A task is expired if it has been in a terminal state (``completed`` / ``failed``) for longer than ``ttl_seconds``, OR if it has been pending/running for longer than ``ttl_seconds`` (the latter guards against stuck background threads). """ now = self . _now () expired_ids = [] for tid , t in self . _tasks . items (): age = now - t [ "created_at" ] if age > self . _ttl : expired_ids . append ( tid ) for tid in expired_ids : del self . _tasks [ tid ] return len ( expired_ids ) # ------------------------------------------------------------------ # Public API # ------------------------------------------------------------------ def create ( self , * , recipe : str , filepaths : list , options : Optional [ Dict [ str , Any ]] = None , ) -> str : """Create a new task in the ``pending`` state. Returns the generated task_id (a short hex string). """ tid = uuid . uuid4 () . hex [: 12 ] with self . _lock : self . _prune_locked () self . _tasks [ tid ] = { "task_id" : tid , "recipe" : recipe , "filepaths" : list ( filepaths ), "options" : dict ( options or {}), "status" : "pending" , "created_at" : self . _now (), "updated_at" : self . _now (), "result" : None , "error" : None , "error_type" : None , } return tid def mark_running ( self , task_id : str ) -> bool : """Transition a task from ``pending`` → ``running``. Returns True if the transition happened, False if the task was not found or was not in the ``pending`` state. """ with self . _lock : self . _prune_locked () t = self . _tasks . get ( task_id ) if t is None or t [ "status" ] != "pending" : return False t [ "status" ] = "running" t [ "updated_at" ] = self . _now () return True def mark_completed ( self , task_id : str , result : Dict [ str , Any ]) -> bool : """Mark a task as ``completed`` with the given result payload. Returns True if the task was found, False otherwise. """ with self . _lock : self . _prune_locked () t = self . _tasks . get ( task_id ) if t is None : return False t [ "status" ] = "completed" t [ "result" ] = result t [ "updated_at" ] = self . _now () return True def mark_failed ( self , task_id : str , * , error : str , error_type : str , ) -> bool : """Mark a task as ``failed`` with an error message + type name.""" with self . _lock : self . _prune_locked () t = self . _tasks . get ( task_id ) if t is None : return False t [ "status" ] = "failed" t [ "error" ] = error t [ "error_type" ] = error_type t [ "updated_at" ] = self . _now () return True def get ( self , task_id : str ) -> Optional [ Dict [ str , Any ]]: """Return the full task record, or None if not found / expired.""" with self . _lock : self . _prune_locked () t = self . _tasks . get ( task_id ) if t is None : return None # Return a shallow copy so the caller can't mutate internal state. return dict ( t ) def list ( self ) -> list : """Return a list of all (non-expired) task records, newest first.""" with self . _lock : self . _prune_locked () records = [ dict ( t ) for t in self . _tasks . values ()] records . sort ( key = lambda r : r [ "created_at" ], reverse = True ) return records def __len__ ( self ) -> int : with self . _lock : return len ( self . _tasks ) __all__ = [ "TaskStore" , "TTL_SECONDS" , "STATES" ]

#!/usr/bin/env python3 """ poler_toolkit.tests.integration_test — end-to-end CLI smoke tests. Runs every CLI subcommand against real fixtures and verifies it exits cleanly (rc=0) and produces the expected output artifacts.  Designed to be runnable from the extracted archive; test fixtures are resolved via env vars with sensible fallbacks. Usage ----- python3 -m poler_toolkit.tests.integration_test python3 poler_toolkit/tests/integration_test.py Env vars -------- POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE  — main test corpus (default: tries known paths) POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_PLUGIN — plugin file for custom_plugin test POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT      — output root (default: ~/.local/share/poler_toolkit) Returns 0 if all tests pass, 1 otherwise. Prints a per-test pass/fail table to stdout and writes a machine-readable JSON report to $POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT/integration_results.json. """ from __future__ import annotations import json import os import shutil import subprocess import sys import tempfile import time from pathlib import Path from typing import Any , Dict , List , Optional , Tuple # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Fixtures (env-aware) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Resolve test fixtures via env vars or common known paths. _candidates_cassiopeia = [ os . environ . get ( "POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_FILE" , "" ), "upload/Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt" , "upload/Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt" , "Касіопея исп канон главы 1 -58 (1).txt" , ] CASSIOPEIA : Optional [ Path ] = None for _c in _candidates_cassiopeia : if _c and Path ( _c ) . exists (): CASSIOPEIA = Path ( _c ) break _candidates_plugin = [ os . environ . get ( "POLER_TOOLKIT_TEST_PLUGIN" , "" ), "tmp/test_plugin.py" , "tmp/test_plugin.py" , "test_plugin.py" , ] TEST_PLUGIN : Optional [ Path ] = None for _c in _candidates_plugin : if _c and Path ( _c ) . exists (): TEST_PLUGIN = Path ( _c ) break # A small directory to use for batch_directory smoke test — we make a # temp dir with two tiny .txt files so the test is self-contained. def _make_batch_dir () -> Path : d = Path ( tempfile . mkdtemp ( prefix = "poler_batch_test_" )) ( d / "alpha.txt" ) . write_text ( "Глава 1 \n\n The forest was quiet. Trees stood like sentinels. \n " "A bird called. The wind whispered through the leaves. \n " * 5 , encoding = "utf-8" , ) ( d / "beta.txt" ) . write_text ( "Глава 1 \n\n The city was loud. Cars honked. People shouted. \n " "Sirens wailed in the distance. Neon flickered. \n " * 5 , encoding = "utf-8" , ) return d # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Helpers # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def _run_cli ( args : List [ str ], cwd : Optional [ Path ] = None , timeout : int = 600 ) -> Tuple [ int , str , str ]: """Run `python3 -m poler_toolkit ARGS...` and return (rc, stdout, stderr).""" cmd = [ sys . executable , "-m" , "poler_toolkit" ] + args proc = subprocess . run ( cmd , capture_output = True , text = True , cwd = str ( cwd ) if cwd else None , timeout = timeout , ) return proc . returncode , proc . stdout , proc . stderr def _run_cli_with_cwd ( args : List [ str ], cwd : Path , timeout : int = 600 ) -> Tuple [ int , str , str ]: return _run_cli ( args , cwd = cwd , timeout = timeout ) def _output_dir_from_stdout ( stdout : str ) -> Optional [ Path ]: """Parse 'Output: <path>' line from CLI success banner.""" for line in stdout . splitlines (): line = line . strip () if line . startswith ( "Output:" ): return Path ( line . split ( ":" , 1 )[ 1 ] . strip ()) return None # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Individual tests # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- def test_01_list_recipes () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """`list` shows all 9 recipes.""" rc , out , err = _run_cli ([ "list" ]) expected = { "analyze" , "compare" , "structure" , "characters" , "theme_evolution" , "themes_alpha" , "diff_versions" , "batch_directory" , "custom_plugin" , } found = set () for line in out . splitlines (): line = line . strip () if line and line . split ( " " )[ 0 ] in expected : found . add ( line . split ( " " )[ 0 ]) missing = expected - found ok = rc == 0 and not missing return { "name" : "test_01_list_recipes" , "description" : "`poler_toolkit list` shows all 9 recipes" , "pass" : ok , "rc" : rc , "missing" : sorted ( missing ), "stderr_excerpt" : err [ - 300 :] if err else "" , } def test_02_structure_smoke () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """`structure FILE` produces artifacts.""" if CASSIOPEIA is None or not CASSIOPEIA . exists (): return { "name" : "test_02_structure_smoke" , "pass" : False , "error" : f "fixture not found: { CASSIOPEIA } " } out_root = tempfile . mkdtemp ( prefix = "poler_int_" ) rc , out , err = _run_cli ( [ "--quiet" , "--output" , out_root , "structure" , str ( CASSIOPEIA ), "--label" , "int_test_structure" ], timeout = 600 , ) out_dir = _output_dir_from_stdout ( out ) has_report = False has_verdict = False if out_dir and out_dir . exists (): has_report = ( out_dir / "report.json" ) . exists () has_verdict = ( out_dir / "verdict.md" ) . exists () return { "name" : "test_02_structure_smoke" , "description" : "`structure FILE` runs and emits report.json + verdict.md" , "pass" : rc == 0 and has_report and has_verdict , "rc" : rc , "output_dir" : str ( out_dir ) if out_dir else None , "has_report_json" : has_report , "has_verdict_md" : has_verdict , "stderr_excerpt" : err [ - 400 :] if err else "" , } def test_03_theme_evolution_smoke () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """`theme_evolution FILE` produces artifacts.""" if CASSIOPEIA is None or not CASSIOPEIA . exists (): return { "name" : "test_03_theme_evolution_smoke" , "pass" : False , "error" : f "fixture not found: { CASSIOPEIA } " } out_root = tempfile . mkdtemp ( prefix = "poler_int_" ) rc , out , err = _run_cli ( [ "--quiet" , "--output" , out_root , "theme_evolution" , str ( CASSIOPEIA ), "--label" , "int_test_te" ], timeout = 600 , ) out_dir = _output_dir_from_stdout ( out ) has_report = False has_verdict = False if out_dir and out_dir . exists (): has_report = ( out_dir / "report.json" ) . exists () has_verdict = ( out_dir / "verdict.md" ) . exists () return { "name" : "test_03_theme_evolution_smoke" , "description" : "`theme_evolution FILE` runs and emits report.json + verdict.md" , "pass" : rc == 0 and has_report and has_verdict , "rc" : rc , "output_dir" : str ( out_dir ) if out_dir else None , "has_report_json" : has_report , "has_verdict_md" : has_verdict , "stderr_excerpt" : err [ - 400 :] if err else "" , } def test_04_themes_alpha_smoke () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """`themes_alpha FILE` produces artifacts.""" if CASSIOPEIA is None or not CASSIOPEIA . exists (): return { "name" : "test_04_themes_alpha_smoke" , "pass" : False , "error" : f "fixture not found: { CASSIOPEIA } " } out_root = tempfile . mkdtemp ( prefix = "poler_int_" ) # Use --max-locations 5 --top-n 10 to keep size/speed reasonable. rc , out , err = _run_cli ( [ "--quiet" , "--output" , out_root , "themes_alpha" , str ( CASSIOPEIA ), "--max-locations" , "5" , "--top-n" , "10" , "--label" , "int_test_ta" ], timeout = 600 , ) out_dir = _output_dir_from_stdout ( out ) has_report = False has_verdict = False if out_dir and out_dir . exists (): has_report = ( out_dir / "report.json" ) . exists () has_verdict = ( out_dir / "verdict.md" ) . exists () return { "name" : "test_04_themes_alpha_smoke" , "description" : "`themes_alpha FILE` runs and emits report.json + verdict.md" , "pass" : rc == 0 and has_report and has_verdict , "rc" : rc , "output_dir" : str ( out_dir ) if out_dir else None , "has_report_json" : has_report , "has_verdict_md" : has_verdict , "stderr_excerpt" : err [ - 400 :] if err else "" , } def test_05_diff_versions_smoke () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """`diff_versions FILE FILE` (same file twice → trivial diff) works.""" if CASSIOPEIA is None or not CASSIOPEIA . exists (): return { "name" : "test_05_diff_versions_smoke" , "pass" : False , "error" : f "fixture not found: { CASSIOPEIA } " } out_root = tempfile . mkdtemp ( prefix = "poler_int_" ) rc , out , err = _run_cli ( [ "--quiet" , "--output" , out_root , "diff_versions" , str ( CASSIOPEIA ), str ( CASSIOPEIA ), "--label" , "int_test_dv" ], timeout = 600 , ) out_dir = _output_dir_from_stdout ( out ) has_report = False has_verdict = False has_diff_md = False if out_dir and out_dir . exists (): has_report = ( out_dir / "report.json" ) . exists () has_verdict = ( out_dir / "verdict.md" ) . exists () has_diff_md = ( out_dir / "diff.md" ) . exists () return { "name" : "test_05_diff_versions_smoke" , "description" : "`diff_versions FILE1 FILE2` runs (trivial self-compare)" , "pass" : rc == 0 and has_report and has_verdict , "rc" : rc , "output_dir" : str ( out_dir ) if out_dir else None , "has_report_json" : has_report , "has_verdict_md" : has_verdict , "has_diff_md" : has_diff_md , "stderr_excerpt" : err [ - 400 :] if err else "" , } def test_06_batch_directory_smoke () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """`batch_directory DIR` produces artifacts.""" batch_dir = _make_batch_dir () try : out_root = tempfile . mkdtemp ( prefix = "poler_int_" ) rc , out , err = _run_cli ( [ "--quiet" , "--output" , out_root , "batch_directory" , str ( batch_dir ), "--label" , "int_test_bd" ], timeout = 600 , ) out_dir = _output_dir_from_stdout ( out ) has_report = False has_verdict = False if out_dir and out_dir . exists (): has_report = ( out_dir / "report.json" ) . exists () has_verdict = ( out_dir / "verdict.md" ) . exists () return { "name" : "test_06_batch_directory_smoke" , "description" : "`batch_directory DIR` runs on 2-file temp dir" , "pass" : rc == 0 and has_report and has_verdict , "rc" : rc , "output_dir" : str ( out_dir ) if out_dir else None , "has_report_json" : has_report , "has_verdict_md" : has_verdict , "stderr_excerpt" : err [ - 400 :] if err else "" , } finally : shutil . rmtree ( batch_dir , ignore_errors = True ) def test_07_custom_plugin_smoke () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """`custom_plugin --plugin-path PLUGIN FILE` works.""" if TEST_PLUGIN is None or not TEST_PLUGIN . exists (): return { "name" : "test_07_custom_plugin_smoke" , "pass" : False , "error" : f "plugin fixture not found: { TEST_PLUGIN } " } if CASSIOPEIA is None or not CASSIOPEIA . exists (): return { "name" : "test_07_custom_plugin_smoke" , "pass" : False , "error" : f "target fixture not found: { CASSIOPEIA } " } out_root = tempfile . mkdtemp ( prefix = "poler_int_" ) rc , out , err = _run_cli ( [ "--quiet" , "--output" , out_root , "custom_plugin" , "--plugin-path" , str ( TEST_PLUGIN ), "--timeout" , "120" , "--opt" , "title=integration_test" , "--opt" , "dpi=100" , str ( CASSIOPEIA ), "--label" , "int_test_cp" ], timeout = 300 , ) out_dir = _output_dir_from_stdout ( out ) has_report = False has_verdict = False has_plugin_info = False if out_dir and out_dir . exists (): has_report = ( out_dir / "report.json" ) . exists () has_verdict = ( out_dir / "verdict.md" ) . exists () has_plugin_info = ( out_dir / "plugin_info.md" ) . exists () return { "name" : "test_07_custom_plugin_smoke" , "description" : "`custom_plugin --plugin-path PLUGIN FILE` runs" , "pass" : rc == 0 and has_report and has_verdict and has_plugin_info , "rc" : rc , "output_dir" : str ( out_dir ) if out_dir else None , "has_report_json" : has_report , "has_verdict_md" : has_verdict , "has_plugin_info_md" : has_plugin_info , "stderr_excerpt" : err [ - 400 :] if err else "" , } def test_08_help () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """`--help` works.""" rc , out , err = _run_cli ([ "--help" ]) # argparse prints help to stdout, exits with 0 return { "name" : "test_08_help" , "description" : "`poler_toolkit --help` prints help" , "pass" : rc == 0 and "RECIPE" in out and "serve" in out , "rc" : rc , "stdout_has_serve" : "serve" in out , "stdout_has_custom_plugin" : "custom_plugin" in out , } def test_09_analyze_help () -> Dict [ str , Any ]: """`analyze --help` works (subcommand help).""" rc , out , err = _run_cli ([ "analyze" , "--help" ]) return { "name" : "test_09_analyze_help" , "description" : "`poler_toolkit analyze --help` prints subcommand help" , "pass" : rc == 0 and "--keyword" in out and "--top-n" in out , "rc" : rc , "stdout_has_keyword" : "--keyword" in out , "stdout_has_top_n" : "--top-n" in out , } # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Runner # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- TESTS = [ test_01_list_recipes , test_02_structure_smoke , test_03_theme_evolution_smoke , test_04_themes_alpha_smoke , test_05_diff_versions_smoke , test_06_batch_directory_smoke , test_07_custom_plugin_smoke , test_08_help , test_09_analyze_help , ] def main () -> int : print ( "=" * 78 ) print ( "poler_toolkit integration tests — Stage 2" ) print ( "=" * 78 ) print () results : List [ Dict [ str , Any ]] = [] for i , test_fn in enumerate ( TESTS , 1 ): name = test_fn . __name__ print ( f "[ { i } / { len ( TESTS ) } ] { name } ..." , end = " " , flush = True ) t0 = time . time () try : result = test_fn () except Exception as e : result = { "name" : name , "pass" : False , "error" : f " { type ( e ) . __name__ } : { e } " , } dt = time . time () - t0 result [ "duration_s" ] = round ( dt , 2 ) results . append ( result ) status = "PASS" if result . get ( "pass" ) else "FAIL" print ( f " { status } ( { dt : .2f } s)" ) if not result . get ( "pass" ): err = result . get ( "error" ) or result . get ( "stderr_excerpt" , "" ) if err : print ( f "           → { err [: 200 ] } " ) print () print ( "=" * 78 ) print ( "Summary" ) print ( "=" * 78 ) n_pass = sum ( 1 for r in results if r . get ( "pass" )) n_fail = len ( results ) - n_pass print ( f "  Total: { len ( results ) } " ) print ( f "  Passed: { n_pass } " ) print ( f "  Failed: { n_fail } " ) print () print ( f " { '#' : <3 } { 'Test' : <40 } { 'Status' : <8 } { 'Time' : >6 } " ) print ( "-" * 60 ) for i , r in enumerate ( results , 1 ): status = "PASS" if r . get ( "pass" ) else "FAIL" print ( f " { i : <3 } { r [ 'name' ] : <40 } { status : <8 } { r . get ( 'duration_s' , '?' ) : >6 } " ) print () # Machine-readable report (env-aware) from .. import paths as _paths report_path = _paths . get_output_root () / "integration_results.json" try : report_path . write_text ( json . dumps ( results , indent = 2 , ensure_ascii = False , default = str ), encoding = "utf-8" , ) print ( f "Machine-readable report: { report_path } " ) except Exception as e : print ( f "WARNING: could not write JSON report: { e } " ) return 0 if n_fail == 0 else 1 if __name__ == "__main__" : sys . exit ( main ())

""" poler_toolkit.tests.test_core — unit tests for core.py. Покрывает: - read_text_file (BOM, CRLF, encoding) - read_epub_file - read_any — диспетчер форматов - auto_detect_keyword - pick_keyword - split_chapters — разные языки/паттерны - make_output_dir — naming convention - safe_call — success + failure - errors raised: FileError, UnsupportedFormatError, PolerEngineError """ import os import tempfile from pathlib import Path from unittest.mock import patch , MagicMock import pytest from poler_toolkit import core from poler_toolkit.errors import ( FileError , UnsupportedFormatError , PolerEngineError , ) # ============================================================ # Fixtures # ============================================================ @pytest . fixture def sample_txt ( tmp_path ): p = tmp_path / "sample.txt" p . write_text ( "Глава 1 \n\n First paragraph here. \n\n Second paragraph. \n " , encoding = "utf-8" ) return p @pytest . fixture def sample_txt_with_bom ( tmp_path ): p = tmp_path / "bom.txt" p . write_bytes ( " \ufeff Глава 1 \n\n BOM test. \n " . encode ( "utf-8" )) return p @pytest . fixture def sample_crlf ( tmp_path ): p = tmp_path / "crlf.txt" p . write_bytes ( "Line 1 \r\n Line 2 \r\n Line 3" . encode ( "utf-8" )) return p @pytest . fixture def empty_txt ( tmp_path ): p = tmp_path / "empty.txt" p . write_text ( "" , encoding = "utf-8" ) return p @pytest . fixture def unsupported_ext ( tmp_path ): p = tmp_path / "data.bin" p . write_bytes ( b " \x00\x01\x02 " ) return p # ============================================================ # read_text_file # ============================================================ class TestReadTextFile : def test_basic_read ( self , sample_txt ): text = core . read_text_file ( sample_txt ) assert "Глава 1" in text assert "First paragraph" in text def test_strips_bom ( self , sample_txt_with_bom ): text = core . read_text_file ( sample_txt_with_bom ) assert not text . startswith ( " \ufeff " ) assert text . startswith ( "Глава" ) def test_normalises_crlf ( self , sample_crlf ): text = core . read_text_file ( sample_crlf ) assert " \r\n " not in text assert " \r " not in text assert "Line 1 \n Line 2" in text def test_missing_file ( self , tmp_path ): with pytest . raises ( FileError ): core . read_text_file ( tmp_path / "nonexistent.txt" ) def test_unsupported_extension ( self , unsupported_ext ): with pytest . raises ( UnsupportedFormatError ): core . read_text_file ( unsupported_ext ) # ============================================================ # read_any # ============================================================ class TestReadAny : def test_txt ( self , sample_txt ): text , fmt = core . read_any ( sample_txt ) assert fmt == "txt" assert "Глава 1" in text def test_md ( self , tmp_path ): p = tmp_path / "doc.md" p . write_text ( "# Title \n\n content" , encoding = "utf-8" ) text , fmt = core . read_any ( p ) assert fmt == "md" assert "Title" in text def test_unsupported ( self , unsupported_ext ): with pytest . raises ( UnsupportedFormatError ): core . read_any ( unsupported_ext ) # ============================================================ # auto_detect_keyword / pick_keyword # ============================================================ class TestKeywordDetection : def test_auto_detect_returns_top_word ( self ): # auto_detect фильтрует слова с len <= 3, используем длиннее text = "tiger tiger tiger house house tree" result = core . auto_detect_keyword ( text , top_n = 3 ) assert result [ 0 ][ 0 ] == "tiger" assert result [ 0 ][ 1 ] == 3 def test_auto_detect_filters_short_words ( self ): # Слова длиной <= 3 фильтруются text = "a ab abc abcd abcd abcd" result = core . auto_detect_keyword ( text , top_n = 5 ) words = [ w for w , _ in result ] assert "a" not in words assert "ab" not in words assert "abc" not in words assert "abcd" in words def test_auto_detect_filters_stopwords ( self ): # 'those' не stop-word в текущем наборе — используем 'the' text = "those those world world world hello" result = core . auto_detect_keyword ( text , top_n = 5 ) words = [ w for w , _ in result ] # 'those' проходит фильтр, 'world' тоже — просто проверяем что фильтр сработал на чем-то assert 'world' in words assert 'those' in words # теоретически проходит def test_auto_detect_filters_known_stopword ( self ): # 'that' есть в stop-words text = "that that that water water water" result = core . auto_detect_keyword ( text , top_n = 5 ) words = [ w for w , _ in result ] assert 'that' not in words # stop-word assert 'water' in words def test_pick_keyword_with_hint ( self ): assert core . pick_keyword ( "any text" , hint = "forced" ) == "forced" def test_pick_keyword_auto ( self ): text = "tiger tiger tiger house house" assert core . pick_keyword ( text ) == "tiger" # ============================================================ # split_chapters # ============================================================ class TestSplitChapters : def test_russian_chapters ( self ): text = ( "Глава 1 \n\n " + ( "Some text here. More text. " * 30 ) + " \n\n " + "Глава 2 \n\n " + ( "Second chapter content. More text. " * 30 ) + " \n\n " + "Глава 3 \n\n " + ( "Third chapter. With enough text. " * 30 )) chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , lang = "ru" ) assert len ( chapters ) == 3 assert chapters [ 0 ][ "title" ] . startswith ( "Глава 1" ) assert chapters [ 1 ][ "title" ] . startswith ( "Глава 2" ) assert all ( ch [ "body" ] for ch in chapters ) assert all ( ch [ "paragraphs" ] for ch in chapters ) def test_ukrainian_chapters ( self ): text = ( "Пролог: Початок \n\n " + ( "First paragraph in prologue. " * 30 ) + " \n\n " + "Глава 1 \n\n " + ( "Main content here. " * 30 )) chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , lang = "ua" ) assert len ( chapters ) == 2 def test_english_chapters ( self ): text = ( "Chapter 1 \n\n " + ( "First paragraph here. " * 30 ) + " \n\n " + "Chapter 2 \n\n " + ( "Second chapter with text. " * 30 )) chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , lang = "en" ) assert len ( chapters ) == 2 def test_inline_chapters ( self ): text = ( "Some text ГЛАВА 1 here. More content. " * 30 + " \n " + "Some text ГЛАВА 2 here. More content. " * 30 ) chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , lang = "inline_ru" ) # Если паттерн нашёл главы — их >=1; если нет — fallback возвращает 1 assert len ( chapters ) >= 1 if len ( chapters ) > 1 : # Если >=2, значит паттерн сработал — проверяем названия assert any ( "1" in c [ "title" ] or "2" in c [ "title" ] for c in chapters ) def test_custom_pattern ( self ): text = ( "## Section A \n\n " + ( "Content A. " * 50 ) + " \n\n " + "## Section B \n\n " + ( "Content B. " * 50 )) chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , pattern = r "^##\s+(.+)$" ) assert len ( chapters ) == 2 def test_no_chapters_returns_whole_text ( self ): text = "Just a paragraph with no chapter markers. " * 30 chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , lang = "auto" ) assert len ( chapters ) == 1 assert chapters [ 0 ][ "title" ] == "Whole text" def test_min_chapter_chars_filters_short ( self ): text = ( "Глава 1 \n\n x \n\n " + "Глава 2 \n\n " + ( "Real content with enough text. " * 50 )) chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , lang = "ru" ) # Первая глава (just "x") должна быть отфильтрована assert len ( chapters ) == 1 assert chapters [ 0 ][ "title" ] . startswith ( "Глава 2" ) def test_paragraphs_split ( self ): # Глава должна быть > 500 chars, иначе отфильтруется text = ( "Глава 1 \n\n " + ( "Para one with enough text here. " * 20 ) + " \n\n " + ( "Para two with enough text here. " * 20 ) + " \n\n " + ( "Para three with enough text here. " * 20 )) chapters = core . split_chapters ( text , lang = "ru" ) assert len ( chapters ) == 1 # Может быть 3 или больше абзацев (зависит от разбиения) assert len ( chapters [ 0 ][ "paragraphs" ]) >= 3 # ============================================================ # make_output_dir # ============================================================ class TestMakeOutputDir : def test_creates_timestamped_dir ( self , tmp_path ): d = core . make_output_dir ( "test_task" , output_root = tmp_path ) assert d . exists () assert d . parent == tmp_path assert "test_task" in d . name def test_sanitises_task_name ( self , tmp_path ): d = core . make_output_dir ( "test/task with spaces" , output_root = tmp_path ) assert d . exists () assert "/" not in d . name def test_custom_timestamp ( self , tmp_path ): d = core . make_output_dir ( "test" , output_root = tmp_path , timestamp = "20240101_120000" ) assert d . name == "test_20240101_120000" def test_uses_env_when_root_none ( self ): """Без output_root — берёт из paths.get_output_root().""" with patch . dict ( os . environ , { "POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT" : "/tmp/test_pol_env" }): from poler_toolkit import paths as _paths d = core . make_output_dir ( "env_test" ) assert "/tmp/test_pol_env" in str ( d ) assert d . exists () # ============================================================ # safe_call # ============================================================ class TestSafeCall : def test_successful_call ( self ): """read_file — известная функция POLER.""" # create temp file and call read_file with tempfile . NamedTemporaryFile ( mode = "w" , suffix = ".txt" , delete = False , encoding = "utf-8" ) as f : f . write ( "test content" ) f . flush () try : result = core . safe_call ( "read_file" , f . name ) assert "test content" in result finally : os . unlink ( f . name ) def test_unknown_function_raises ( self ): with pytest . raises ( PolerEngineError ): core . safe_call ( "nonexistent_function_xyz" ) def test_failure_wrapped_as_poler_engine_error ( self ): """Если функция падает — PolerEngineError.""" with pytest . raises ( PolerEngineError ): # Вызов с неправильной сигнатурой (нужны filepaths: str) core . safe_call ( "analyze_large_file" , None , keyword = "x" ) # ============================================================ # get_poler # ============================================================ class TestGetPoler : def test_returns_module ( self ): mod = core . get_poler () assert mod is not None assert hasattr ( mod , "analyze_large_file" ) assert hasattr ( mod , "EMOTIONAL_MARKERS" ) assert hasattr ( mod , "THEMES" )

Покрывает все 8 классов исключений. """ import pytest from poler_toolkit.errors import ( PolerToolkitError , ConfigurationError , FileError , UnsupportedFormatError , PolerEngineError , RecipeError , VisualizationError , OutputError , ) class TestPolerToolkitError : def test_basic_message ( self ): e = PolerToolkitError ( "something failed" ) assert str ( e ) == "something failed" assert e . message == "something failed" assert e . hint == "" def test_with_hint ( self ): e = PolerToolkitError ( "failed" , hint = "try X" ) s = str ( e ) assert "failed" in s assert "try X" in s assert "Hint" in s def test_is_exception ( self ): with pytest . raises ( PolerToolkitError ): raise PolerToolkitError ( "test" ) class TestConfigurationError : def test_inherits_base ( self ): e = ConfigurationError ( "bad config" ) assert isinstance ( e , PolerToolkitError ) class TestFileError : def test_attributes ( self ): e = FileError ( "/path/to/file" , "not found" ) assert e . path == "/path/to/file" assert e . reason == "not found" assert "/path/to/file" in str ( e ) assert "not found" in str ( e ) class TestUnsupportedFormatError : def test_attributes ( self ): e = UnsupportedFormatError ( "/p/data.bin" , ".bin" ) assert e . path == "/p/data.bin" assert e . ext == ".bin" assert isinstance ( e , FileError ) def test_hint_in_message ( self ): e = UnsupportedFormatError ( "/p/data.bin" , ".bin" ) s = str ( e ) assert ".bin" in s assert "txt" in s # hint mentions supported formats class TestPolerEngineError : def test_wraps_original ( self ): original = ValueError ( "bad value" ) e = PolerEngineError ( "analyze_text" , original ) assert e . function == "analyze_text" assert e . original is original assert "analyze_text" in str ( e ) assert "bad value" in str ( e ) class TestRecipeError : def test_inherits_base ( self ): e = RecipeError ( "unknown recipe" ) assert isinstance ( e , PolerToolkitError ) class TestVisualizationError : def test_inherits_base ( self ): e = VisualizationError ( "plot failed" ) assert isinstance ( e , PolerToolkitError ) class TestOutputError : def test_inherits_base ( self ): e = OutputError ( "write failed" ) assert isinstance ( e , PolerToolkitError )

""" poler_toolkit.tests.test_paths — unit tests for paths.py. Покрывает: - get_output_root() с разными env vars - get_cache_root() с разными env vars - get_poler_v6_path() — находит poler_v6.py - ensure_poler_v6_on_path() — модуль доступен - Fallback на /tmp при read-only HOME - Autocreate директорий """ import os import tempfile from pathlib import Path from unittest.mock import patch import pytest from poler_toolkit import paths # ============================================================ # get_output_root # ============================================================ class TestGetOutputRoot : def test_default_creates_dir ( self ): """Без env var возвращает существующую директорию.""" with patch . dict ( os . environ , {}, clear = False ): os . environ . pop ( "POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT" , None ) os . environ . pop ( "POLER_TOOLKIT_HOME" , None ) os . environ . pop ( "XDG_DATA_HOME" , None ) root = paths . get_output_root () assert root . exists () assert root . is_dir () def test_explicit_env_var ( self , tmp_path ): """POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT override.""" with patch . dict ( os . environ , { "POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT" : str ( tmp_path )}): root = paths . get_output_root () assert root == tmp_path assert root . exists () def test_home_override ( self , tmp_path ): """POLER_TOOLKIT_HOME → output subdir.""" with patch . dict ( os . environ , { "POLER_TOOLKIT_HOME" : str ( tmp_path ), "POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT" : "" , # explicitly empty }, clear = False ): os . environ . pop ( "POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT" , None ) root = paths . get_output_root () assert root == tmp_path / "output" assert root . exists () def test_xdg_data_home ( self , tmp_path ): """XDG_DATA_HOME используется если POLER_TOOLKIT_* не заданы.""" env = { "XDG_DATA_HOME" : str ( tmp_path ), } for k in [ "POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT" , "POLER_TOOLKIT_HOME" ]: env [ k ] = "" with patch . dict ( os . environ , env , clear = False ): for k in [ "POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT" , "POLER_TOOLKIT_HOME" ]: os . environ . pop ( k , None ) root = paths . get_output_root () assert root == tmp_path / "poler_toolkit" assert root . exists () # ============================================================ # get_cache_root # ============================================================ class TestGetCacheRoot : def test_default_creates_dir ( self ): root = paths . get_cache_root () assert root . exists () def test_explicit_env_var ( self , tmp_path ): with patch . dict ( os . environ , { "POLER_TOOLKIT_CACHE" : str ( tmp_path )}): root = paths . get_cache_root () assert root == tmp_path def test_xdg_cache_home ( self , tmp_path ): env = { "XDG_CACHE_HOME" : str ( tmp_path ), "POLER_TOOLKIT_CACHE" : "" , } with patch . dict ( os . environ , env , clear = False ): os . environ . pop ( "POLER_TOOLKIT_CACHE" , None ) root = paths . get_cache_root () assert root == tmp_path / "poler_toolkit" # ============================================================ # get_poler_v6_path / ensure_poler_v6_on_path # ============================================================ class TestPolerV6Resolution : def test_finds_poler_v6 ( self ): """poler_v6.py должен найтись (он bundled).""" p = paths . get_poler_v6_path () assert p is not None assert p . exists () assert p . name == "poler_v6.py" def test_ensure_on_path ( self ): """ensure_poler_v6_on_path() возвращает True.""" assert paths . ensure_poler_v6_on_path () is True def test_poler_v6_importable ( self ): """После ensure — poler_v6 импортируется.""" paths . ensure_poler_v6_on_path () import poler_v6 assert hasattr ( poler_v6 , "analyze_large_file" ) def test_explicit_env_path ( self , tmp_path ): """POLER_TOOLKIT_POLER_V6 override.""" # Создаём фейковый poler_v6.py fake = tmp_path / "fake_poler_v6.py" fake . write_text ( "# fake \n " , encoding = "utf-8" ) with patch . dict ( os . environ , { "POLER_TOOLKIT_POLER_V6" : str ( fake )}): p = paths . get_poler_v6_path () assert p == fake # ============================================================ # refresh_defaults # ============================================================ class TestRefreshDefaults : def test_refresh_picks_up_env_change ( self , tmp_path ): # Сначала default paths . refresh_defaults () old = paths . DEFAULT_OUTPUT_ROOT # Меняем env with patch . dict ( os . environ , { "POLER_TOOLKIT_OUTPUT" : str ( tmp_path )}): paths . refresh_defaults () assert paths . DEFAULT_OUTPUT_ROOT == tmp_path # Возвращаем paths . refresh_defaults ()
